The Frostback Mountains
Dragon Age: Inquisition
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
Haven as the Inquisition's Stronghold
At the request of
Divine Justinia V
in 9:35
Dragon Age
, Haven was restored and brought back into the fold of the
Maker
, giving shelter for the pilgrims who come to visit the
Temple of Sacred Ashes
. Following the events at the Conclave, Haven serves as the initial base of the re-established
Inquisition
. After
the Inquisitor
seals
the Breach
, the town is attacked by
Corypheus
's army. Though the Inquisition manages to bury a portion of Corypheus's army under an avalanche, the people in Haven are effectively trapped.
Chancellor Roderick
, however, reveals that he knows of a route which can be used to escape. The Inquisitor buys them time by realigning a trebuchet to cause another avalanche to bury the village. After a brief confrontation with Corypheus, the Inquisitor sets off the avalanche and escapes into a small cavern, while Corypheus' dragon flies him to safety.
With the loss of Haven, the Inquisition elects to relocate to the fortress of
Skyhold
.
Following the battle and the Inquisition's arrival at Skyhold, a monument is built at Haven to commemorate the villagers and soldiers that lost their lives in the attack.

Notes
(
Origins
) This location is labeled "The Village of Haven".
(
Inquisition
) If
the Inquisitor
is not a rogue they can ask
Sera
to unlock the doors in the cellars of the
chantry
.
Interact with one of the locked doors and the message "Requires a rogue" will appear, unlocking the necessary dialogue option with Sera ("I need a lock picked.").  On PC, if the message does not appear, try going into mouselook mode and using the interact button instead of the mouse.
(
Inquisition
) Masterwork schematics imported in Haven using the
Golden Nug
do not have masterwork slots available. The schematics will function as intended after relocating to
Skyhold
.
Trivia
(
Origins
) If you return to the village of Haven after completing
the Urn of Sacred Ashes
, a graveyard (previously not accessible) becomes available behind one of the buildings in the lower village. The gravestones have
entertaining messages
from the development team.
(
Origins
) The
Avvar
were originally supposed to be involved in the quest
The Urn of Sacred Ashes
. During production, one of the origin backstories was set to be an Avvar from the surrounding area. However, this was later changed as the team did not have the resources to develop a new art style for the barbarians. This was prior to the conceptualization of Haven as a village populated by Andrastian dragon cultists.

Dragon Age: Inquisition
Haven Chantry
is the chantry located in the
Village of Haven
. It is found on a high hill, above the level of the rest of the village.
Dragon Age: Origins
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Unlike mainstream Chantries, the chantry of Haven is run by the
Disciples of Andraste
. It is led by
Father Eirik
, who is giving a sermon when
the Warden
enters. After a brief discussion with Eirik, he attacks the Warden and a fight ensues. Afterward the Warden can find
Brother Genitivi
behind a secret door. The brick wall can slide away and reveal the hidden area.

Dragon Age: Inquisition
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
Haven as the Inquisition's Stronghold
Following the disaster at the
Temple of Sacred Ashes
, forces loyal to
Divine
Justinia V
used the Haven Chantry as their base of operations. A few days later, the Chantry became the headquarters of the
restored Inquisition
, founded on Justinia's orders. The Chantry served in this capacity until the Inqusition was successful in sealing
the Breach
. Shortly thereafter, forces loyal to
Corypheus
assaulted Haven. When the Elder One revealed his
red lyrium dragon
, the Inquisition and the people of Haven retreated to the Chantry for shelter, before fleeing using a hidden escape route. The Haven Chantry was likely destroyed along with the rest of the village when the
Herald of Andraste
triggered a massive avalanche.

Location
Hawke Estate
Type
Manor
Terrain
Residence
Location
Hightown
,
Kirkwall
Inhabitants
Hawke
Leandra Amell

Hightown
Appearances
Dragon Age II
Dragon Age: Inquisition
(mentioned)
The
Hawke Estate
is the ancestral seat of the
Amell family
which later passes into the possession of the
Hawke
cadet-branch of the family.
Background
The Hawke estate, formerly known as the
Amell Estate
, was owned by the parents of
Leandra Amell
. Once the Amells passed, their will stated that the estate be left to Leandra and all of her children. However, Leandra was unable to attend the funeral, having just given birth to her twins
Carver
and
Bethany
. Thus, she never got to see the will, leaving Leandra's younger brother
Gamlen
to take control of the estate and all Amell family fortunes.
Gamlen got into trouble with slavers, and ended up having to "sell" the estate to them.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age II
.
Hawke
and their sibling searches the
estate cellar
while investigating their mother's
Birthright
. The estate later comes into the ownership of Hawke after Hawke purchases it back using funds gained from
The Deep Roads Expedition
. Leandra,
Sandal
, and
Bodahn Feddic
move in with Hawke. It is also possible to have
Dog
and
Anders
or
Merrill
, if they are a romance companion, live there with Hawke as well. Hawke can also recruit the elf
Orana
as a slave or servant who will also reside in the estate from then on. According to in-game dialogue, the Estate has a view of the homes of many other neighbouring nobles, such as the “Arenburgs”.
The estate is also where
Cassandra
is interrogating
Varric
in 9:40
Dragon
.
Places
Amell Estate Cellar
Characters
Hawke
Leandra Amell
Sandal
Bodahn Feddic

(conditional)

(conditional)

(conditional)
Merrill
(conditional)
Notable items

Special objects

Storage Chest
Codex entries
Thedas Calendar
Notes
During Act 1 at night, the Amell family crest is displayed outside the estate in
Hightown
, but it is the damaged version, also seen at end of the game.
During Act 2, the fixed up crest is displayed during the day, but at night the damaged version appears instead.

Location
Herald's Rest
Type

Location

Dragon Age: Inquisition
Magekiller
Herald's Rest
is a
tavern
located in
Skyhold
. It becomes available once the
Inquisition
assumes control of the fortress.
A place for all in service here,
To rest, recoup, and persevere.
Though weighs the heart, remember best,
Your saving grace, the Herald's Rest.
—
Welcome sign
in the tavern
Involvement
Herald's Rest sign
The Herald's Rest tavern is a place for soldiers and other members of the Inquisition to gather for relaxation. It is also the location of certain
companions
, offering a place in which the Inquisitor can engage with them in private. There are three levels to the tavern, with a companion found on each one.
The tavern employs a bard,
Maryden Halewell
, and through the course of the Inquisition's journey, her songs change in accordance with various events and decisions made by the Inquisitor.

PAGE: Hercinia
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Hercinia
======================================================================

Location
Hercinia
Type
City-State
Terrain
Urban
Location
Free Marches
Inhabitants
Humans
Elves
Appearances
Dragon Age: Inquisition
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: Magekiller
Hercinia
is a palatial city-state in the
Free Marches
located on the Amaranthine coast. It is known for its taxes and frequent raider activity.
Background
Like the Marcher states of
Kirkwall
and
Starkhaven
, Hercinia has tried and failed to claim the island of
Estwatch
, a notoriously lawless port held by the
Felicisma Armada
, whose raiders often target Hercinia.
The agent "Mollnir," a suspected alias of the notorious
Black Hart
revealed a conspiracy against the Crown in Hercinia.
One of the inmates in the
Aeonar
was rumored to be a noble from Hercinia, hidden by his family as he practiced blood magic on their servants. He quickly became an
abomination
and was killed by the
templars
.
Involvement
Dragon Age: Magekiller
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Magekiller
.
Docks of Hercinia
Marius
and
Tessa Forsythia
have been hired to rescue a young man named Buckingham from a
maleficar
. After they safely return the hostage to his family, they are followed to their inn by
Flavius
, a slave in the service of
Magister
Illeneva, who asks them for help in taking down a group of blood mages in
Tevinter
.
Dragon Age: Inquisition
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
In the process of
investigating
Lord Enzo of
Antiva
's connections to the
Venatori
,
the Inquisitor
discovers a Venatori-held storehouse just outside Hercinia, which is being used as a staging ground for smuggling
red lyrium
into Antiva. After fleeing to Hercinia, Lord Enzo is killed by either the
Antivan Crows
or
Zevran Arainai
.
References

Location
High Anderfels
Type
Desert
Terrain

Location
Anderfels
Inhabitants
Grey Wardens
Griffons
Enemies
Darkspawn
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
The
High Anderfels
is a remote region in the
Anderfels
.
Background
The
Grey Wardens
has chosen the High Anderfels as the location of their Griffon Aerie, where they train a new generation of
griffons
. Since the Grey Wardens desires to keep the survival of griffons a secret, the isolation of the High Anderfels will serve them well.
Involvement
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
Rook and their companions visit the High Anderfels as part of the quest
A Warden's Best Friend
where they are tasked with finding Grey Wardens who are willing to assist them in their dealings with darkspawn and blight.
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
During this outing, Rook's party meets up with two Warden contacts sought out by
Harding
,
Evka
and
Antoine
. They point the team in the direction of a few Wardens responsible for training juvenile
griffons
, born from a recently rediscovered clutch of eggs. The team assists one of the Wardens,
Davrin
, in investigating the sudden appearance of the
Gloom Howler
, a seemingly intelligent darkspawn responsible for hunting Grey Wardens. The team fights off waves of darkspawn only to lose all but one griffon to the Gloom Howler, who steals them away for unknown purposes.

A Warden's Best Friend

PAGE: Highever
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Highever
======================================================================

Location
Highever
Type
Teyrnir
Location
Ferelden
Population
20,000
(9:30
Dragon
)
Inhabitants
Humans
Elves
Dwarves
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
The Darkspawn Chronicles
(mentioned)
Witch Hunt
(mentioned)
Dragon Age II
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: Inquisition
(mentioned)
Situated on the north coast of
Ferelden
,
Highever
is the ancestral home of the
Couslands
and one of only two
Teyrnirs
left in Ferelden.
The
heraldic device
of Highever are two green spears crossed in front of a pale green raindrop. The heraldic device of the ruling
Cousland family
is a laurel wreath, which resembles two white wings.
Background
Highever heraldry
Castle Cousland
has existed since the
Divine Age
, making it somewhere around 800 - 850 years old. Originally an outpost of the then-expanding
Amaranthine
Bannorn
, it was ruled by the Elstan family, cousins of the
Howe family
. During the
Towers Age
(approximately 300 years later), however, Bann Conobar Elstan was allegedly murdered by his wife,
Flemeth
, which ended the bloodline. Conobar's captain of the guard, Sarim Cousland, acquired land and title, which the Cousland family has controlled ever since.
Eventually, the Couslands declared their independence from the Amaranthine, and instigated a war that lasted for thirty years. When the war finally ended, Highever was not only granted independence, but also half of what used to be southwestern Amaranthine..
In 4:80 Black, during the first Orlesian invasion of Ferelden, Highever was sacked by Orlesian forces.
During the
Black Age
, Highever became a teyrnir almost by default after the ruling Cousland (scholars disagree as to whether this was Mather or Haelia Cousland) battled, and defeated, the
werewolf
threat encroaching upon the land.
Cousland forces leaving Highever to fight the
Fifth Blight
.
During the
Unification of Ferelden
, Teyrna Elethea Cousland fought
King Calenhad
in an attempt to maintain Highever's independence. When King Calenhad's army reached Highever, Teyrna Elethea led the local banns against him, but Calenhad won. After Elethea's men were defeated, Calenhad asked her to swear fealty. She did, thus allowing the Couslands to retain the teyrnir.
In the present, Highever is one of only two teyrnirs, the other being
Gwaren
, granting the Cousland family power and influence second only to the
King
.
Places
Castle Cousland
Harper's Ford‎
Highever
alienage
Inhabitants
The former rulers of Highever are
Teyrn Bryce Cousland
and
Teyrna Eleanor Cousland
. Players who select the
Human Noble
origin will play as the second child of the Couslands, and the younger sibling of
Fergus Cousland
.
Arl Rendon Howe
, during the early part of the game, will usurp the Teyrnir of Highever from the Couslands. Either the
attack
is implicitly mentioned if the Warden is not a Human Noble, or witnessed first-hand during the Warden's Origin if he or she is a Human Noble.
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
After the Warden kills Howe and the
Blight
is finally thwarted, Highever is returned to the surviving Couslands (Fergus being the only survivor should the player not choose the human noble origin). As the eldest child of Bryce, Fergus is made Teyrn of Highever.
Highever's
alienage
is home to
hahren
Sarethia.
Notes
Although present on the world map, it's impossible to go or return to Castle Cousland, Human Noble origin or not.
If you talk to the
Mad Hermit
in the
East Brecilian Forest
and are a mage, human noble or dwarf, he will ask you where you came from. One of the choices is to say you came from Highever.
During the Landsmeet's introduction scene, if you are a Human Noble, you can tell Loghain that you are Teyrn of Highever, after which you are rebuked by Arl Howe.
Highever was home to several of the most famous figures in the Fereldan history.
Human Noble Warden
,
Flemeth
, and
Calenhad Theirin
were once living in this region.
Duncan
was also born here.
References

Location
Hightown
Type
District
Terrain

Location
Kirkwall
,

Appearances
Dragon Age II
The Black Emporium
(mentioned)
Legacy
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: Inquisition
(mentioned)
Trespasser
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: Hard in Hightown
Hightown
is the wealthy district of
Kirkwall
. As its name implies, it sits on the higher part of the city.
Background
At the height of the
Tevinter Imperium
's
slave
trade,
Kirkwall
's elite prospered beyond dreams of avarice.
Hightown
was built for the wealthiest slavers, its glitzy mansions rising atop a great wall of rock that borders, on one side, the
Waking Sea
.
Lowtown
cowered on its other side until Kirkwall's slaves rose to plunder and destroy Hightown's riches.
Today, Hightown's prominent buildings are
the Keep
, home to the ruling viscount, and
the chantry
, home to the
grand cleric
and the city's religious center. Both are converted estates that once housed wealthy
magisters
, rebuilt and converted after the uprising.
—From
In Pursuit of Knowledge: The Travels of a Chantry Scholar
, by Brother
Genitivi
—
From
Kirkwall - Hightown
Everyone who is anyone in
Kirkwall
lives in
Hightown
, and they give little thought to what goes on in
Lowtown
other than to complain when the wind drags up the stench from its foundries or ancient mines. People in Hightown feel safe, not because the city's walls are impregnable, but because an invader would need to scale the stairs from Lowtown in order to reach them. Many bloody battles have been fought on those narrow stairs, and in several wars Hightown has held out for months after Lowtown was taken. The wealthy often forget, however, that those stairs are also their only escape.
The rich estates of Hightown fall into three groups. The dwarven palaces are mostly located in the Dwarven Enclave. The Enclave is known for the imposing Paragon statues erected around the area. The Foreign Quarter is where the Orlesian and Antivan merchants prefer to stay while in Kirkwall. The most successful of these merchants will have ship captains, shop clerks and accountants all in their employ. It is typical for these merchants to make biannual trips to Kirkwall to manage their holdings. The human nobility of Kirkwall form the third group; some families are able to trace their lineages all the way back to Orlesian and even Tevinter notables. The oldest and grandest families reside in imposing mansions.
In the lowest alley of the district, several merchants peddle their wares. In a higher alley, the
Dwarven Merchants' Guild
is headquartered with one street leading to the
Blooming Rose
and the other to the
Viscount's Keep
and the
Hawke Estate
. The street eventually leads to the
Kirkwall Chantry
. A small staircase from there leads to the several estates of Kirkwall's elite. Although Hightown is a tranquil section of Kirkwall during the day, many gang operations surface during night time: Hightown is a center for many criminal factions.

Notes
In the
Hightown Estates
the large rectangular courtyard at the west end of the estates (furthest point west in all of Hightown) contains a hidden alley that doesn't appear on the minimap (easily detected visually though). It is the only area in Hightown that extends outside of the borders on the minimap, you can find others along the Wounded Coast. During Act 1 it has random loot during the day and night, no loot during Act 2 and then a
Master
level locked chest during Act 3 at night (40
cunning
to open - 200 XP).  This alley is also a spawn point during the initial fight during
Best Served Cold
.
There is a book on a stand containing
Codex entry: The Dwarven Merchants Guild
, it is found between
Bartrand Tethras
and
Bodahn Feddic
during Act 1. It can be very easily missed because it does not show up when inspecting the area for "interactable" objects.

The
Hillside Path
is a
random encounter
triggered during
Dragon Age: Origins
. The encounter occurs while traveling with
Wynne
—in the active party across the world map—only after
the Warden
asks if she has ever
encountered abominations outside of the Circle
.
Background
The party is ambushed by a large group of
darkspawn
.
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Upon approaching the map exit after the fight, a short cut-scene will play showing Wynne fall to the ground.  The next time the party returns to camp, Wynne will reveal what happened to her in the
Circle Tower
before the Warden's arrival.  This conversation is required to trigger the
random encounter
in which Wynne gains her
Vessel of the Spirit
ability.

.

.
“
Fereldans
are a puzzle. As a people, they are one bad day away from reverting to barbarism... if you insult their
dogs
, they are likely to declare war.”
―
Empress Celene I
Location
Hinterlands

Vale

Appearances
Dragon Age: The Stolen Throne
Dragon Age: Origins
Dragon Age: Inquisition
The
Hinterlands
is a region in
Ferelden
located between the fortress of
Ostagar
and
Redcliffe
.
Background
The Hinterlands comprises a good deal of fertile agricultural land and as such is populated with a great many farmsteads throughout the area. The regions' main settlement is
Redcliffe Village
, situated in the western hills of
Ferelden
protected by
Redcliffe Castle
. Since the end of the
Fifth Blight
, the Hinterlands have seen an influx of settlers from
Redcliffe Village
looking to escape unpleasant memories of the
darkspawn
and the other dark
events
which befell
Redcliffe
during the last
Blight
.
Nestled in the heart of
Ferelden
are the old forests and farmsteads of the Hinterlands. This rocky, rolling getway to Redcliffe has now fallen into chaos. The conflict between
mages
and
templars
forced many off their lands,
demons
stalk the hills, and reports of strange magic abound near Redcliffe.
—Excerpt from the quest journal
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
"I retired to the Hinterlands for peace and quiet away from the politics, and because the wide open spaces were perfect to let my horses run. Instead, the war between the mages and the templars has turned your beautiful hills into a series of burning battlefields. The farmers who live in the Hinterlands are good folk. Many of them left
Redcliffe village
because they couldn't bear to be there anymore, not after the
Blight
and the walking dead left so many bad memories. Now we've got apostates running around setting fire to anyone who looks at them sideways, and templars looting houses and cutting down those who protest as mage sympathizers."
—
Dennet
, former horsemaster to
Arl Teagan
of Redcliffe
Ever since the outbreak of the
Mage-Templar War
, the Hinterlands has suffered from ongoing confrontations between
apostate
mages and rebel templars. The local population has been victimized from attacks by both sides.
The Inquisitor
travels to the Hinterlands to seek out Mother
Giselle
, who has been tending to refugees in the region, in an attempt to gain some
Chantry
support. The Inquisitor can also restore order to the
region
by helping the inhabitants in a variety of ways, including putting an end to the Mage-Templar conflict in the region.
Ocularum
shards
, which had remained hidden in plain sight until the
Breach
disrupted the magic that kept them hidden, are scattered about the Hinterlands.
After the discovery of a workshop on the Redcliffe docks, the origins of the Ocularum can be traced back to the
Venatori
, who have been killing
tranquil
mages to use their skulls to uncover the shards. After obtaining shards, the Inquisitor can look into the significance of the shards
collected
and why the Venatori are so interested in acquiring them.
The Inquisitor also finds themselves investigating an abundance of mercenary activity in the region.
This leads to the discovery of an underground
lyrium
smuggling operation run out of
Valammar
, an ancient hidden
thaig
, by the
Carta
.
The Inquisitor can gain access to the Hinterlands by completing the
Scout the Hinterlands
war table
operation in
Haven
.

Trivia
During the
epilogue
of
Dragon Age: Origins
, a Dalish Warden may ask that the Dalish be officially granted territorial sovereignty. The following possible conversation between Alistair and Merrill during
King Alistair
suggests a reason why no Dalish are seen in the Hinterlands as of Inquisition.
Merrill
: We… we heard the Dalish were given land in Ferelden. Is it true?
Alistair
: Yes. I wish I could say that went better.
Merrill
: Why? What happened?
Alistair
: It’s… a long story. I intend to make it up to your people, however. I owe an old friend of mine too much to do otherwise.
Bugs
ps3
ps4
xbox360
xboxone
The player's controller may not vibrate while traveling across the Hinterlands, regardless of whether or not the "Vibrate" function in the "Options" menu is turned 'on' or 'off'. The feature however seems to properly trigger while in Valammar, The Gull & Lantern, and Redcliffe's Chantry.

PAGE: Hissing Wastes

Location
Hissing Wastes
Type
Desert
Terrain
Dunes
Location
Western Orlais
Inhabitants
Dwarves
(formerly)
Venatori
Red Templars
Enemies
Venatori
Red Templars
Spiders
Lurkers
Varghests
Wyverns
Dragonlings
Appearances
Dragon Age: Magekiller
Dragon Age: Inquisition
The
Hissing Wastes
is a large stretch of desert in western
Orlais
.
Background
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
The sun burned above oceans of sand, but in the sand was Stone, strong and true.
Fairel
hewed the Stone, and built—as great as any
thaig
in the
deep
. And with his sons' help, he ensured the thaig prospered and grew. Fairel,
Paragon
, fled from the strife his brilliance created, the strife that destroyed thaigs, sundered
houses
, from weapons that
clan
used against clan. His own clan and his two sons followed Fairel to the pitiless
surface
, the surface where they would hide from the war that took their home.
After many years Fairel, greatest of Paragons, could not bear life's burden. And with the burden growing, he called his sons to his bedside. He bade each son swear he would take care of his brother, and the brothers swore, and mourned when their father returned to
the Stone
. Fairel's sons built monuments to their father, locking away his great works, and worked together, for a time, side by side. Each ruled half the thaig, but each ruled differently. They argued, and heated words made the brothers duel, and where one brother fell, the other raised bloodied axe in hand, alone.
This is the tale of Fairel, Paragon among Paragons, father of two sons, who, against their father's wishes, fought from foolish words and foolish pride. For pride these halls were made—to honor a father's deeds, and grieve his loss. And for loss these halls were made, to honor a brother mourned. A father taken by time, a brother dead by my own hand. With this work behold my grief, in Stone and shifting sand.
The Hissing Wastes is a stretch of sandy desert north of the
Western Approach
, a lonely place where the wildlife is hungry and hostile. Among the hungriest and most hostile are the
dragons
that make their nests here. The rare person wandering into the wastes is either hunting prized beasts or hoping to uncover evidence of a
long-vanished civilization
in the dunes.
—Excerpt from the quest journal
Involvement
Dragon Age: Magekiller
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Magekiller
.
Tessa Forsythia
and
Marius
are sent to the Hissing Wastes to deal with Venatori excavating the ruins. Unable to deal with the problem, Tessa asks
Charter
to send additional support. This support comes in the form of
Dorian Pavus
and some of the
Bull's Chargers
led by
Krem
. Their main target is a camp of fifty Venatori. While Dorian and Marius provide a distraction, Tessa frees the captive slaves; once all the slaves are clear, Rocky, the Chargers' sapper, brings the cliff wall down on the camp, killing the remaining Venatori.
Dragon Age: Inquisition
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
During the conflict between the
Elder One
and the re-established
Inquisition
, the
Venatori
establish a significant presence in the Hissing Wastes. Thousands of years ago,
Paragon
Fairel
fled to the surface with his clan to escape a catastrophic civil war among the
dwarves
waged with his inventions and founded
Kal Repartha
, the only known
thaig
built on the surface. The Venatori are searching the ruins of the long-abandoned colony for the powerful
runes
that Fairel created by excavating his
tomb
.
According to
Solas
, the Hissing Wastes used to be a lush forest, before encroaching sands destroyed the trees' roots. Exploring the Hissing Wastes and establishing Inquisition presence will reduce that of the Venatori and the
Red Templars
. To unravel the
reason
for the Venatori's presence in the desert
the Inquisitor
can gain access to the Wastes by completing the
Stop Venatori Activity in the West
war table
scouting operation in
Skyhold
.

Dragon Age II
The
Holding Caves
are an abandoned set of slave pens located just outside
Kirkwall
dating back to the days when the
Tevinter Imperium
still ruled over the city. It is only accessible during
Fenris
's companion quest
A Bitter Pill
.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age II
.
In Act 2,
Tevinter
hunters catch up with Fenris. After defeating them, Fenris insists on going to the Holding Caves to kill
Hadriana
, the
magister
apprenticed to
Danarius
. Upon arrival,
Hawke
is attacked by slavers guarding the entrance.

Bugs
pc
xbox360
The Holding Caves remain on the world map after A Bitter Pill, and when revisited, the only way to exit is to run through the
Abandoned Slaver Den
. Fenris remains locked into the party until Hawke returns to
Hawke Estate
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Honnleath
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins locations, Locations

Location
Honnleath
Type
Village
Location
Southwest Ferelden
Inhabitants
Humans
Quests
The Golem in Honnleath
Exits
World map
Wilhelm's Cellar
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
-
The Stone Prisoner
Dragon Age: Inquisition
(mentioned)
Honnleath
is a village in southwestern
Ferelden
and part of the Arling of
Redcliffe
.
Background
Honnleath is the home of a
mage
named
Wilhelm Sulzbacher
and his family. Wilhelm was a hero of the
Fereldan Rebellion
against
Orlais
, fighting alongside
Maric Theirin
personally on multiple occasions. Afterwards he was permitted to retire to Honnleath, where he lived with his wife and
son
. A stone statue which the villagers decorate during festivals stands in the village square and is loved by many local birds as a resting and feeding place.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Gallows
Honnleath is overrun by the
Darkspawn
during the
Fifth Blight
.
Matthias
saves several of the villagers using his father's magical defenses installed beneath their home.
As
the Warden
's party arrives, a pair of villagers flee from a trio of
genlocks
. The majority of the citizens have been slaughtered and the town is swarming with
darkspawn
.
The stone statue is in fact a
golem
named
Shale
which can become a
companion
to the Warden.
Places
Wilhelm's Cellar
Characters
Shale
(only after aiding
Matthias
)
Quests
The Golem in Honnleath
Enemies
Map of the area
Darkspawn
Genlock
(13) (
Normal
)
Hurlock
(7) (Normal)
Hurlock alpha
(
Lieutenant
)
The darkspawn are divided into three groups:
First: 3 Genlocks
Second: 5 Genlocks (1 with bow) and 3 Hurlocks
Third: 6 Genlocks (2 with bows), 4 Hurlocks (2 with bows), and the Hurlock alpha
Resources

Elfroot

- source:
Dead Villager
.

- source:
Olaf's Chest
.
Special objects

Codex entries

Trivia
Cullen Rutherford
is originally from Honnleath.

======================================================================
PAGE: Hossberg Wetlands
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Hossberg_Wetlands
======================================================================

Location
Hossberg Wetlands
Type
Bog
Terrain
Marshland
Location
Eastern
Anderfels
Enemies
Darkspawn
Demons
Factions
Grey Wardens
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
The
Hossberg Wetlands
are a swamp in the
Anderfels
, near the capital of
Hossberg
.
Background
The
Blight
has surged in the Hossberg Wetlands, choking the whole area. The Blight, in its new form, struck quickly and as far as
Evka Ivo
and
Antoine
can tell,
Elgar'nan
and
Ghilan'nain
didn't appear in the Wetlands directly. The local village,
Lavendel
, is caught up in the blight surge and the Grey Warden couple has been aiding the village in fending it off and helping the sick.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
Blight
has surged through the Hossberg Wetlands. Strange new growths, almost like boils, have taken root through the area. This time, however,
Antoine
can hear another voice in the blight's song. Wardens
Evka Ivo
and Antoine believe the blight has changed, but they need more proof to show the
First Warden
. The two enlists the
Veilguard
's help in collecting Blight samples. Ultimately, Antoine and Evka tried to convince the First Warden that the blight was changed and the
elven gods
,
Elgar'nan
and
Ghilan'nain
, were responsible, but he refused to listen. Evka and Antoine were ordered back to
Weisshaupt
but promised to continue studying the Blight and update the Veilguard on any new developments.
Ghilan'nain
led her
Darkspawn
and her
Archdemon
Razikale
in an attack on the
Grey Wardens
. Despite the
Veilguard
rushing to guide the Grey Wardens in forming a defense and slaying Razikale, they could not slay Ghilan'nain. Ultimately,
Weisshaupt was blighted; the Veilguard and Grey Wardens were forced to flee the battle
. The surviving Grey Wardens were forced to relocate to
Lavendel
.
Davrin
received word that the
Gloom Howler
has attacked a secret Grey Warden redoubt, the Cauldron. Davrin and the
Veilguard
confront the Gloom Howler at the Cauldron, where Davrin was horrified to learn that the monster was there to extract
Archdemon
blood in a dastardly plan to
blight
the kidnapped
griffons
. The Gloom Howler believes she is protecting the new generation of griffons from suffering the same fate as the griffons who died from the
Fourth Blight
and sees her mad plan as the only way to save them. The Gloom Howler escaped but leaves behind a strip of cloth for Davrin and
Assan
to track her with.
The Gaspin family settled in Gaspin Manor but a door Lord Gaspin could not initially open drove him to obsession. Lord Gaspin was believed to be influenced by a sealed
demon
, and when he became disappointed by the contents of the room he finally had access to, he sealed the door again. It is unknown what happened to Lord Gaspin, whether he died from the demon or became one, but Gaspin Manor fell to ruin and rumors emerged that it was cursed and held some sort of treasure. The
Blight
stirred something in the manor and many feared it could threaten
Lavendel
. A male
Venatori
leader, believing Gaspin Manor contains a great power that will ingratiate him to
Elgar'nan
, mobilized a Venatori group to the Hossberg Wetlands in order to loot the hidden treasure of Gaspin Manor. The Venatori however, could not breach the magical barriers protecting the supposed treasure and the Venatori leader was recently killed by a sealed demon called Consuming Obsession in the cellar. As the Venatori combed Gaspin Manor, the Venatori encountered the Veilguard and were defeated. The Veilguard also defeated Consuming Obsession, who was luring treasure hunters to their deaths with their desire for treasure.
One of Ghilan'nain's
blighted
dragons
is spotted in the Hossberg Wetlands. The Grey Wardens assemble a defense in the area and endure a series of hit and run attacks from the darkspawn. A few Wardens try to lure out the blighted dragon, who has settled in a ruined tower, but were unsuccessful and led to eleven Grey Warden casualties. The Veilguard arrives in Hossberg to help;
Taash
lures out the blighted dragon in the open. As the Veilguard and Grey Wardens engange the blighted dragon, Ghilan'nain and her second blighted dragon attack the Veilguard Alliance. As the battle rages on, either the
Shadow Dragons
or the
Antivan Crows
arrive with specialized ballistas to aid the Veilguard Alliance. Eventually the Veilguard kills the blighted dragons but Ghilan'nain begins to heal them using the Blight. In order to prevent her from reanimating the dragons,
Rook
shoots her with a ballista bolt and wounds her. Before the Veilguard Alliance can press the attack,
Elgar'nan
arrives to teleport Ghilan'nain to safety. Before they leave, Rook overhears that the elven gods are plotting something in
Arlathan Forest
.
After defending Lavendel from a small horde of darkspawn,
Antoine
sensed a new, evil
Calling
in the blight. Antoine explained that at first, Antoine sensed the blighted elven gods, but then the blight song he began hearing called to something dark in the Wetlands. He claimed the intelligence emitting the new blight song felt pleased.
Evka and Antoine learned that after the elven gods changed the blight, it may have independently altered itself. The Blight is now answering to something other than the elven gods. The Grey Warden couple believes the source of the new blight song controlling the Blight is a blight eruption, a construct of the Blight. If the eruption can call the blight, it could also affect the Grey Wardens and spark the Calling sooner. Evka and Antoine enlist Rook's aid in finding and destroying the blight eruption while the Grey Wardens guard any territory the Blight hasn't spread to yet. Antoine provides Rook his explosive, alchemical flame concoction to destroy the blight eruption. After destroying the Blight eruption, Rook reports back to Evka and Antoine at the Sheltered Glade. Evka and Antoine explained that after the Blight eruption was destroyed, the blight withdrew and the bigger tendrils pulled back. When the Blight withdrew, flowers that survived by being buried and untouched by the blight began to open up, and the land became healthy enough for the flowers to return.
Places
A map of the various zones in the Hossberg Wetlands.
Blighted Grove
- An area in the central Wetlands with a large Blighted tree which can compel Wardens who hear it.
Blighted Shallows
- A large, flat section of southeast Hossberg not accessible until beginning
Fire and Ice
. This area remains accessible after this quest. It also has
a hidden achievement
.
Blighted Tunnel
- Small connective tunnels in northeastern Hossberg. Blight boils drop from the ceiling regularly and will detonate as Rook passes. On initial arrival to the Wetlands, only this passage out of Lavendel will be open.
Castle Gates
- The foreground of the abandoned Warden castle in Hossberg.
Castle Grounds
- The large grounds of the expansive castle surrounding the abandoned fortress, the
Cauldron
. Not accessible until beginning
The Cauldron
.
Cavernous Tunnels
- Large connective tunnels in eastern Hossberg. These are initially blocked by a very long chain of Venatori crystals, then later fill with darkspawn. There is evidence of deliberate excavation in some of the larger rooms.
Flooded Farmlands
- Farmlands east of Lavendel and the home of the Voss family. This area has been destroyed by the Blight, and a mysterious well (X on the map) may be related to the destruction. A ballista rests in the center of the farms, but it cannot be used until the wall to the south (where the Blight feeder trail runs) breaks during
Something Wrong
.
Gaspin Manor
- A large haunted manor in eastern Hossberg. Its previous owner, Lord Gaspin, died under mysterious circumstances. Many Venatori can be found here as part of
An Academic Interest
, as well as several chests and puzzles. A wisp is needed to open the southern gate to Solitude's Edge. From the Gaspin Manor courtyard, pair each of three Warden statues with a brazier, which will light green when correctly paired. This will open a wooden door on the left side of the upper courtyard, which gives access to the needed wisp.
East statue: rotate once clockwise.
Northwest statue: rotate twice.
Southeast statue: rotate once counterclockwise.
Grey Hold
- The northernmost point of the accessible Wetlands and the home of the region's eluvian. Antoine and Evka can be found here, as well as the faction merchants
Adal
(pre-
Siege of Weisshaupt
) and
Holden
and
Mila
(post-Weisshaupt). Many Warden survivors of Weisshaupt resettle here.
Inner Courtyard
- The courtyard of the Warden fortress
the Cauldron
, not accessible until
The Cauldron
begins.
Lavendel
- A city in Hossberg recently overrun with Blight and darkspawn. Many civilians have retreated back to Grey Hold to seek the Wardens' protection.
Lavendel Barricade
- Just east of Lavendel, the barricade serves to hold back much of the encroaching darkspawn. This area is initially blocked until Rook opens the door from behind during
Something Wrong
.
Lost Village
- A village in central Hossberg recently lost to the Blight with no apparent survivors. Not accessible until
Something Wrong
has been completed.
Old Crossroads
- A region in eastern Hossberg with a high stone wall, likely previously used to defend the farmers from attack. The Blight Cyst containing
The Disciple-in-Ichor
can be found here.
Sheltered Glade
- A region just west of Lavendel with several groves of Brona's Bloom. A ladder to the south can be dropped from the Swamp Road side for future access. Blight blocks the westward promontory until completing
Something's Coming
.
Solitude's Edge
- A large section of the eastern Wetlands with several notable features:
A cabin smothered in Blight, inaccessible until beginning
The Cabin in the Blight
A Blight Cyst housing
Amarell the Unwoven
A short stone pillar near the Blight Cyst burning with green flame, containing one of the enemies of
Pinnacle of Its Kind
A northern passage blocked with Blight until completing
Something's Coming
Two large Blight Abscesses to the east, part of a chest puzzle
The exit to Gaspin Manor, which must be opened from the manor side
Swamp Road
- A road in central Hossberg leading to the southern portion of the map. The southern exit is blocked until the completion of
Something's Wrong
.
The Blight Eruption
- A large deposit of Blight. Inaccessible outside of the quest
Something's Coming
.
The Cauldron
- An ancient Grey Warden redoubt in the southwestern Wetlands, hidden for centuries. Many dangerous and secret Warden artifacts are hidden here. Most of this section is not accessible outside of the quest
The Cauldron
.

Phoenix

This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.

The Profane and the Lost
, they can be found nesting in Grey Hold.
Enemies

Darkspawn

The Formless One
Venatori
Chest locations
A map of the chests and chest clues in the Hossberg Wetlands.
Eighteen regular chests, two lyrium-dagger chests (20 chests total), and four Evanuris statues are required for 100% completion. One additional chest appears on the exploration map but does
not
count towards the 100% total (chest #19). See the map for individual locations.
An open wall on the west side of this building allows access to this chest.
Several feeder cysts must be destroyed to access this chest, including one far to the west over the wall to Lavendel Barricade. One of the last cysts (inside the same building as the chest) can be tricky, but Rook can shoot it from certain careful angles here, including from the ground to the west. This chest is warded; Neve's Unbind Wards ability must be used to open it.
Break the Blight boil blocking the ladder inside the destroyed hut just northeast. Climb up the ladder and some stone ledges to access this chest.
One of two lyrium chests. Sealed by Blight until the completion of
Something's Coming
.
After breaking the long chain of Venatori feeder crystals and exploding crystals, take the scaffolding to the upper level and use Neve's Unbind Wards ability on the blue warded wall. The chest is just behind it.
After breaking all the Venatori crystals, break the wooden barricade blocking access to this hall. Use Neve's ability to unbind the blue ward wall and continue into the hidden room. Defeat
Consuming Obsession
and any spawned
demons
to access this chest.
This chest is tricky and has several steps.
Stand as close to the Blight-blocked main passage as possible, then climb up two levels of stone ledges to Rook's right.
Shoot the Blight boil straight ahead (visible over the blocked passage) and then the feeder boil to Rook's left, which allows the central passage to now be cleared.
Walk through the passage and clear the Blistering Drakestone with Taash's dragonfire, then use Assan's Blight Hunter ability on the Abscess high on the wall (now behind and above Rook) and destroy the revealed cyst.
Use Assan's ability on the Abscess again, then continue left around the opened passageway and shoot the newly activated cyst to the upper left. Clear the main passageway to finally access the chest.
Sealed by Blight until the completion of
Something's Coming
.
This chest is hidden by a breakable wooden barricade to the right of the barred gate out of Gaspin Manor.
After clearing all the Venatori and their crystals, enter Gaspin Manor and take the stairs up immediately to the left. Enter the archway straight ahead, and then take a long running jump to clear the gap. While it appears too wide to jump, Rook can grab the far edge with enough speed and lead-up. Take the plank up to the right for a
memento
, a
note
, and a shortcut ladder which can be dropped.
Two large Blight Abscesses block access to this chest. One is directly atop the house; there is another up a ledge behind and to Rook's left which must also be destroyed, along with all feeder vessels for both Abscesses. Note that both Abscesses must be activated simultaneously to destroy one of the central cysts. Once all cysts have been destroyed and Rook has access to the interior, the back left wooden wall can be broken to reach the chest behind the building.
One of two lyrium chests. Destroy the Blight Cyst and defeat
Amarell the Unwoven
.
From ground level, use Assan's Blight Hunter ability on the Blight boil, then shoot the two connecting feeder cysts. (If having trouble destroying these, Rook may have a better angle if standing atop the nearby elevated platform.) Climb up the newly accessible ladder; this statue puzzle requires each of three Warden statues to face a paired lamp, which will light green when correct. When all three are paired, jump across the small gap to access the chest.
Northern statue nearest the ladder: one turn clockwise.
Southeastern statue (corner): two turns.
Southwestern statue: this statue's lamp has fallen off the platform and is on the ground to the north. Rotate the statue once clockwise to pair it.
Sealed by Blight until the completion of
Something's Coming
.
Not accessible until beginning
Lives Spared
. Clearing the new Blight from this area will open the passageway towards the water and allow access to the last Warden(s) and this chest.
Tightrope across the wooden beam to access this chest.
Not accessible until the completion of
Fire and Ice
. Turn left at the roundpole fence and loop left around the rocks. Collapse the Resonant Slab to destroy all three Blight boils, then jump across the gap. The chest is through a breakable wooden wall to the right.
Activate the wisp at the yellow X with Emmrich's Placate Wisp ability, then carry it through the Blight to the receptacle at the red X. A ladder will drop immediately next to Rook that will allow access to the chest and a
memento
. Note: this chest is trapped.
Within a destructible blight cyst as part of the
Cauldron quest
. This chest is not accessible outside of this quest, and it does
not
count towards 100% map completion.
Jump or take the ladder down to the lowest level of the curved wooden platforms. Use Emmrich's Soothe Torment ability to calm the wisp, Drag the wisp up the ladder, then follow the platforms up and around to the left to the wisp receptacle. A ladder will drop to the left; take it, then jump across the gap to reach the chest.
Not accessible until acquiring
The Cauldron
. Take the southeast stairs of the Inner Courtyard up to the second level, then jump across the gap to reach the chest.
Evanuris altars
A map of the Evanuris altar locations in the Hossberg Wetlands.
Four Evanuris
altars
(two
Fen'Harel
, two
Evanuris
) can be found in the Hossberg Wetlands.
This Fen'Harel statue is in the eastern Flooded Farmlands. The statue is across the water on the roof of a small building. Use Harding's Stone Song on the stone behind the building to reach the roof.
This Fen'Harel statue is in the Lost Village. The statue is across the water, but the water is too deep for Rook. Destroy the surrounding Blight to the left to reveal a small jump and some stone ledges which lead up instead.
This Evanuris altar is blocked by a warded wall within Gaspin Manor. Take the left stairs upwards and jump down into the large room straight ahead. Rook can now use Neve's ability to unbind the warded door from behind and allow future access. The altar (which bears the mark of
Sylaise
) is straight ahead.
This Evanuris altar in the Inner Courtyard cannot be accessed until beginning
The Cauldron
. The altar (which bears the mark of
Mythal
) is on ground level to the south.

Trivia
There are several NPC banter series which take place in the Hossberg Wetlands.
In eastern Lavendel, a fisherman discusses his frantic escape from the Blight with a friend.
In a hut near the merchant Hansel, a young man named Jakob watches over his father's sickbed.
A dyemaker near the southern Lavendel beacon checks on her friend.
After Weisshaupt, Martin and Flynn discuss Flynn's role in the Wardens.

======================================================================
PAGE: Hunter Fell
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Hunter_Fell
======================================================================

Location
Hunter Fell
Type
City
Terrain
Urban
Location
Central Nevarra
Inhabitants
Humans
Elves
Dwarves
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: Inquisition
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: Tevinter Nights
Hunter Fell
is a city in
Nevarra
. It is located along the northern shore of the
Minanter
river, west of
Nevarra City
.
History
In 2:46
Glory
,
Caspar Pentaghast
of Hunter Fell deposed King Ionas before he could run
Nevarra
into the ground or hand it over to
Orlais
or
Starkhaven
.
The
Third Blight
began in 3:10
Towers
with the awakening of the
Old God
Toth
. After first attacking southern Tevinter and northern Orlais, the
darkspawn
horde moved east. By 3:18, they had ravaged the bustling cities found along the Minanter river. The
Grey Wardens
eventually convinced the reluctant nations of Orlais and Tevinter to send aid to the beleaguered city-states of the Free Marches. Their armies met the Wardens in Hunter Fell in 3:25 and defeated the
Archdemon
Toth in the Battle of Hunter Fell. It is said to be one of the bloodiest battles in history, with darkspawn corpses piled a hundred feet high.
The blade of the Warden who killed Toth shattered in three pieces upon striking the killing blow and lay unclaimed on the battlefield for years. Eventually, a Nevarran woman picked up the pieces and sold them to a blacksmith, who reforged the pieces into identical daggers,
Thorn of the Dead Gods
.
Celebrations were cut short when the victorious armies of Orlais and Tevinter turned on the Marcher cities. Hunter Fell was claimed by the Imperium, while Orlais captured Nevarra City. Hunter Fell fought back against occupation and eventually regained its independence in 3:49.
When
Tylus Van Markham
deposed King Nestor Pentaghast in 5:37
Exalted
, the surviving Pentaghasts fled to Hunter Fell.
Involvement
Dragon Age: Inquisition
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
Iron Bull
's
Ben-Hassrath
contacts tell the
Inquisition
that they suspect one of the "Five Belles of Hunter Fell," Duke Tythas Pentaghast's old adventuring companions and possible lovers, to be a
Venatori
agent. If
the Inquisitor
chooses to investigate and eliminates the correct target, then Duke Tythas cuts off trade with
Tevinter
. If the Inquisitor has the wrong target eliminated, then Duke Tythas remains subject to Venatori influence and Tevinter mercenaries continue to be expedited through the city.
Dragon Age: Tevinter Nights
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Tevinter Nights
.
The plot follows BioWare's own canon
, meaning it may not follow some of the player's choices in the games.
The Dread Wolf Take You
Charter
, a spy of the Inquisition, calls for a meeting of
Thedas
' best spies to share information about
Solas
and his plans to restore the empire of the ancient elves. They meet at an establishment on the docks of Hunter Fell called The Teahouse.
Places
The Teahouse
- a teahouse located on the docks of Hunter Fell
Notable residents

Caspar Pentaghast
– founder of the first
Pentaghast
dynasty
(
Glory Age
)

(
Blessed Age
)

– Duke of Hunter Fell in 9:41

(war table) (conditional)

Trivia
Hunter Fell currency is struck from lead, which can cause lead poisoning in people practicing the "travelers' bend," hiding bent coins underneath the tongue or against the palate for safekeeping.

PAGE: Hunterhorn Mountains
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Hunterhorn_Mountains
======================================================================

Location
Hunterhorn Mountains
Type
Mountain range
Terrain
Mountainous
Location
Western Thedas
Inhabitants
Dwarves
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
(mentioned)
Dragon Age II
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: Inquisition
(mentioned)
The
Hunterhorn Mountains
stretch across western
Thedas
. They form the largest mountain range and hold some of the continent's tallest peaks, including its highest; Mt. Ambrosia.
Background
In the southern reaches of this massive mountain range, within
Orlais
, lie the Gamordan Peaks, which surround from one side the
Western Approach
.
This desert is marked by a great chasm called the Abyssal Rift, and on its edge stands the
Adamant Fortress
once belonged to
Grey Wardens
. This was also the site of a major battle during the
Second Blight
.
The Hunterhorn Mountains then expand north with the
Tirashan
forest to the east. Then the mountain range splits off into several ranges which enter the
Anderfels
. Beneath one of them, to the east, is located the
great thaig
of
Kal-Sharok
, once capital of the
dwarven empire
.
The northeast point of the mountain range ends in the city of
Laysh
, which lies next to the Volca Sea.
The following information is only mentioned in Dragon Age Tabletop. Certain portions of this media may no longer reflect currently established lore.
Griffons
were native to the northern reaches of the Hunterhorn Mountains before their extinction during the
Exalted Age
.
Codex entries
The Western Approach
Note texts
The Art of the Winter Palace (Bear)

. It is here that the Duke conducts his annual Wyvern hunts, attracting many

nobles hoping to climb higher in the Grand Game. The eastern grounds provide an idyllic view of the region, being home to a pristine lake as well as being sheltered beneath the Chateau on the nearby mountainside. Like the rest of the grounds, the eastern hunting grounds have several Ghast nests and are also home to Wyverns' preferred feeding spots.

======================================================================
PAGE: Imperial Highway
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Imperial_Highway
======================================================================

Location
Imperial Highway
Type
Highway
Terrain
Tevinter Architecture
Location
Across
Thedas
Quests
Lothering and the Imperial Highway
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Dragon Age II
The Black Emporium
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: Inquisition
Paying the Ferryman
Dragon Age: Tevinter Nights
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
The
Imperial Highway
is an internationally-spanning road made of stone and
magic
by the
Tevinter Imperium
during the
Ancient Age
in order to connect the distant lands of its vast empire.
Background
The Highway is an elevated road that runs from
Minrathous
to the southwestern Imperium before then turning south to
Nevarra
and crossing the
Silent Plains
and the
Minanter River
, until it reaches the city of
Cumberland
next to the
Waking Sea
. Then it turns west to
Orlais
with one branch leading to
Andoral's Reach
and another going through the Heartlands of Orlais. Then the Highway turns east to
Ferelden
after passing through the
Frostback Mountains
and above
Orzammar
via
Gherlen's Pass
. In Ferelden, it circles
Lake Calenhad
and the
Bannorn
with one branch ending in
Denerim
and the other at
Ostagar
, its southmost point.
A segment of the Highway used to span across the Waking Sea, connecting the branch going west of Val Royeaux with its southern continuation that leads to Lydes.
The Highway is commonly considered an ancient architectural wonder. Its construction took many generations to complete.
The stone was mined by
slaves
, the largest quarry being located near Emerius - now known as
Kirkwall
.
One of the materials used in construction was marble.
The extension of the Highway into the Fereldan Valley was backed by the Krayvan family, a move that would cost them considerable political influence after the resistance of the
Alamarri
tribes.
In -715
Ancient
, the Imperium began a campaign against the Alamarri. Eventually, Tevinters managed to claim lands in the south, which allowed them to extend the Imperial Highway across the Frostback Mountains.
The Fereldan part of the Highway connected two major sites that Tevinter used for magical experimentation: Ostagar in the south and Aeonar in the north.
Lake Calenhad, encircled by the Highway, was also a point of interest to the Imperium - Tevinters believed waters of the Lake to be blessed by
Razikale
and built a tower on its central island where they would conduct magical research.
According to the
dwarves
, construction of the Highway required the use of magic.
It's been suggested the Imperial Highway was created in a joined effort by Tevinter Magisters and the dwarves, although the latter's level of involvement is unclear.
The intended project was never finished, and many sections have fallen into disrepair over the centuries.
Parts of the Highway have been replaced, sometimes mimicking the ancient Tevene style with new elements added.
Nevertheless, it remains useful to many travelers and merchants who are looking for a safe and quick road.
Quests
Lothering and the Imperial Highway

Kirkwall - The Gallows

======================================================================
PAGE: Isana Negat
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Isana_Negat
======================================================================

Location
Isana Negat
Location
Anderfels
Quests
The Heart of the Titan
,
The Eternal Hymn
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
Isana Negat
is an ancient
dwarven
outpost built atop a mountain deep in the
Anderfels
.
Background
Isana Negat was originally built as an
Kal-Sharok
outpost for trade with the
Tevinter Imperium
. The mountain it was built upon had a massive
lyrium
deposit at its heart, and the dwarves added a large network of the
Deep Roads
into the mountain to access the lyrium, process it, and bring it to the surface.
While dwarves traditionally avoided the surface, Isana Negat's connections to the Deep Roads meant that as long as a dwarf stayed within the fort's walls, it would be as though they hadn't left
The Stone
, leaving their
caste
names and classes unaffected.
However, the lyrium in Isana Negat was unusually powerful. According to records, as the
dwarves
followed the lyrium deep into the earth, the miners suffered "maladies of the mind." The lyrium mining site was eventually abandoned and has remained so for a long time.
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
During
The Heart of the Titan
, it is revealed that the mountains and
Deep Roads
that comprise Isana Negat are actually a dead
Titan
.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
The exterior of Isana Negat seen in The Heart of the Titan.
The
Veilguard
first visits Isana Negat as part of
Harding
's
companion quest
The Heart of the Titan
. Several dwarves from Kal-Sharok have gone missing, including
Stalgard
, and Harding insists on finding them—as well as whatever has been hunting her since
The Waiting Stone
.
As the Veilguard descends into the long-abandoned mining tunnels, they discover several dwarves imprisoned in slabs of stone choked with
red lyrium
. They rescue all of them, including Stalgard, though the dwarves cannot explain their survival inside the airless stone and caution Harding against going deeper into the Deep Roads. However, this red lyrium is strange: it is not
blighted
the way red lyrium usually is, only angry. The team continues into the Deep Roads, fighting off
undead
and
demons
lured there by the thinned
Veil
.
The Titan's heart.
Eventually, the tunnels empty into the deepest part of the mountain, where a cavernous room reveals the truth: the entire mountain is a dead Titan, and the Veilguard now stands in its heart. The cavern itself is formed of the Titan's rib cage, heart, spine, and veins, and it is all colored a vivid red with an ancient rage.
When Harding approaches the center of the chamber, a red-lyrium mirror of herself, The Titan's Shade, emerges. This is the creature that has been abducting the Kal-Sharok dwarves to lure Harding to it, and it is both a remnant of the dead Titans' rage and a reflection of Harding's own rejected anger. Harding and Rook confront the Shade—and Harding's own anger—to cleanse the rot within the
Stone
.
A different exterior of Isana Negat as seen during The Eternal Hymn.
Later, Harding and Rook return to Isana Negat as part of
The Eternal Hymn
. Pending Harding's outcome during the Heart of the Titan, she will either speak to Stalgard and Durra, Stalgard's sister, about isatunoll and share a moment of one-ness with both of them; or she will speak to the Oracle about how to move forward from the realization that dwarves were only born from the destruction of the Titans.
Either way, she will come to terms with how the dwarves came to be, and she intends to use the knowledge she gained here at Isana Negat to help the dwarves in the future.

Trivia
The name Isana Negat derives, in part, from the dwarven word for lyrium -
isana
. Negat is a word found only in the phrase
Stalata Negat
, used in the title of a book of dwarven history
. The book's full title, however, implies that
Stalata Negat
might mean "The Stone Unheld", which if true, would make Isana Negat "Lyrium Unheld" - appropriate for a lyrium mining outpost.

======================================================================
PAGE: Jader
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Jader
======================================================================

Location
Jader
Type
City
Terrain
Urban
Location
Eastern Orlais
Inhabitants
Humans
Elves
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: Asunder
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: The Masked Empire
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: Inquisition
(mentioned)
Jader
is a city in
Orlais
. It is situated in the eastern part of the country, on the coast of the
Waking Sea
, right at the border with
Ferelden
.
Background
Jader is the nearest major Orlesian city to Ferelden. Because of this, it often serves as a place for negotiation between the two countries.
Jader claims to be the birthplace of the prophet
Andraste
, vying for the title with several other settlements in Thedas, most notably
Denerim
, the capital of Ferelden.
During the
Fifth Blight
, Jader was one of the cities that experienced a large influx of Fereldan refugees, which led to an outbreak of disease and famine.
Involvement
Dragon Age: Asunder
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Asunder
.
As a
civil war
brews in Orlais, Grand Duke
Gaspard de Chalons
attempts to conscript archmage
Wynne
when she crosses through Jader, but she refuses his offer.
Once Gaspard puts the plan to overthrow Empress Celene into motion, luring Celene out of
Val Royeaux
and launching a coup d'etat, rumors arise that Celene holed up with her army at Jader, and that Gaspard has cut off the western highway.
Dragon Age: The Masked Empire
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: The Masked Empire
.
After the purge of
Halamshiral
, Celene's troops are ambushed by Gaspard's forces. Celene and Ser
Michel
manage to escape. They plan to retreat and rally their forces at Jader, a city that belongs to Lady
Seryl
, a noblewoman loyal to Celene. However, the way is blocked by Gaspard's scouts, so they head for Val Royeaux instead.
Meanwhile, Lady Seryl sends her men to destroy villages where troops loyal to Gaspard may have billeted themselves to discourage the peasants from aiding the rebels.
Dragon Age: Inquisition
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
The ruler of Ferelden reaches out to the Inquistor to request aid in uprooting
Venatori
cultists in the Royal Palace at Denerim. With the aid of the Inquisition's agents, four Tevinter spies and a possible plot to assassinate the monarch are discovered. Once the
Orlesian Civil War
is resolved with the help of the Inquisition, the ruler of Ferelden writes for a second time to ask that they help facilitate peace talks in Jader between Ferelden and Orlais. The Inquisition sends
Josephine Montilyet
to moderate peace talks, and the conflict is avoided.
A resident of Jader is suspected of being an abomination. When the Inquisition's agents investigate the matter, it's discovered that the man in question, Noam Willit, is actually a mage whose powers recently manifested.
If the Inquisitor is of dwarven origin, the Inquisition may contact Comte Gerard of Jader to inform him about lyrium smuggling in the
Storm Coast
. This leads to discovery and elimination of a Venatori group that was hiding from Comte's coastal guard.
The Inquisition lays siege of the
Adamant Fortress
in the
Western Approach
using siege equipment and personnel provided by Lady Seryl of Jader.

Trivia
"Kadan" in the Qunari language is a term for someone who is valued highly. The Warden is called this for retrieving
Asala
, which translates to "my heart."
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
The Kadan-Fe use shields which bear the emblem of
Gwaren
,
Loghain
's teyrnir. It is not clear how they acquired these shields, though given how the other quests relate to people helping Howe and Loghain, it's likely they were hired on by Loghain and outfitted accordingly. Ignacio heavily implies they were at least on Loghain's side, since he states that every contract the Warden performed was ultimately a gain for the Warden.

======================================================================
PAGE: Kal Repartha
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Inquisition locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Kal_Repartha
======================================================================

Location
Kal Repartha
Type
Lost Thaig
Terrain
Dwarven Architecture
Desert
Dunes
Location
Hissing Wastes
Inhabitants
Dwarves
(formerly)
Dragons
Wyverns
Appearances
Dragon Age: Inquisition
Kal Repartha
is a lost
dwarven
thaig
that lies on the surface of the
Hissing Wastes
. In
dwarven language
it means "a place where we may meet in peace".
History
At least a hundred years before the
First Blight
several dwarven
houses
left their thaigs to escape war
, emerged from the
Deep Roads
and sealed the exit.
They were lead by
Paragon
Fairel
, master smith and the most talented
Shaper of Runes
in dwarven history. They settled on the desert of the Hissing Wastes, where they built the only known dwarven colony on the surface; this surface colony remained unknown to the rest of their kind, who, according to the
Memories
, believe that they had died in the Deep Roads.
With his sons' help, Fairel ensured the thaig prospered and grew. Before his death he made each son swear he would take care of his brother. For a time, Fairel's sons worked together, each of them ruling half the thaig. Eventually, they argued and one of them killed the other in a duel. The remaining son realized that his actions were foolish and built elaborate tombs to honor and mourn his father and brother.
The tombs were sealed with the use of
veilfire
and
gangue demons
were bound into the rocks to guard them.
By 9:41
Dragon
, no dwarves live in Kal Repartha anymore and its long-abandoned ruins now dot the desert's landscape.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
The
Elder One
sends a
Venatori
expedition to excavate the ruins in hope of replicating Fairel's masterpieces.
The Inquisitor
however can disrupt their plans and gain access to
the Tomb of Fairel
themselves.
References

======================================================================
PAGE: Kal'Hirol
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Kal'Hirol
======================================================================

Location
Kal'Hirol
Type
Great Thaig
Underground Fortress
Terrain
Dwarven Architecture
Underground
Location
Beneath
Amaranthine
Inhabitants
Dwarves
Darkspawn
(formerly)
Exits
Knotwood Hills
Main Hall
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening
Kal'Hirol
is a
great thaig
of the
dwarves
, long known as a center of learning for the
smith caste
. It is named after
Paragon
Hirol
. It is situated beneath the
Arling of Amaranthine
in
Ferelden
.
History
Its workshops are where Paragon Hirol conceived his famous improvements to
golem
resilience and power, and where Hirol's favorite student developed a method for storing refined
lyrium
that is still used centuries after its conception. These breakthroughs brought Kal'Hirol great prosperity; its passageways are said to have glittered with gold and silver.
This didn't last long however, as it fell to the
darkspawn
during the
First Blight
.
According to
Voldrik Glavonak
the dwarves of Kal'Hirol had almost finished the construction of dwarven barrier doors which would have kept them safe from darkspawn for years to come. However they didn't manage to finish the door mechanism in time.
As the dwarves of Kal'Hirol were evacuating the thaig, five hundred
casteless
were left behind.
Dailan
, feeling that the guard would fail to delay the horde enough, proposed to the casteless that they join with him in the suicidal battle, and suggested they prove that they were not worthless as dwarven society claimed. Despite opposition amongst some of the casteless, approximately two hundred volunteered for the duty. It is not known what happened to the others though at least a few attempted to find a route to the
surface
.
With the fall of Kal'Hirol, much of the dwarven knowledge of smithing was lost to the darkspawn horde.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening
.
The Warden-Commander
successfully ventures into the great thaig in order to eliminate the broodmothers which reside in its lower levels, as well as learn more about the
darkspawn civil war
. There it is discovered that two hundred
casteless
dwarves were the last defence of the fortress in order to give enough time for the rest of the population of Kal'Hirol to escape to Orzammar in safety.
In the
epilogue
, it is mentioned that Kal'Hirol is cleared of
darkspawn
, and eventually the dwarves of Orzammar attempt to reclaim it.
House Helmi
ultimately leads a host of dwarves to clear the
Deep Roads
between Orzammar and Kal'Hirol and, though the losses were great, they are ultimately successful in 9:31
Dragon
.

Notable items
Inferno Golem Shell
- source: looted from the
Inferno golem
.
Staff of the Lost
- source: looted from
The Lost
.
Battlemage's Cinch
- source: looted from
The Lost
.
Bugs
There is a chance that after the cut-scene the party will not automatically be in the circular chamber with The Lost and Inferno golem, i.e. be stuck outside, still in the long corridor. You cannot move into the room due to an invisible barrier at the entrance, because the event script has closed it off. The console runscript debug mode has no option to "teleport" the party into it. Solution is to initiate combat by targeting with a ranged weapon or spell. Stay in the corridor away from the golem's flame attacks and continue with spells and ranged weapons to kill The Lost first,. Then, attack the Inferno golem. If the golem comes near the doorway, use melee weapons. Once both are defeated, the event script will reopen the chamber, and you can then enter and loot the corpses.

The pit with the three
broodmothers

Location
