======================================================================
PAGE: Party Camp
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Party_Camp
======================================================================

Location
Party Camp
Type
Camp
Terrain
Clearing
Location
Ferelden
Inhabitants
Companions
and other
characters
Exits
World map
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
The
Party Camp
is a place where
the Warden
rests and coordinates future adventures. The camp becomes available after the Warden leaves
Flemeth's Hut
following the
Battle of Ostagar
. To travel to the camp, open the
world map
and click on the camp in the top right corner.
Features
The Party Camp is a night environment and appears to be situated amongst various forested hills, with one unobstructed entrance.
Alistair
,
Leliana
and
Zevran
can be found standing around the large fire in the south end of the camp.
Wynne
and
Oghren
stand beside the nearby tents.
Bodahn Feddic
and
Sandal
stand next to their caravan further north, and
Shale
can be found standing near them.
Sten
stands a little bit north of Alistair near the center of the camp, and
Morrigan
stands next to her own fire and shelter at the far northeast end of the camp.
Dog
, meanwhile, guards the entrance to the camp.
As the game progresses additional people may join the camp, including representatives of the Warden's allies who will stand east of the tents next to the
Allied Supply Crates
.
Levi Dryden
will temporarily locate himself near the entrance to the camp, but will leave after the Warden completes the
Warden's Keep
DLC
.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Archdemon in a vision
After completing four of the five
main questlines
, the Warden and Alistair will have a dream of the
Archdemon
. Subsequently, a group of
shrieks
will attack your camp. Though you have no party to command, all your
companions
will join the fray to fend off the intruders. If the Warden is a
Dalish elf
, they will discover the fate of
Tamlen
during this event.
Note:
To trigger this event, the Warden will need to travel from place to place, not to or from the camp. This event will not trigger if the Warden only heads to camp prior to venturing to other locations. If party members were holding position upon entering the camp, they will continue to hold position in battle; their positioning cannot be change during the ambush. Party members that get covered in blood during the battle will also appear covered in blood when gathered.
Note:
This incident takes place in a version of the camp that does not include Bodahn Feddic, Sandal, or any of the ally emissaries. The Allied Supply Crates will appear close to the camp entrance, but cannot be interacted with. The camp will return to its normal configuration after the Warden ventures out from the camp and then returns.
Characters
Bodahn Feddic
– a dwarven merchant peddling unique wares.
Sandal
– Bodahn's adopted son and a gifted rune enchanter who provides
Enchantment
services.
Any party member recruited by
the Warden
:
Alistair
– former templar initiate recruited into the Grey Wardens.
Dog
– a loyal Mabari War Hound.
Leliana
– a bard and cloistered sister from Orlais.
Morrigan
– a shapeshifting apostate mage known as a Witch of the Wilds.
Oghren
– a disgraced warrior caste dwarf.
Shale
– a war golem created.
Sten
– a Qunari warrior of the Beresaad.
Wynne
– a senior enchanter from Kinloch Hold.
Zevran Arainai
– an elven assassin and member of the Antivan Crows.
Levi Dryden
– a human who provides the starting point for the
Warden's Keep
questline.
Up to four emissaries, from the various armies the Warden recruited:
Emissary
Pether
– the mage and templar representative.
Emissary
Bulfa
– the werewolf representative (or)
Emissary
Caron
– the Dalish representative.
Emissary
Fellhammer
– the dwarven representative.
Lieutenant
Cadrim
– Redcliffe's representative.
Schmooples
– appears after the
Cute Nug
is given as a
gift
to Leliana.
Enemies
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Darkspawn
Shrieks
(
Critter
)
Shrieks
(
Normal
)
Shriek Alpha
(
Lieutenant
)
Tormented Shriek
(Lieutenant) - only encountered by a Dalish Elf Warden.
Effects
Entering (or being in) camp has several effects:
Injuries
are automatically treated.
Companions will discuss personal, even intimate, matters. Many discussions are only available at the camp. If the Warden is in a romance with any party member, a romantic encounter may be initiated - or a romance broken off - in camp. Interacting with Dog occasionally triggers humorous dialogue sequences involving party members interacting with (or complaining to Warden about) Dog.
Your active party is removed; attempting to leave the camp will prompt you to form a party.
The equipment and status of all currently available party members is accessible from the inventory screen, in the same manner that the equipment and status of your active party is available in normal gameplay, except you're no longer restricted to active members.
You cannot have any of your companions use an ability, or craft or use items.
In addition, characters who remain in the camp whilst the main party are off adventuring still gain experience, remaining one level behind the Warden. Note this is not a gradual trend; the passive party does not gain experience over time. Instead, when the Warden levels up, anyone at the camp who is two or more levels below is instantly raised to one level below. Returning to camp after the Warden has leveled up will also level up members in the active party who were two or more levels below. If the levelling up does not occur, it can be 'forced' by bringing up the party selection screen, either when leaving the camp, or at any location that allows active party members to be changed. (Actually leaving the camp after bringing up the screen, or changing party members, is not necessary.) If an ally is added to a party after having been left in camp for a long time, the player may find there are many unused character points to be allocated.
It should also be noted that there is no travel time between the camp and the place you last visited on the world map. For example, if you were in
Denerim
, then moved to the camp, you can return to Denerim without traveling on the world map, thus avoiding triggering an
encounter
.
Notable items
Companion Gifts
Golden Rope Necklace
- sold by
Bodahn
.
Special objects
Allied Supply Crates
- allows the Warden to invest resources in the equipment of the allied army that will eventually battle the
Blight
.
Notes
When exiting from camp, all sustained effects on party members other than the Warden will be removed.
Trivia
Originally, there was going to be different camps in specific places around the map. For a while there was a complicated system where the party members would do things in camp that would give the player items and help out in such ways — like a party member who made potions, ones that could be interacted with and asked to craft, a whole crafting system relating to that.
Bugs
Bodahn's inventory resets after you exit camp following your first meeting with him. This has two major consequences:
You can buy two of any of his initial items: once during the first meeting and again after the reset. This can get you an early extra backpack as well as some extra tomes.
Any items sold to him during the first meeting will be lost.
Attempting to set
tactics
presets for your companions in camp will result in blank entries. You will need to add them to your party and go elsewhere before you can change presets.
pc
Reinstalling the game, while keeping your saves, may cause Levi Dryden to respawn. This will have the effect of showing two Levi Drydens in camp.
If you throw the DLC item
Stick
out of bounds, it will be unrecoverable. However, a replacement is obtainable from
Bodahn Feddic
, after leaving and re-entering the camp.
See also
Arl of Redcliffe's Estate
Gallery
Map of the Area
Bodahn Feddic
Sandal
References

======================================================================
PAGE: Perendale
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Perendale
======================================================================

Location
Perendale
Type
City
Terrain
Urban
Location
Western Nevarra
Inhabitants
Humans
Elves
Dwarves
Appearances
Dragon Age II
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: Inquisition
(mentioned)
Perendale
is a city in western
Nevarra
. It was taken from
Orlais
during the
Blessed Age
.
Background
Perendale is located in a mountainous region, on the southern edge of the Hunterhorn Mountains. The area surrounding the city is rich in silver mines,
wyverns
, and mountain goats. In the past, the mountains near the city were also full of
dragons
.
As it used to be an
Orlesian
city until the
Blessed Age
, Perendale still looks like a provincial Orlesian town. A carving of the Lions Slaying the Dragon adorns the city's gate, and many lions can be found decorating the city's buildings.
History
In 7:82
Storm
, Emperor
Etienne Valmont I
set his childless wife of seventeen years to marry Princess
Sotiria Pentaghast
, theoretically ushering peace and cooperation between the historically feuding nations of
Orlais
and
Nevarra
. However, in 7:97 Storm, as the Emperor remained without child, he sent Empress Sotiria to a cloister and married his
mistress
. The Pentaghasts were gravely insulted, but took no immediate action beyond sending a war party to retrieve their princess and chose to bide their time instead.
War with Orlais
In 8:46
Blessed
, when the bulk of the Orlesian forces was committed to a war in
Ferelden
, the Pentaghasts chose to retaliate, starting a war of their own against Orlais. The Nevarrans captured the cities of Arlesans, Ghislain, and Perendale. While the Orlesians eventually recaptured the former two, Perendale was permanently ceded to Nevarra.
In 8:70, another war broke out between Nevarra and Orlais over control of the western hills around Perendale. This conflict also resulted in  Nevarran victory. However, Orlais had sown dissent and rebellion among the locals, so that many of Perendale's inhabitants still resent Nevarran rule and hope to return to the Empire.
Victory in Perendale inspired the king of Nevarra to attempt to conquer the
Free Marches
. Nevarran expansion was eventually stopped by Cade Arvale, the
Champion
of Tantervale.
Involvement
Dragon Age: Inquisition
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
If
Champions of the Just
was completed:
The frightened people of Perendale ask the
templars
serving under the
Inquisition
to remove a group of
mages
who have locked themselves in their former
Circle of Magi
, fearing what they might do to the city. If
the Inquisitor
chooses to fulfill their request, they learn that the mages barricaded themselves inside the tower out of fear of what the populace might do to them if they left its confines. The mages are safely escorted out of the city and brought to the Inquisition.
Trespasser
This section contains spoilers for
:
Trespasser
.
If the
Bull's Chargers
were saved:
The Bull's Chargers are credited for preventing the start of another civil war in
Orlais
due to their actions in Perendale. The
Iron Bull
is particularly noted for having defeated the would-be usurper in personal combat.
Places
Circle of Magi
Chantry
Quests
The Order's Obligations
(war table) (conditional)
Codex entries

Mantle of the Champion
The Perendale War
Trivia
Blackwall
campaigned in Perendale in his younger years.

Merrill (short story)
Pride's End
is a small cave located in northeastern-most area of
Sundermount
. The cavern can be reached through the
Sundermount Passage
, past the Sundermount Graveyard. The area can only be accessed in Act 3 during the companion quest
A New Path
.
Background
Merrill
states that the cave was once the site of a battle between the
Tevinter Imperium
and
Elvhenan
, where the
elves
fought upon the bones of their ancestors in a desperate last stand.
Merrill had been to the location twice prior, once with
Keeper
Marethari
and once alone. Her second ascent was solely to acquire knowledge from an imprisoned
demon
she and her Keeper had encountered prior. While there, the demon informs her how to cleanse the
darkspawn
taint
from her shard, that she had acquired from the events that had unfolded during
Dragon Age: Origins
, by the use of
blood magic
.
Involvement
Activating the "Strange Idol" will result in a confrontation with Keeper Marethari and a
pride demon
named
Audacity
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Primeval Thaig
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age II locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Primeval_Thaig
======================================================================

Location
Primeval Thaig
Type
Lost Thaig
Terrain
Dwarven Architecture
Underground
Location
Underground
(presumably deep beneath the
Free Marches
)
Inhabitants
Dwarves
(formerly)
Profane
Demons
Golems
Quests
The Deep Roads Expedition
Caption
Bartrand's Folly
Appearances
Dragon Age II
Dragon Age: Inquisition
(mentioned)
The
Valdasine Thaig
is an ancient
thaig
and the home of
House
Valdasine. In present day it is known as the
Primeval Thaig
or colloquially as
Bartrand's Folly
.
Background
In ancient times, prior to the
darkspawn
, House Valdasine was single-handedly able to supply the dwarven empire with lyrium. Tale has it that the thaig's doors were shut unannounced not allowing for anyone to exit or enter—not even to visiting
Paragons
. After days of silence the doors were finally breached, however no one remained, nor was there any evidence of what had transpired. Those that entered uncovered a
red lyrium
idol
, that was purportedly worshiped, and a strange metal
staff
gleaming as if it were pure lyrium. When the dwarven
king
received word he ordered the idol and the staff to remain untouched inside and to have the thaig sealed. Such was the danger the thaig posed, that the existence of the thaig was stricken from the very
Memories
so that it would be completely forgotten in time.
The thaig's architecture is completely distinct from others and appears to be untainted by the darkspawn's corruption. There are vast statues and temples—several of which seemed to have been created entirely by magic. As this is extremely out of character for the dwarves of old, the thaig is completely shrouded in mystery—questions pertaining to why these dwarves worship gods and how they were able to sculpt using
magic
—may never be fully answered.
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age II
.
When
Hawke
and the
expedition
descend into the
Deep Roads
using ancient Grey Warden maps, they descend so far that they enter the Primeval Thaig, located below the official level of the Roads. The thaig is overgrown by
red lyrium
, and a lyrium idol is found in it. The thaig however is not uninhabited:
rock wraiths
now roam the halls, as well as
demons
.
If Hawke
meets
Nathaniel Howe
in Act 3, he tells that the
Grey Wardens
took great interest in the thaig and the
First Warden
commissioned a special expedition to learn more.
Characters
Primeval Thaig Entrance Map

– encountered before entering the Thaig proper.
Sandal
– encountered before entering the Thaig proper.
Bartrand Tethras
–
Varric
's older brother and head of the
Tethras family
.
Stores
Bodahn's Goods
– available before entering the Thaig proper.
Quests
The Deep Roads Expedition
Enemies
Primeval Thaig Interior Map

(2)

- at the top of the stairs in the first room after reaching the thaig.
Codex entries
The Primeval Thaig
Valdasine
Trivia
Although Bartrand suggests that the thaig appears to be very ancient, well before even the
Blights
, it contain
golems
, which were initially created to fight darkspawn at the height of the First Blight.
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
Bianca Davri
learns from Varric the location of the thaig, who inadvertently informs Warden
Larius
(or) Warden
Janeka
about it. However, that Warden in reality was
Corypheus
who utilized the red lyrium for his own purposes.

======================================================================
PAGE: Prison of Regrets
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Prison_of_Regrets
======================================================================

Location
Prison of Regrets
Type
Maze
Terrain
Floating platforms with hand decorations, architecture of ancient ruins, roots, statues of regrets
Location
The
Fade
Quests
A Cage for Gods
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
The
Prison of Regrets
is a prison within the
Fade
built by
Solas
to contain
Elgar'nan
and
Ghilan'nain
.
Background
“Regret like all emotions is a powerful thing. It can bruise us, break us... or blind us to the truth. Regret is even strong enough to serve as the lock on a prison built to hold gods. But such a prison can hold any captive... even
you
.”
―Solas describing the Prison of Regrets in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
The
Evanuris
weaponized the
Blight
in their wars of supremacy, especially during Solas' rebellion against them. Solas told
Mythal
of the other elven gods' abuse of the Blight and she sought to convince them to cease its use on behalf of Solas. The other elven gods murdered Mythal with
Solas' lyrium dagger
when she confronted them. Solas recovered the Lyrium Dagger from Elgar'nan and from it extracted a fragment of Mythal that had lain hidden within its depths.
For unleashing the Blight onto the world and murdering Mythal, Solas imprisoned the Evanuris in the
Black City
and used
blood magic
to tie their lives to the
Veil
.
However, Solas overlooked the Evanuris'
archdemons
. Through them, the elven gods were able to manipulate the
Magisters Sidereal
into breaching the
Black City
and releasing a portion of the Blight into
Thedas
.
By 9:51
Dragon
, the Black City's hold on the remaining Evanuris -
Elgar'nan
and
Ghilan'nain
- had begun to weaken. Solas, fearing what would happen if the Evanuris were to escape, created the Prison of Regrets and prepared to transfer the two gods to it.
The prison has no bars but is a floating platform whose space is saturated with achromatic color. Falling from the floating platform or trying to walk the grounds just causes the prisoner to be trapped in a inescapable loop of traversing the same landscape. The Fade manifests regrets, usually as stone statues that may speak to the prisoner, weigh them down, or otherwise move to trap them. Solas built the prison in such a way that not even an
elven god
could escape.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
In 9:51 Dragon, Solas begins a ritual to transfer Elgar'nan and Ghilan'nain to the Prison of Regrets, then tear down the Veil. The ritual is interrupted by
Varric Tethras
, who attempts to talk Solas out of his plan. While the two argue, Varric's second-in-command Rook leads the
Veilguard
to tear down the scaffolding at the ritual site. One of the statues at the site almost falls on Solas, distracting him long enough for Varric to grab his
ritual knife
. In the ensuing struggle, Solas stabs Varric to death and is unable to regain control of the ritual. Elgar'nan and Ghilan'nain escape, and Solas himself is pulled into the prison.
The ritual ends with an explosion that severely concusses Rook and causes them to bleed on the ritual site. Solas uses this to create a blood magic connection with their mind - his only connection to the outside world. The two form an uneasy alliance, and unbeknownst to Rook, Solas causes them to forget Varric died and to hallucinate Varric in the
Lighthouse
infirmary.
They begin to speak in the prison in Rook's dreams, separated by a chasm.
During the
Sixth Blight
, Rook makes a number of pivotal decisions; first choosing whether to save
Minrathous
or
Treviso
from Ghilan'nain, then choosing which team members should take which role in the
assault
on
Tearstone Island
. These decisions led to devastation and a large number of losses, including the kidnap of one of Rook's
mage
companions (
Neve Gallus
or
Bellara Lutare
) quickly followed by the death of either
Davrin
and
Assan
or
Lace Harding
. Rook expresses their sense of mounting frustration and guilt to 'Varric' and Solas, but continues to press forward.
Following
Lucanis Dellamorte
's assassination of Ghilan'nain, Rook retrieves Solas' lyrium dagger from Ghilan'nain's corpse to prevent it from potentially destroying the Veil in the area. The Veil is intact but weakened, allowing Solas to pull Rook into the prison. He tells them that ever since their meeting, he has been molding them into someone the prison would accept in his place, as he belives mortals cannot defeat the gods. Rook tries to use the lyrium dagger to keep from falling, but their guilt over their missing and dead companions (and the realisation that Varric is among the dead) causes them to lose their grip and fall. Solas reclaims the dagger and uses it to cut his way out of the prison.
Rook
lands in the Prison of Regrets
, on the side of the chasm from which Solas spoke to them in their dreams; there is even a statue of them on the other side. As they explore the prison, they find a number of Fen'Harel statues and begin hearing Varric's voice, encouraging them to keep going and find a way out. On the way, they encounter statues of their lost companions. The companion who was taken by Elgar'nan accuses Rook of choosing them to die, to which Rook says that they are willing to live with their failures. The companion who was killed tells Rook that Rook inspired them to make their final sacrifice, and Rook promises to make it worth it.
After coming to terms with the loss of their teammates, Rook reaches the prison's recreation of the ritual site, where they have a flashback to the ritual and realise that Varric has been dead all along. Rook expresses guilt for his death, but Varric tells them that trying to talk to Solas was his choice, not theirs, and that they don't need him; they already have everything they need. He also tells them that Solas wants to be a hero, and he's unable to move past his own regrets.
After thanking Varric and bidding him farewell, Rook hears the voices of their companions coming out of a
Fade tear
. Lucanis,
Emmrich Volkarin
and
Taash
pull Rook through the tear and into the Lighthouse.
Characters
Solas
Rook
Quests
A Cage for Gods
Codex entries
On a Cage of  Regret
References

======================================================================
PAGE: Qunandar
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Qunandar
======================================================================

Location
Qunandar
Type
Capital City
Terrain
Urban
Location
Par Vollen
Inhabitants
Qunari
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
(mentioned)
Dragon Age II
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: Inquisition
(mentioned)
Qunandar
is the capital of
Par Vollen
and the
Qunari
's main base of operations and command. It is located on the southern coast of the island, on the
Boeric Ocean
.
Background
Qunandar was likely constructed when the island was invaded in the
Steel Age
. Not much is known about this city as very few outsiders have been permitted to visit. Outsiders who have had success entering the city have described it as one of the wonders of the world, and one of the only places where it is possible to understand Qunari society in its entirety.
The Qunari have built massive domes and aqueducts that rival Par Vollen's ancient pyramids, clearly showing the advancement of their architecture.
Qunari buildings have also been compared to beehives in their structure.
Qunandar is one of Thedas' greatest cities, easily on par with
Cumberland
,
Val Royeaux
and
Minrathous
.
Codex entries
Par Vollen: The Occupied North
The Pyramids of Par Vollen
References

======================================================================
PAGE: Qunari Compound
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age II locations, Kirkwall locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Qunari_Compound
======================================================================

Location
Qunari Compound
Type
Harbor
Terrain
Dwelling
Location
Docks
,
Kirkwall
Inhabitants
Qunari
Appearances
Dragon Age II
Mark of the Assassin
(mentioned)
The Qunari Compound
is a walled off section in the center of the
Kirkwall
Docks
. It is here that the
Qunari
expedition and their leader
the Arishok
reside. For the safety of the public, and the Qunari, it remains sealed off.
Background
Following a storm which left the Qunari shipwrecked, they were forced to take up lodgings here until a new ship could be sent to return them to
Par Vollen
. According to the
Viscount
, it was a gift to the Qunari to "contain them".
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age II
.
The compound will first be visited during the quest
Blackpowder Promise
where
Hawke
meets the Arishok for the first time. It can also optionally be returned to after the conclusion of
Shepherding Wolves
where it is possible to disclose to the Qunari the fate of
Arvaarad
and
Ketojan
. Hawke will be recalled here at the inception of Act 2, at the request of both the Viscount and the Arishok for
Blackpowder Courtesy
to investigate the merchant
Javaris Tintop
. It is also possible to return to after completing
Offered and Lost
to inform the Arishok of the fate of the Qunari delegation. The compound is also briefly visited during
Following the Qun
where Hawke and the Arishok will discuss the role of
Saemus Dumar
. Hawke will return to the compound one final time during
Demands of the Qun
at the request of  of
Aveline
where the Arishok will attempt to apprehend Hawke to prevent their interference in the Qunari invasion of Kirkwall.
By 9:37 Dragon, with the Qunari gone from Kirkwall, the compound has been sealed off and a large
Chantry
banner hangs overhead. A large statue depicting an armored figure standing atop the severed head of the Arishok has been erected not far from the compound gates.
Characters
The Arishok
Quests
Act 1
Blackpowder Promise
Shepherding Wolves
Act 2
Blackpowder Courtesy
Offered and Lost
Following the Qun
Demands of the Qun
Trivia
According to
Dragon Age II: The Complete Official Guide
, the remains of the Qunari dreadnought destroyed in the storm were recycled into the compound's structure. This explains why they hadn't simply repaired their ship, as the Qunari expected to stay in Kirkwall for an indefinite time.

PAGE: Rainesfere
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Rainesfere
======================================================================

Location
Rainesfere
Type
Bannorn
Location
Arling of Redcliffe
,
Ferelden
Quests
Reparations for Redcliffe
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
(mentioned)
The Darkspawn Chronicles
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: Inquisition
(mentioned)
Rainesfere
is a bannorn in the Arling of
Redcliffe
, a tiny province squeezed between the
Frostback Mountains
and
Lake Calenhad
.
Background
Ser Thorval of Rainesfere was the sixth son of a sixth son, a child of ill-fortune. It showed on his 13th birthday, when he narrowly avoided being run over by a cart, only to have a tree fall on him. It also showed on his wedding day, when his bride ran off with a roving
dwarven
tinker. But nowhere was Thorval's misfortune more obvious than on the battlefield as every blade and shield he took into battle shattered. After another unfortunate accident Thorval finally won himself honor and acclaim by taking up a hammer in a tournament winning his matches easily. From that day forth, he used no other weapon.
Rainesfere was occupied during the
Second Orlesian Occupation
.
Involvement
Dragon Age: Origins
Younger brother to Arl
Eamon Guerrin
of Redcliffe, and uncle to
King Cailan
,
Teagan Guerrin
holds the bannorn of Rainesfere. Bann Teagan avoids the Denerim court except to go hunting with his nephew, and rarely makes himself heard at the Landsmeet, preferring to leave politics to his brother.
Dragon Age: Inquisition
If
the Inquisitor
elects to side with the
mages
during the main quest
In Hushed Whispers
, Teagan Guerrin will ask for reparations for one of his freeholds in Rainesfere that was burned to the ground when a mage inside lost control of his abilities.
Quests
Reparations for Redcliffe
(war table)
Codex entries
A Rolled-Up Note
Bann Teagan Guerrin
Thorval's Luck
Trivia
A
Rolled-Up Note
can be found in the quest codex entries and seems to send the Warden to the bannorn, but it was never meant to be a quest as confirmed by Bioware.

PAGE: Redcliffe Castle
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Inquisition locations, Dragon Age: Origins locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Redcliffe_Castle
======================================================================

Location
Redcliffe Castle
Type
Fortress
Location
Hinterlands
,
Ferelden
Sections
Origins
:
Main Floor
Upper Floor
Redcliffe Dungeons
Inquisition
:
Upper Royal Wing
Lower Royal Wing
Courtyard

Torture Chambers
Guard Barracks
Upper Cells
Lower Cells
Inhabitants
Humans
Exits
Origins
:
World map
Redcliffe Dungeons
Inquisition
:
Hinterlands
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Dragon Age: Inquisition
Redcliffe Castle
is the seat of the
Guerrin family
. In the valley below is
Redcliffe Village
. The fortress provides soldiers to the entire Redcliffe region. Redcliffe's coat of arms is a tower atop a red cliff on a white background.
Background
Castle Redcliffe has stood for far longer than the village it protects. Since the days of the
Alamarri
clans, the castle has guarded the main pass through the
Frostback Mountains
into
Orlais
. Redcliffe Castle is the first and last line of defense for the sole land route into Ferelden, and the country has never fallen to any force that did not first take Redcliffe.
It was here that the
Avvar
hill folk came to strike at the lowlands to the east, and it was this castle that the Orlesian Empire needed to control before conquering
Ferelden
. That was no easy task, for while the folk of Redcliffe may be simple, they are also proud of their ancient role as Ferelden's protectors from incursion. "There is iron in the hills, as there is in the people" goes the local saying, and no one disbelieves it.
The fortress has endured many sieges. On only three such occasions was it successfully taken—first by
Calenhad Theirin
,
much later by Orlesian nobility, and most recently when restored to the Guerrins in the
Fereldan Rebellion
. The castle is popularly described as "unassailable" in spite of these three attacks.
According to
Cullen
, Redcliffe Castle is one of Ferelden's most defensible fortresses, having repelled thousands of assaults.
Involvement
Dragon Age: Origins
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Upon
the Warden
's arrival at Redcliffe, the castle has been taken over by an
undead
horde which is attacking Redcliffe Village. No matter if they decide to help the village or not, he or she will have to fight through the undead guarding the castle where they will, in the end, find
Connor Guerrin
possessed
by a
desire demon
. After ending the threat of the
abomination
, the Warden revived a comatose Arl
Eamon Guerrin
with a
Pinch of Sacred Ash
.
Later Redcliffe Castle serves as a staging area for troops fighting against the
Blight
.
Dragon Age: Inquisition
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
The Monarchy offered the
Rebel Mages
sanctuary in
Ferelden
, in the
Arling
of
Redcliffe
. The mages gratefully accepted the offer however the
Templar
host pursued them across the border.
Chaos erupted after the explosion that caused the
Breach
destroyed the
Chantry
's peace
summit
. Eventually
Venatori
infiltrators persuaded the desperate mages under
Fiona
that their only hope of survival was to escape to the
Tevinter Imperium
. One of the Venatori,
Magister
Gereon Alexius
, traveled in time to the rebel mages' most desperate moment and offered the mages citizenship in exchange for ten years indentured servitude. Feeling they had no choice, the mages accepted. Alexius banished Arl
Teagan Guerrin
and seized Redcliffe Castle as his stronghold.
Redcliffe Castle after a year of Alexius' rule
If the Inquisitor recruits the Mages:
Magister Alexius invites the
Herald of Andraste
to Redcliffe Castle to negotiate for the southern mages' aid. Secretly he intends to capture the Herald and present them as a trophy to the Venatori's mysterious deity,
the Elder One
.
Inquisition
soldiers infiltrate the castle during the negotiation however and kill the Venatori before they can proceed with their plan. In desperation Alexius attempts to use his unique time-distorting magic and kill the Herald, however
Dorian Pavus
intervenes and the Herald and Dorian are flung forward through time a year into the future.
They discover that Alexius has held on to Redcliffe Castle, turning it into a major Venatori stronghold in southern
Thedas
and both the Inquisition and
Ferelden
have failed in their attempts to oust the Venatori from the fortress. Magister Alexius has greatly expanded the Castle's underground chambers and outer fortifications and the castle is now overgrown with
Red lyrium
deposits. Alexius has also allowed rifts into
the Fade
to open throughout the Castle, leaving whole sections of the fortress overrun with
Demons
. Alexius himself has installed a unique and seemingly impenetrable barrier door which blocks access to the throne room, a door that can only be opened with all five of the red lyrium rings held by his most trusted Venatori mages.
The Herald and Dorian, together with their allies, kill Alexius and return to their original time and Magister Alexius is swiftly captured thus preventing Alexius' version of Redcliffe Castle from ever coming to be.

PAGE: Redcliffe Chantry

For the Chantry in
Dragon Age: Origins

Village Chantry
.
Location
Redcliffe Village Chantry
Type
Chantry
Location
Redcliffe Village
,
Hinterlands
Inhabitants
Humans
Exits
Redcliffe Village
Appearances
Dragon Age: Inquisition
The
Redcliffe Village Chantry
is a
Chantry
located in the eastern district of
Redcliffe Village
. As Redcliffe Village expanded in the wake of the
Fifth Blight
, a second chantry was erected on the other side of the village to accommodate parishioners.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
The Inquisitor
visits to the Chantry during the
main quest
In Hushed Whispers
after receiving a note from
Felix
,
Alexius

Redcliffe Farms
is an area composed of several similar farms that are situated in the
Hinterlands
in relative proximity to
Redcliffe Village
.
Background
In 9:41
Dragon
the farms have been plagued with several issues, most pertaining to the
Mage-Templar War
. There are
demon
-controlled
black wolves
running wild, and bandits have taken to attacking travelers.
I retired to the Hinterlands for peace and quiet away from the politics, and because the wide open spaces were perfect to let my horses run. Instead, the war between the
mages
and the
templars
has turned your beautiful hills into a series of burning battlefields. The farmers who live in the Hinterlands are good folk. Many of them left
Redcliffe village
because they couldn't bear to be there anymore, not after the
Blight
and the walking dead left so many bad memories. Now we've got apostates running around setting fire to anyone who looks at them sideways, and templars looting houses and cutting down those who protest as mage sympathizers.
—
Dennet
, former horsemaster to
Arl Teagan
of Redcliffe

======================================================================
PAGE: Redcliffe Village
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Inquisition locations, Dragon Age: Origins locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Redcliffe_Village
======================================================================

Location
Redcliffe Village
Type
Village
Location
Hinterlands
,
Ferelden
Population
200
(9:30
Dragon
)
Leaders
Mayor Murdock
Inhabitants
Humans
Elves
Dwarves
Exits
Origins
:
Redcliffe Castle
World Map
Inquisition
:
Hinterlands
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
The Stone Prisoner
(mentioned)
The Darkspawn Chronicles
(mentioned)
Dragon Age II
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: Inquisition
Jaws of Hakkon
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
(mentioned)
Sitting on the western shore of
Lake Calenhad
, the village of
Redcliffe
is so named for the reddish hues of the cliffs that tower above it. A path leads up into the hills and across a bridge to an island where the ancient
Castle Redcliffe
has stood far longer than the village itself.
Redcliffe heraldry (Argent, a tower cendrée issuant from a cliff gules.)
Background
“The fate of Redcliffe is the fate of all
Ferelden
.”
―
Calenhad Theirin
Redcliffe is located in the western hills of Ferelden. The village below the castle (and
others
under Redcliffe's authority) grew until the
arling
was as powerful a settlement as any
teyrnir
. Redcliffe Castle is the first and last line of defense for the sole land route into Ferelden and the country has never fallen to any assault that did not first take Redcliffe.
Redcliffe is one of the largest and most prosperous towns in Ferelden, due to its position between
Orzammar
and
Orlais
. As such it is a center of foreign trade. Such favorable conditions caused the domain to be named an arling in spite of its small size. The citizens of Redcliffe are primarily fishermen or merchants engaged in shipping
dwarven
goods from Orlais.
In late autumn the villagers smoke the fish they have caught, to the dismay of visiting merchants.
Witch Hunt
This section contains spoilers for:
Witch Hunt
.
By 9:32
Dragon
, the
Bannorn
increasingly looked to the Arl of Redcliffe for guidance and leadership. It was speculated that if this continued, Redcliffe would become a rival of
Denerim
.
Dragon Age: Origins
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Redcliffe concept art
As
the Warden
approaches the village the first time, they will be greeted by
Tomas
, a member of the Redcliffe militia. Tomas will then take your party to Bann
Teagan
,
Arl Eamon
's brother, in the
Chantry
. There they discover that the village is under siege from
undead creatures
spawning from
Redcliffe Castle
at night. The Villagers ask the Warden and their companions to join them and lead the defense against the animated dead.
Later the village is attacked by
Darkspawn
.
Redcliffe Village continued to expand and grow, establishing a new district east of Redcliffe Castle in the years following the
Fifth Blight
.

will trigger a conversation about his heritage. Afterward, if you talk to him and his
approval
is high enough, he'll start the quest

.

and
The Attack at Nightfall
will result in the deaths of all the inhabitants except Bann Teagan, who will still take his part in continuing the
Arl of Redcliffe
quest line.

, it's inaccessible by normal means and is a remnant of the original process to acquire

before it was added as
downloadable content
.

pc
which may cause fires to still be present after the quest
A Village Under Siege
is completed.
There is a bug where the game will not load at the after the
defense of the village from the undead
.
Dragon Age: Inquisition
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
The Monarchy offered the
rebel mages
sanctuary in
Ferelden
, in the
Arling
of
Redcliffe
. The mages gratefully accepted the offer however the
Templar
host pursued them across the border.
Map of the new eastern district in Inquisition
Chaos erupted after the explosion that caused
the Breach
destroyed the
Chantry
's peace
Conclave
. As the brutality of the
Mage-Templar War
escalated, many hunted rebel mages and
apostates
congregated to
Fiona
's refuge in Redcliffe village. Some of these foreign mage refugees were in fact
Venatori
infiltrators who encouraged an alliance with the
Tevinter Imperium
as the situation with the mage rebellion grew dire.
Grand Enchanter Fiona conceded to the alliance with
Magister
Gereon Alexius
when Alexius used time warping magic to manipulate the rebel mages when they were most desperate. Alexius was also a
Venatori
agent and usurped
Redcliffe Castle
with the rebel mages' aid, banishing Arl
Teagan Guerrin
. With the mage rebellion's survival now dependent on their alliance with the Tevinter Imperium, Alexius changed the terms of the alliance: from protection and eventual Tevinter citizenship in exchange for indentured servitude to conscription of all the rebel southern mages into military service under his command. He then ordered his forces to displace the people of
Redcliffe
from their homes.
Eventually however both the Venatori and the rebel mages vacate Redcliffe Village and life subsides back into normality.

Involvement
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
The strange, new darkspawn that
Elgar'nan
and
Ghilan'nain
had deployed to southern Thedas have spread fear and corruption greater than any previous Blight in history. Ferelden would have fallen had it not received crucial aid from
Orzammar
. Nevertheless,
Denerim
was lost and the Fereldans were holding the line at Redcliffe.
Soon, the hordes of darkspawn have conquered Redcliffe and the shores of
Lake Calenhad
writhe with the blight's corruption.

======================================================================
PAGE: Rivain Coast
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Rivain_Coast
======================================================================

Location
Rivain Coast
Type
Coast
Terrain
Coastline
Location
Western
Rivain
Enemies
Demons
Antaam
Mercenaries
Reavers
Undead
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
The
Rivain Coast
is a stretch of coast in western
Rivain
on the Rialto Bay.
Background
The Rivain Coast is filled with abandoned ruins and hidden tombs. Some of these ruins were ancient fortresses for the
Grey Wardens
, who teamed up with a
seer
to fight
demons
and
darkspawn
inhabiting a volcano. More recently, the
Antaam
rebels have made camp here as they seek to capture
dragons
. Without the Qunari builders to support them, the rebel Antaam have been seizing the abandoned Grey Warden fortresses for refuge.
Some dragons inhabit this coast to keep their hoarded treasure in their lairs. The shipwrecks of either the Antaam navy or Rivaini Armada litter the beaches.
Spirits
roam the land and some possess the corpses of dead pirates who died seeking the hidden treasures of the coast. Others possess the southern
Chantry
zealots who died during the
Annulment
of
Dairsmuid
's
Circle of Magi
. These
undead
attack any interlopers they come across.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
The Rivain Coast is unlocked by starting the quest
The Dragon Slayer
.
Taash
guides Rook and Rook's companion through the Winding Shoreline to clear out the local high dragon, a
Vinsomer
, from her lair so that the Lords of Fortune can steal her hoarded treasure. However,
Antaam
have recently been spotted on the Coast, making this task more difficult. Taash intends to lure out the dragon via drugged food laid out as a lure, and Rook offers to help. Taash leads the Veilguard to a nearby beach, explaining on the way that they only kill dragons that need killing and the Vinsomer is generally not a threat to humans unless provoked.
Rook assists in killing a number of
deepstalkers
and their
matriarch
, and Taash doses the bodies with a sleeping tonic to put the dragon to sleep for a few hours. On the way to the next vantage point, Rook explains that the
Blighted gods
are corrupting dragons to their service, which upsets Taash. The team travels through the ruins of a Grey Warden outpost, which was abandoned ages ago after Sharksmouth's volcano erupted and flooded the
Deep Roads
with magma. Several pockets of Antaam hide inside the fort, as they cannot build their own (builders are deployed under the
Arigena
, not the
Arishok
).
As the team pursues the Antaam through the fort, they discover the Antaam have surrounded the now-sleeping Vinsomer, which the Antaam apparently want for unknown purposes. Taash claims the right of the kill, then breathes fire across the Antaam, initiating combat. Once Rook and the Veilguard dispatch the remaining Antaam, the Vinsomer flies off safely. After a brief conversation with Taash's mother,
Shathann
, and Lord of Fortune faction leader
Isabela
, Taash joins the team.
Taash will then take Rook on a walk through a stormy portion of the coast during
Fire on the Sands
, where they reveal their dragonfire and melt an Antaam war banner. They also briefly discuss Shathann and introduce Rook to
Karash
, a Qunari navigator who was dragged into the Antaam against his will and now hides here in asylum.
The Rivain Coast holds the ruins of a large abandoned Grey Warden fortress. It was used during the Steel Age
as an outpost against darkspawn from the nearby mountain, but when Sharksmouth erupted, the primary darkspawn tunnels were sealed
, greatly reducing the danger. With the threat eased, the Wardens were reassigned elsewhere and the fortress fell to ruin. The Antaam have since claimed it as their own.
Two portions of the ruined Warden fort can be explored in the Coast. The portion near Dragon Beach (Warden Castle and Castle Bailey) is large and accessible immediately after completing
The Dragon Slayer
; it contains several puzzles and chests, including those part of the quest
The Warden Vault
. The other portion of the fortress is contained within the Lava Caves to the north and is not accessible until beginning
The Lair of the Dragon King
. In addition, several chests, notes, mementos, and quest fights, such as
The Treasure of Sharksmouth Mountain
, will not become available until after completing that quest.
Several regions of the Rivain Coast are haunted by memories of templars and mages in conflict, as seen in
Wrongs of Annulment
. Ambient combat banter from the spirits possessing some of the undead suggest they are mage and templar remains from the
annulment
of the
Circle
at
Dairsmuid
. Caches of abandoned or forgotten pirate loot can be found scattered throughout the Coast, and one large hoard is specifically looted during
Dead Men Tell Some Tales
.
Dragons are known to linger in the shallows of the Rivain Coast, including the
Vinsomer
and
Kataranda the Stormrider
. The wreckage of both Rivain and Antaam ships can be seen from the beaches, and several of these ships were destroyed by the Vinsomer
.
Sharksmouth Mountain.
A large dormant volcano named Sharksmouth Mountain caps the northern portion of the coast. According to local Rivaini custom, it is cursed, and locals are discouraged from disturbing it. Isabela and the Lords of Fortune respect these customs and do not enter the mountain except in dire circumstances
.
The Dragon King has claimed the portion of the fort within Sharksmouth Mountain as his seat of power. When he kidnaps Shathann, he brings her to the mountain's heart to lure Taash, his true target, to her rescue. When Taash and Rook arrive, they are trapped by a cage set on the fortress walls; however, Shathann sacrifices her life to free Taash, who helps Rook defeat the Dragon King's high dragon, then finally kills the Dragon King once and for all, seriously diminishing the Antaam threat to the Coast.

Chest locations
Locations of the chests in the Rivain Coast.
Twenty-five regular chests, three lyrium-dagger chests (28 chests total), and five Evanuris statues are required for 100% completion. See the map for individual locations. Most of chests 1-15 are only accessible after completing
The Dragon Slayer
; chests 16-28 do not become available until acquiring
Lair of the Dragon King
.
Reward for completing
Dead Men Tell Some Tales
.
Reward for completing
Dead Men Tell Some Tales
. One of three lyrium chests.
Reward for completing
Dead Men Tell Some Tales
.
Accessible immediately on reaching the Rivain Coast.
Climb along the northern walls from west to east, using Spite's Reach to reach the highest level. An open cave on the left holds this chest and an Evanuris statue.
Accessible on completing
The Dragon Slayer
.
Three braziers must be lit around the Clifftop in the correct order to access this chest. The number of skeletons surrounding each brazier indicates its position in the order; see the blue numbers on the chest map for their locations.
The gate to this chest is opened by correctly orienting three statues on the floor below. The key is to the right of the locked gate in miniature (see image to right). Drop down the nearby ladder, then arrange the statues from left to right to face: right, left, and backwards. A nearby ladder will drop; take it back up to the now-opened gate and access the chest.
Key to Chest #8.
Chests 9-15 are within the Warden castle. If the main gate near the beacon is barred, explore to the right (west) and then south, taking a staircase up and scrambling up some stacked planks to reach a door on the second floor. This will provide access to the fortress and a lever above the gate to open it for future, as well as a folded ladder which can be released.
To access chest #9, Taash is needed. Detonate the gaatlok in the northwest corner of this room (above, on the second level), to release a ladder. Climb the ladder, then take the balance beams to the right along the entire outer edge of this courtyard. (A room to the left on the way holds the second
Warden Grey
note and some valuables.) A memento can also be picked up before finally dropping down the ladder to reach this chest.
Requires Taash. In the same place as the gaatlok from Chest #9, pick up a wisp. Drop down to ground level, then run through the nearby door north into the fortress and hook an immediate left. Destroy the drakestone if not already done, then carry the wisp to its receptacle to access the chest and a memento.
Note:
One of the levers for The Warden Vault is in this room.
Accessible on reaching the Rivain Coast, encountered as part of the
Dragon Slayer
quest.
Reward for completing
The Warden Vault
.
Reward for completing
The Warden Vault
. One of three lyrium chests.
Reward for completing
The Warden Vault
.
If Rook faces the grate blocking this chest, turn around, exit the room via the door opposite (to the west), and hop over the wall. Behind Rook's landing spot is a nook full of breakable crates and wagons. Break them, then interact with the shields to light all three torches at once. (In order, select left, middle, and then right.) Return to the now-usable lever by the gate to access this chest.
Note:
One of the levers for The Warden Vault is in this room.

Just outside the chest.
In the ruined building at the head of the large skeleton on the Tide Flats beach. Rook must stand on the Resonant Stone before its elevation to reach the walkable spine.
In the south corner of the Tide Flats, hidden in a dirt pile.
Atop the stone arch to the south. If the folded ladder has not yet been dropped, take the ladders nearest the chest until reaching the stone walkway, then hop the south wall after protruding two Resonant Stones from the wall. The brazier is to the right, along with another note from Mel Caravu. Be sure to drop the ladder before hopping down. Once all chests are lit, return to the chest to open the gate.
Accessible after beginning
Lair of the Dragon King
. After reaching the second main level of the Cliff Climb region (near the second folded ladder, about halfway up), take the wooden balance beams westward to reach a series of wooden platforms and beam jumps. The chest is at the top, through a broken stone wall.
Angle needed for chest #19.
Standing northeast from this chest and looking south through the gap in the wall, detonate the gaatlok barrel inside with Taash's dragonfire. The angle on this is extremely tricky; consider standing slightly back and left of the gap in order to find the sweet spot for detonation. After detonation, loop around via the left path to the new break in the wall. Inside, find the chest and a
chipped griffon ornament
.

Evanuris statues
A map of the Evanuris statues in the Rivain Coast.
Five Evanuris statues (two
Fen'Harel
, three
Evanuris
) are required for 100% completion. They are listed left to right.
This Fen'Harel altar is accessible with Harding's ability on the Resonant Stone to the south, then Taash's dragonfire to detonate the gaatlok. The altar is up the stairs to the right, and the statue is southwest, in the shallows.
This Fen'Harel altar in the central Clifftops is high above a tower of cliffs. Clamber all the way up to activate the altar. The statue is in the northwest corner in the pool by the waterfall.
The Evanuris statue in the northern Clifftops is accessed by taking the nearby elevated paths, including Spite's Reach, from west to east. It bears the mark of Falon'din.
From the northeast corner of this looping path in Sharksmouth Mountain, use Harding's ability to elevate the stone platforms. Loot the small chest, then elevate the next set of stone platforms. The Evanuris statue, which bears the mark of Mythal, is on the last elevated ledge.
This Evanuris statue, which bears the mark of Mythal, is tucked inside a nook in the Lava Flows.

Trivia
Several waterfalls in the Rivain Coast have treasure hidden behind or near them.
Several notes can be found from Warden Mel Caravu scattered throughout the region, dated from the Steel Age, discussing her observations of the area and her friendship with another Warden named Tops.

URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/River_Crossing

Location
River Crossing
Type
World map encounter
Terrain
Forest
Location
World map
Enemies
Bandits
Exits
World map
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
River Crossing
is a
World Map
encounter when traveling to or from the
Brecilian Forest
.
Involvement
A group of six bandits will ambush
the Warden
on a hilly river crossing. A trio of archers will rain arrows from the hill, while three melee bandits attack from the linear path leading upwards.
Enemies
Bandits
Bandit (2) (
Normal
)
Bandit Archer (3) (Normal)
Bandit Leader (
Lieutenant
)
Resources
Elfroot
Deathroot
Special objects
Flimsy Leghold Trap
(2) - wooden claw traps, medium difficulty, encountered on the path heading uphill.
Strategy
Map of the Area
Depending on their equipment, the bandits will use the following talents:
Aim
Scattershot
Momentum
Flurry
Cripple
Powerful Swings
Sunder Armor
Sunder Arms
- Bandit leader only.
Critical Strike
- Bandit leader only.
The archers' Scattershot can be very damaging when chained, so make sure to disrupt their shooting.
Paralysis Explosion
is the best, as spells like
Mass Paralysis
or
Blizzard
will require too much time, while
Glyph of Warding
offers no protection from it.
Fireball
or even your own Scattershot will also suffice.
As in the
The Long Road
encounter, try not to send party members uphill to engage the archers, because of the traps. Also, once they have used up their Scattershot, they will be little more than a distraction for you.

Dragon Age: Origins
Upon arriving at the
Roadside
,
the Warden
encounters a battle between several
mages
and
darkspawn
. A mage with two apprentices is standing atop a little hill, preparing to defend against a wave of darkspawn. Luckily for them, the Warden assists in the fight. This encounter only occurs if the Warden sided with the mages during the
Broken Circle
questline.
Characters
Mage
– seen taking the apprentices to
Redcliffe
; First Enchanter
Irving
expressed to him that the Warden needs every able-bodied mage for the war.
Mage Apprentices
(2)
Enemies
Map of the Area
Darkspawn
Hurlock
(
Normal
)
Hurlock Alpha
(
Lieutenant
)
Resources
Deathroot
Strategy
Cleansing Aura
is very effective for helping keep the Mages alive.
Paralysis Explosion
is very useful, incapacitating most darkspawn and  making mages'
Tempest
increasingly powerful.
All of the darkspawn use melee weapons and as such, the only way for them to harm the mages is to run uphill, using one narrow path. While it is impossible to completely block it off with
Glyph of Repulsion
, a melee-oriented party can still bottleneck them. Additionally, it is a great place to lay down traps.
All of the magi allies provide powerful support and are able to cast the following spells:
Mage
Spell Might
Stonefist
Petrify
Earthquake
Fireball
Shock
Lightning
Tempest
Paralyze
Bugs
If the two apprentice allies perish, the Mage will still refer to the apprentices as alive.

sees a battle between

darkspawn

Notes
Any of the (up to 4) Redcliffe soldiers who survive can be talked to, but, regardless of which one the Warden interacts with, the response ("
Arl Eamons
[sic]
sends his regards, Warden. We're trying to keep the roads clear and safe.

is very effective for helping keep all of the Redcliffe Soldiers alive.

Resources

Notable items

Sapphire
Notes
One of the Elven Scouts defending the Rocky Road
The Elven Scouts can use
Rapid Shot
and
Critical Shot
.
Cleansing Aura
is very effective for helping keep the Elven scouts alive.
The shrieks can decimate the scouts. It's best to take them out first to save the scouts.
The scouts appear to be wearing armor similar to the
Ancient Elven Armor Set
.
This battle is particularly easy if you have
Paralysis Explosion
. Almost all darkspawn will be affected and due to their low rank, they go down very quickly under concentrated fire (shrieks notwithstanding.) Also, as all genlocks and hurlocks are archers, they will remain in the same spot, making them sitting ducks for AoE like
Blizzard
or
Inferno
.
See also
The
Wooded Highway
encounter will triggered instead if the Warden sided with
the Lady of the Forest
.

The
Royal Palace
is the ancestral residence of the royal
Theirin family
, and the seat of power in
Ferelden
. It is also where
the Landsmeet
is traditionally held. Ferelden's throne is within the Palace's Landsmeet chamber.
Involvement
Dragon Age: The Calling
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: The Calling
.
In 9:10
Dragon
, King
Maric Theirin
and Teyrn
Loghain Mac Tir
receives a group of
Grey Wardens
in the throne chamber. From
Orlais
, the Wardens are led by
Genevieve
, and are presented by
First Enchanter
Remille
. They state that a
Grey Warden
who had knowledge of the location of the remaining
Old Gods
has been captured by the
darkspawn
and thus
Thedas
possibly faces the threat of a new
Blight
. That Grey Warden's last known location was
Ortan Thaig
. As the only known living persons to have reached the thaig are Maric and Loghain in 8:99
Blessed
, the Wardens request that Loghain accompany them on their mission. When Loghain flatly refuses to help them find the lost
thaig
, King Maric surprises everyone and elects to accompany the Wardens himself.
Dragon Age: Origins
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Teyrn Loghain Mac Tir
and his troops return to the Royal Palace from
Ostagar
, after the
battle
, in which he declares himself as
Queen Anora
's regent to the assembled nobles.
During
the Landsmeet
, the
Fereldan Civil War
is resolved when the Warden confronts Teyrn Loghain in the Palace's Landsmeet chamber, with Ferelden once again united under one banner.
Finally, the new ruler of Ferelden will be officially coronated in the Royal Palace at the end of the
Fifth Blight

This section contains spoilers for

Dragon Age: Origins

Enemies
There will be a duel between the Warden (or a champion chosen by the Warden) and Teyrn Loghain. Note that this fight cannot be avoided, and unlike all other companions, Alistair will immediately kill Loghain if he chosen to fight the duel.
Loghain Mac Tir
- (
Human
,
Boss
)
───────
If the Landsmeet outcome favors Loghain, or the Warden wins the argument but opts to attack Loghain rather than agreeing to a honorable duel, there will be an all out brawl within the chamber. Queen Anora's royal guards will assist whichever side Anora supported during the Landsmeet. Furthermore, the Warden's party will be joined by Redcliffe soldiers, the nobles who supported the Warden, and their soldiers.
Note:
This fight is ended by the Grand Cleric when Loghain's health drops to very low levels. At that point the one-on-one duel takes place.
Note:
When the health of a noble who assists you in this fight falls below a certain level, he or she will retreat from the chamber along with their troops.

Arcane Horror
Corpse
Skeleton archer
Raiders
Raider
Notable items
Spider's Silk Gland
- source:
Venomous spider
.
Bugs
If you re-enter the Ruined Passage after you finish it, Kelder will be still in your party following you.
If the Spider's Silk Gland is not picked up by the end of the Magistrate's Orders quest, then you may be unable to complete the Herbalist's Tasks quest. Once you complete Magistrate's Orders the north-eastern section of the dungeon will be inaccessible due to the doors no longer opening.
A workaround to being unable to go to the item drop is to manually add it to your inventory via
console
command:
runscript additem one100it_gland.uti 1

======================================================================
PAGE: Ruined Temple
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Inquisition locations, Dragon Age: Origins locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Ruined_Temple
======================================================================

Location
Ruined Temple
Type
Ruin
Terrain
Underground
Location
Frostback Mountains
(near
Haven
)
Inhabitants
Cultists
Demons
Exits
Mountainside Caverns
Mountain Top
World map
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Dragon Age: Inquisition
Trespasser
(mentioned)
The
Ruined Temple
is the dilapidated remains of the Temple of
Andraste
. Sitting high atop the frigid
Frostback Mountains
, the temple's interior is swathed with sheets of ice and snow. Nevertheless, considering the number of years since Andraste's followers roamed its halls, the temple is remarkably intact.
Background
Following the execution of Andraste, her disciple
Havard
retrieved her
remains
and carried them back to her homeland. There, her followers interred her ashes among the mountains, building a temple which became a refuge for
Andrastians
in
Alamarri
lands.
The temple was eventually abandoned, however, and became the sanctum of the
Disciples of Andraste
, a society composed of the violent, dragon-worshiping inhabitants of the
Village of Haven
. The Temple is built into the mountain itself, extending deep into its
caverns
, which lead to the
Mountain Top
.
Involvement
Dragon Age: Origins
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
The temple was rediscovered by
the Warden
and Brother
Ferdinand Genitivi
in 9:30
Dragon
, with the former on a
quest
to discover the fabled
Urn of Andraste
in order to heal the ailing
Arl Eamon
of
Redcliffe
. Eventually information of the temple's location leaked to the
Chantry
who restored it to proper condition.
Dragon Age: Inquisition
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
The Temple after refurbishment
A decade after its rediscovery, the temple is refurbished by the
Chantry
and becomes a holy site for the Andrastian faithful. In 9:41 Dragon, the
Temple of Sacred Ashes
hosts a peace summit for
Rebel Mages
,
templars
, and Chantry leaders in hopes of negotiating peace for the ongoing
Mage-Templar War
. Subsequently, all those in attendance are killed by an explosion and the temple laid to ruin. The explosion creates a huge
Veil
tear, called
the Breach
, in the heart of the temple. Somehow, a
lone survivor
escapes unharmed and is immediately apprehended by the
Left
and
Right
hands of the
Divine
.
The survivor sets off with a small party to investigate the Breach at the temple remains. While there, they manage to temporarily close the Breach after battling a
Pride demon
. The lone survivor, now labeled the Herald of Andraste, assists the Chantry in establishing an Inquisition in hopes of closing the Breach once and for all. The Herald requires the aid of
mages
or
templars
to successfully close the Breach; in order to do so they must put an end to the Mage-Templar War by deciding to ally with one of the groups. The combined forces manage to seal the Breach, however, a larger
threat
emerges, revealing the Breach can only be sealed with the
Orb of Destruction
. A final confrontation with the Elder One results in the Breach finally being sealed.

Location

Exits

Appearances
Dragon Age II
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: Inquisition
-
The Descent
The
Ruins of Heidrun Thaig
are all that remain of the once proud Heidrun Thaig. It is located beneath a section of the
Deep Roads
known as the
Darkspawn Warrens
.
Background
The
thaig
was built before the
First Blight
and it served as an important
lyrium
mining source for the
dwarves
.
It was dedicated to
Paragon
Heidrun the Deep whose remains are entombed under a massive stone statue located at the center of the thaig.
It is also known that in
ancient
times an "incident" involving "creatures that wear the faces of the dead" disturbed the mining operations and warranted direct military intervention by the
king
himself.
Centuries later, members of the
Legion of the Dead
would often visit the ruins. Once they encountered a
rock wraith
in an old mine shaft near the thaig.
Another time they found the former prison of an
Archdemon
below Heidrun Thaig, filled with the corpses of
darkspawn
who had died while praying to their god.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
The Inquisitor
may visit the location as part of
The Descent
with Lieutenant
Renn
and
Shaper
Valta
to discover the source of the earthquakes that have plagued
Orzammar
's
lyrium
mines.

Notes
Throughout the thaig and in nearby
Deep Roads
locations,
the Inquisitor
can find
gears
made from a metal that
Shaper
Valta
cannnot recognize.
A level 25 Nug has been spotted near the Gate of Segrummar closest to the Legion Of The Dead Camp, killing it yields roughly 300 XP, a Dwarven Dragon Statuette (varies) and 3500 gold.
[
confirmation needed
]
Five
supply caches
can be found throughout Ruins of Heidrun Thaig.
The lower bridge operation unlocks the southernmost bridge, located near the camp. The central bridge operation unlocks the bridge near the monument. On the map, both are only listed as "Completed Operations" without any distinguishing annotation. The
Deep Roads Expeditions: Heidrun Thaig Upper Bridge
operation, despite the name, creates a bridge next to the fast travel marker in the
Darkspawn Warrens
.
If the Inquisitor goes down the right hand fork of the bridges from the Upper Bridge construction and takes a left at the end, they can enter a house which has a wheel of cheese on a table. Taking this starts an unmarked quest to meet the Nug King in the
Forgotten Caverns
.

Dragon Age: Origins
This
Run-Down Back Street
is one of the many back alleys in the streets of
Denerim
.
Involvement
The Warden
must deal with the gang here (as well as two more in the

) to finish the

questline. The Warden will also find the Hooded Courier for

- encountered during the

Note:
Enemy skills and recommended tactics can be found on the
Back Alley Justice
article.
Special objects
Ser Friden's corpse - looting his
journal
from his corpse initiates
the Last Request
questline and unlocks the
Deserted Building
on the city map.
Bugs
This location can already be seen on the Denerim City Map prior to accepting the
Back Alley Justice
questline. Traveling to this location before accepting the aforementioned quest will create an encounter with a group of
Thugs
. After killing them this quest will be added to the Journal; however, when the quest is updated later (after visiting the other locations) a number of bugs may occur, such as failing to register the fight or not adding the other locations on the Denerim City Map. It is recommended to not venture to the Run-Down Back Street until the Back Alley Justice questline has been initiated.

======================================================================
PAGE: Sahrnia
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Inquisition locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Sahrnia
======================================================================

Location
Sahrnia
Type
Village
Location
Emprise Du Lion
,
Orlais
Inhabitants
Orlesians
Appearances
Dragon Age: Inquisition
Sahrnia
is a village in
Orlais
located in northeast
Emprise Du Lion
. The town's primary income is from the local quarries which export material all over
Thedas
.
Background
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
In approximately 9:41
Dragon
, Sahrnia fell into severe economic decline due to the ongoing
War of the Lions
. The war not only caused Sahrnia's mining trade to completely shut down but allowed for a large
Red templar
force to occupy the surrounding hills, leaving the town completely isolated. Mistress
Poulin
, leader of the village, sold her family's mine to the Red Templars, unaware of their true motives. When they started demanding that she provide them with miners, she sold many of the town's villagers in exchange for supplies for the remaining townsfolk.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
We arrived in the
Highlands
at night. I was immediately given the task of overseeing the acquisition and staffing of the Sahrnia quarry. I asked the
general
why we needed a quarry, if the
crystals
will grow anywhere. Apparently the
Elder One
believes the composition of the earth here will ensure that it grows more rapidly and abundantly.
Some of my men feel we should take the quarry by force. The general did not specify how I was to secure the land, so I am considering a more subtle approach. Any suspicions we raise will increase the chance of a military investigation, perhaps even the newly formed
Inquisition
. We must operate in secret as long as possible.
I scouted the quarry yesterday. It is quiet. With the war raging, I expect demand for luxury granite has decreased significantly. So much is gained through commerce. Why not exploit that?
—From the writings of
Knight-Captain Fornier
Characters
Linette
Louis
Granny Mae
Michel de Chevin
Mistress
Poulin
Quests
The Corruption of Sahrnia
Call Me Imshael

Resources
Herbs

Metal

See also
Knight-Captain's Orders
Trivia
Sahrnia is named for
Sarnia, Ontario
, according to
Dragon Age: Inquisition
editor Ben Gelinas; the name was suggested without revealing to writer Sheryl Chee that the name belongs to a real place.

PAGE: Secluded Courtyard
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Inquisition locations, Locations

Location
Secluded Courtyard
Type
Courtyard
Terrain
Urban
Location
Val Royeaux
,
Orlais
Enemies
Mercenaries
Quests
A Friend of Red Jenny
Exits
World Map
Appearances
Dragon Age: Inquisition
The
Secluded Courtyard
is typical of the city of
Val Royeaux
, where even mundane and functional locales must be decorated.
Background
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
The Secluded Courtyard is only accessible during
Sera
's
companion quest
A Friend of Red Jenny
.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
After
collecting
the red handkerchiefs in Val Royeaux,
the Inquisitor
travels to the Secluded Courtyard at night and is ambushed by the guards of a minor noble who wants to dispatch the Inquisitor. After fighting off several mercenaries, the nobleman proceeds to brag about his importance and how he's certain that the Inquisition has spent large amounts of resources in order to find and assassinate him. Before he can get far into his speech, however, he is struck in the head with an arrow.
The perpetrator turns out to be an
Elven
woman named
Sera
. She is part of the
Friends of Red Jenny
and is the one who tipped you off to the ambush. With great amusement, she tells of how she stole the breeches off of all the guards, who then rush in to attack. Once everyone has been killed, Sera offers her services to the Inquisition.

Boisvert Mansion

working at the Tower. He gives the Warden some additional information about what is happening and what to do. The Warden also encounters three blood mages who attack on sight. Afterwards one will beg for their life. It's the Warden's choice to either kill her or let her go.

may make remarks at certain spots:

Wynne

Zevran

======================================================================
PAGE: Serault
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Serault
======================================================================

“Once, she was a feared player in
the Great Game
. But that was before her Shame. Now she is a pariah-state, a refuge for the unwanted, the misunderstood, and the unlikely.”
Location
Serault
Type
Marquisate
Location
Orlais
Leaders
Marquis of Serault
Inhabitants
Humans
Dwarves
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Last Court
Dragon Age: Inquisition
(mentioned)
Serault
is a
marquisate
in
Orlais
, a forested region located in the far west of the country, on the edge of the
Tirashan
forest. It is a realm of deep forests, stubborn people, and customs that border on heresy. The citizens of the region are largely craftsmen and the pride of the marquisate is its famed glasswork. Notably, Serault is referred to as "she" and "her" rather than "it" by its inhabitants.
The marquisate is the setting for the story-driven text-based game,
Dragon Age: The Last Court
, in which the player, great-grandchild of
the Shame of Serault
, makes decisions as the
Marquis of Serault
. The player takes the role of The Huntress or The Scholar, each with unique abilities and challenges.
Background
Serault was a prosperous and famed Marquisate but fell into ruin due to the former Marquis of Serault who was an
apostate
mage
, who later became an
abomination
. He was described as brilliant, murderous, generous, romantic, ruthless, but eventually he brought shame to the marquisate. His name is now forgotten and he is known only as the "Shame of Serault".
The
Chantry
chapel in Serault has been closed since shame was brought upon the town. Furthermore, the ruling family's ancestral mask, the symbol of the
nobility
in Orlais, was stripped from them by one of the
Divines
, in retaliation for the Shame of Serault's excesses. For over a century, the Glass-Antlered Mask of Serault has been in the possession of the Chantry, eventually passing to Divine
Justinia V
.
Involvement
Divine Justinia V elects to visit Serault to assess whether Serault has overcome its shameful past. The Marquis of Serault sets out to take advantage of this opportunity to restore Serault's station and influence.
The
Inquisition
later seeks out Serault's support and follows up on the Divine's visit.
Governance
Serault heraldry
Serault is ruled by the Marquis of Serault, of the
de Serault
family who have ruled the town for several generations from its ancestral chateau, located on an island in the middle of the river. The mirrored stag is the heraldry of Serault and "Payment in Glass" is its motto.
Previously, Serault was closely tied to her neighbor Alyons, through an alliance of marriage. The
Shame of Serault
was wed to the daughter of the then Marquis of Alyons.
Religion
The beliefs of the
Andrastian
Chantry mingle with curious local customs in Serault, which, fortunately for the believers in Serault, is far from the prying eyes of the
Grand Cathedral
. The Abbey of the Bans is a popular local chantry despite these slight deviations.
There are also stranger, more heretical, chantry groups in the fringes around Serault. The largest splinter group is "The Cult of Masked Andraste" who worship Masked Andraste, a huntress-aspect version of Andraste, and has several shrines in the forests around Serault. The symbol of the cult is a bow. They gather and conduct their rites in chantry robes and black masks of charred wood and pray to a wooden statue of a Masked Andraste.
Industry and Commerce
Although there is trade in the rare trophies and artifacts foraged from the Applewoods, Serault survives primarily off glassware and, in particular, the mirrors produced by its nationally renown Glassworks. Those learned in the arts of glassblowing are highly prized by the town, but to a fault - those who work for the Glassworks are not ever permitted to leave the building for fear talent will be lost to poaching from rival marquisates, emigration, or the general hazards of life on the fringe of civilization. As such, many who work in the Glassworks resent their situation, but love their art too much to do more than stage mild protests at their gilded cage. They are currently represented at Serault's court by the Smiling Guildmistress, a glassworker-turned-advocate.
Serault town is a thriving river port although mediocre management prevents it from reaching its full potential.
Serault's position as Orlais' western-most holding has resulted in lackluster trade, with the few traders willing to  make the journey only interested in purchasing the town's famed glass works with
Royals
, or elegances like
Antivan
silks. As such, the town's economy has struggled in the past as much needed wares have failed to materialize in the town. The occasional bandit outfit has been known to prowl the roads to town, and river pirates have accosted traders utilizing the area's waterways, further placing a stranglehold on the marquisate.
Places
Serault's geographic location
The Applewoods – a region of Tirashan Forest renowned for its hunting and its mysteries
The Greenwood – the tamer area of The Applewoods that teems with game
The Deepwoods – the depths of the Applewoods that teem with things quite sinister
The Greenvault – a clearing where the ash trees soar as tall as temple-pillars
Serault Town – a bustling port town dotted with gold stone and red roofs
Chateau Serault – known as "The Thousand-Windowed Castle"
Tower of Lights – the highest point of Chateau Serault; the tower was built to be the heart of the Shame's heliograph network—a web of towers communicating by mirror-flash
Heron Tower
West Tower
Tilted Tower
The Foal's Tower
The Whistling Gallery
Last River
The Bridge of Masks – the only path east leading from the Chateau to Serault Town
The Green Bridge – the only way west, to the forest
The Anchoress' Dell
The Abbey of the Bans – a prominent but somewhat unorthodox Chantry
Pastulaire
(formerly)
– last held by the late husband of
The Acerbic Dowager
, who is described as "a vassal of Serault... until he permitted his peasants too many liberties"

Notes
In-text mentions of the des Guenoles noble family (including the
The Muttering Banker
and his sister,
The Elegant Abbess
) may imply the existence of a village within the Serault marquisate called Guenoles, where the family would be based and hold surrounding lands.
