======================================================================
PAGE: Taoran's Camp
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Taoran's_Camp
======================================================================

Location
Taoran's Camp
Type
Scripted encounter
Terrain
Clearing
Location
World map
Inhabitants
Blackstone Irregulars
Quests
A Change in Leadership
Exits
World map
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Taoran's Camp
is a
scripted encounter
in
Dragon Age: Origins
that occurs on the
World map
.
Background
After
the Warden
accepts
A Change in Leadership
, the party will need to confront
Raelnor Hawkwind
in the
Denerim Market District
. They can choose to either kill Raelnor or side with him against his son,
Taoran
. The encounter will trigger upon leaving
Denerim
to another
Ferelden
location after aligning with one or the other.
Note:
If this quest is accepted from a
Blackstone Liaison
, other than the one in Denerim, the party will encounter Taoran's Camp prior to talking to Raelnor. If this occurs, do not converse with anyone else until confronting Raelnor in Denerim's Market District.
Involvement
Taoran will be accompanied by eight Blackstone Irregulars (three of which are archers). If the Warden killed his father in Denerim before, they won't be able to initiate any kind of discussion with him. However, if the Warden sided with Raelnor they can initiate a fight with him and his men or intimidate him into paying 3
for not killing him (intimidation always works - no coercion skill required) or just walk away from the family business.
Resources
Elfroot
Gallery
Map of the area

Location
Tapster's Tavern
Type
Tavern
Terrain

A Tale of Orzammar
Dragon Age II
(mentioned)
Tapster's Tavern
is a popular
tavern
in the
Orzammar Commons
which serves
dwarven liquor
as well as food.
Background
A sign in Tapster's Tavern
The tavern does not serve
casteless
. Furthermore, it is also known that it shares some ties with the
Carta
when it was under leadership of
Beraht
, since the owner of the tavern seen in the
Dwarf Commoner Origin
allows the Dwarf Commoner to find one of Tapster's
current customers
and kill him.
Involvement
Unlike other taverns and inns in
Ferelden
,
the Warden
can't trade in Tapster's. However, there are several characters here that they can talk to. The Warden can talk to
Corra
.
If the Warden is a Dwarven Noble she will not give you any information, otherwise she can tell you about the Shaperate, the Diamond Quarter, Dust Town, and the Proving.
You can order some drinks from her, either an ale for 2
, some mead for 3 copper or order the best brew for your whole party for
1
20
. This does not seem to have any effects except losing money however.
Dwarven drinking song
The Warden can also listen to the following
dwarven
drinking song here:
"Ooohh, I've heard of dwarves who get in fights, 'bout every time they drink,
And those who need to have a woman just to help them think,
And if you want to see a dwarf whine and beg and plead,
Just pour out all his ale and take away his mead!
There's never been a
Paragon
of Wisdom or of Thinking,
And though I tried a time or two, I ne'r got raised for stinking,
But hang around here long enough and don't you go a blinking,
'
Cuz someday I am gonna be the Paragon of Drinking!"
Note:
To hear the translation, the subtitles need to be turned on as it is sung in dwarven.

deshyr
of

and the associated

PAGE: Tavern (Redcliffe Village)

======================================================================

For taverns in other
Dragon Age
titles, see
Taverns
.
Location
Redcliffe Village Tavern
Type
Tavern
Location
Redcliffe Village
Inhabitants
Humans
Elves
Quests
A Village Under Siege
Exits
Redcliffe Village
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
The
Redcliffe Village Tavern
is a
tavern
located in the village of
Redcliffe
. It is owned by
Lloyd
prior to the
events
in Redcliffe.
Characters
Bella
– the waitress
Berwick
– a strange elf
Lloyd
– the owner and bartender
Militia
– several complaining customers
Quests
A Village Under Siege
Enemies
Berwick
(conditional)
Special objects
"
Mysterious Chest in Redcliffe
" - once outside of the tavern, the Warden will see a ship dry dock with a chest and wooden crate on top. The Mysterious Chest can be reached using a ramp located on the side of the dock.
pc
There is a non-interactive keg inside the tavern containing a
control rod
. It was a remnant of the original process needed to acquire
Shale
before the golem was cut from the main game and then later added back in as a
DLC
.
Notes
If the Warden intimidates Lloyd into fighting, Bella will assume control of his
inventory
and charge nothing when "buying" from her and give nothing when "selling" to her. It is possible to use her as a free "storage chest," although how long items will remain in the chest is unconfirmed. On the downloaded
pc
ps3
xbox360
version, the Warden cannot "sell" items to Bella.
If Lloyd survives the
battle
, he will return to the tavern, but Bella will still be in charge. He will resume running the tavern (i.e. a trading
merchant
with unlimited flasks) after the Warden helped her start a new life.
Lloyd can be killed and thus the ownership of the tavern will pass on to the Warden. Bella will then open up the
free stock
to them.
Lloyd can be intimidated into accepting the Warden as a partner in the tavern. His inventory will then shift from
trading merchant
to non-trading
free supplies
and will close if forced to go fight. After
The Attack at Nightfall
, the Warden can transfer ownership to Bella, instead of helping her leave Redcliffe.
Trivia
There is a small, clickable area next to Lloyd when he is behind the bar, when clicked upon it will read '"Don't eat the cheese."
If Bella takes permanent ownership of the Tavern she renames it "The
Grey Wardens
' Rest".
When the
Inquisition
visits Redcliffe in 9:40 Dragon, they find no sign of the old tavern, Lloyd, or Bella. Instead, a much larger tavern named the "
Gull and Lantern
" serves beverages to the villagers.
Bugs
An infinite money glitch can be performed here if you have the
The Stone Prisoner
expansion installed.
Intimidating
Lloyd
into fighting for the militia leaves Bella in charge, she gives you supplies for free. Plus, every time you leave the tavern (and return), the
Crystals
for
Shale
will have regenerated in her inventory. Simply repeat the process of buying, exiting and entering till you have a healthy stash of crystals, then sell them to the blacksmith in the village.
Upon Lloyd's leaving or death the text changes to Take/Stash from Buy/Sell. However It seems impossible to deposit anything.
Bella will "Sell" items for
0
after the Warden kills or recruits Lloyd; the problem with this is she will "Buy" items for
0
too, which means you're giving away items for nothing.
Gallery
The Tavern's kitchen
Map of the area

======================================================================
PAGE: Tearstone Island
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Tearstone_Island
======================================================================

Our community
needs more
information on this page! Can you help out?
Click here to add more
.
Location
Tearstone Island
Type
Island
Terrain
Coastline
Location
Venefication Sea
[
confirmation needed
]
Enemies
Antaam
Darkspawn
Deepstalkers
Mercenaries
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
Tearstone Island
is an island off the northern coast of
Rivain
.
Background
Not much is known about Tearstone Island except that it contains many
elven
ruins. It is suspected that its local stronghold may have belonged to
Mythal
in ancient times.
In contemporary times, the local stronghold is the base of
Elgar'nan
and
Ghilan'nain
, who have
blighted
the stronghold and use
darkspawn
and
blood magic
wards to guard against intruders. The stronghold is the location of the
elven gods
' ritual site, which is where Elgar'nan generated an eclipse and where he crafts a
Red lyrium
dagger that can pierce the
Veil
. Recently, the elven gods recruited the entire
Rebel
Antaam
and mercenaries to serve in their army, which has bolstered the island's impressive defenses.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
During the quest
For the Love of Treviso
, the
Butcher
gives in to
Ghilan'nain
's control in order to find out where she and
Elgar'nan
are planning their ritual to complete their blighted dagger. He tells
Rook
that the two are planning to finish it on Tearstone Island.
During
The Shadowed Sun
, the team discuss their plans to head to Tearstone Island.
Taash
says that the entire
Antaam
fleet has gathered there, and it will be difficult to slip through. Nevertheless, the team head to Tearstone island in a small rowboat, beginning the quest
Isle of the Gods
.
The
Veilguard
was forced to react immediately to the sudden initiation of Elgar'nan's ritual and attack the island with just their small team. The Veilguard split into two teams: one led by Rook to disrupt the ritual to create the
red lyrium
dagger while a distraction team (led by either
Davrin
or
Lace Harding
) drew the Antaam army away from Rook's team. The distraction team exploded some Antaam
dreadnaughts
and continued to lead the bulk of Antaam troops away while Rook's team faught their way to the gods' ritual site. Eventually the elven gods caught on that Rook's team was making their way to them and alerted the Antaam in order to stop them.
As the elven gods created their magical dagger, their ritual began creating
Fade tears
throughout the island. The Veilguard was hindered by the gods' blood magic wards, with either
Bellara
or
Neve Gallus
having to risk their lives to dispel them. The mage who dispelled the wards was soon abducted by Elgar'nan and taken through an eluvian.
The Veilguard was forced to press on without their companion and faced Ghilan'nain. The Veilguard planned to ambush Ghilan'nain with a two-pronged attack but found that Ghilan'nain was stalking and capturing Rook's companions during the battle. Rook freed some of their companions from their blight tendril traps which helped the Veilguard endure the battle. Ghilan'nain however, was able to subdue
Lucanis
, so the leader of the distraction team attacked Ghilan'nain as a distraction so Lucanis could slay Ghilan'nain with
Solas' lyrium dagger
. While Lucanis was successful in assasinating Ghilan'nain, the leader of the distraction team was murdered while fighting Ghilan'nain. After witnessing Ghilan'nain's death, Elgar'nan escaped the island with the red lyrium dagger.
Solas
had planned on the Fade tears caused by Ghilan'nain being slain with his
lyrium dagger
, and used the opportunity to trade places with
Rook
as he escaped the
Prison of Regrets
, while he trapped Rook there. Fortunately, Rook was able to navigate his way out of the prison and Rook's companions pulled them out of there with a Fade tear.
The island contains Elven Ruins which the team had to fight through to reach Ghilan'nain. After her death and Rook's disappearance into the
Fade
, Elgar'nan becomes furious and casts a pillar of fire on the whole island. The spell is strong enough that survivors see purple for hours afterwards. It decimates the Antaam, and turns the whole island to glass.
Places
Beaches
Antaam Camp
Cliffside Ruins
Characters
Elgar'nan
Ghilan'nain
Issbaqoun
Quests
Isle of the Gods
Creatures
Hostile creatures

Instructions in a Shaky Hand

Trivia
Tearstone does not have a location on the world map.
Gallery

PAGE: Tellari swamps

======================================================================

Location
Tellari swamps
Type
Marsh
Terrain
Wetland
Location
Antiva
Inhabitants
Humans
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: The Silent Grove
The
Tellari swamps
are an area of marshland at the head of the major
river
in
Antiva
, near the town of Seleny.
Background
Antivans believe that
Yavana
, a
Witch of the Wilds
, lives in the swamps. They tell ghost stories about the wetlands and talk about beast men and the unborn children of drowned girls. The swamps are feared greatly, and devoid of civilized habitation.
Involvement
Dragon Age: The Silent Grove
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: The Silent Grove
.
King
Alistair
of
Ferelden
,
Varric Tethras
and
Isabela
venture into the swamps to try and locate Yavana. They search for a guide in Seleny, but nobody will accompany them, even when offered a fortune.
Deep in the swamps, the trio are attacked by a
high dragon
, who is soothed when Yavana appears.
Places
Silent Grove

======================================================================
PAGE: Templar's Nightmare
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Templar's_Nightmare
======================================================================

Location
Templar's Nightmare
Location
The Fade
Inhabitants
Spirits
Demons
Enemies
Demons
Quests
Broken Circle
The Fade: Lost in Dreams
Exits
Fade Pedestal
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
The
Templar's Nightmare
is a location in
the Fade
created by the
Sloth demon
that
the Warden
ventures during the
Lost in Dreams
questline.
Background
This area is notable as being a worst-case scenario for
Templars
—as it is filled with
apostate
mages
and
demons
.
Involvement
Navigational map of the area
This is one of the more confusing areas to navigate, since it is riddled with Mouse Holes, Fade Portals and a Spirit Door that will transport the Warden between locations.
A Mouse Hole is always directly connected with another mouse hole so that it is possible to go back and forth between them, and it is usually reasonably obvious from looking at the map where the Warden will end up when entering one. The only exception in this area is that the last mouse hole (which you enter when following
Vereveel
at the end) has no return path. However, turning around and going back through a Fade Portal or Spirit Door that the Warden just came through does not necessarily return the Warden to the previous location
The modified map to the right illustrates the navigational paths of the area. Note that some paths have a little red arrow on them, meaning that they can only be traveled in the indicated direction.
Quests
Broken Circle
Lost in Dreams
Hostile creatures
Masquerading Ghoul
Blight Wolf
(
Normal
)

(Normal)

(
Critter
and Normal)

(
Lieutenant
) - one of five demons encountered prior to reaching the

.

PAGE: Temple of Mythal
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Inquisition locations, Locations

Location
Temple of Mythal
Type

Location

,

Appearances

(mentioned)
The
Temple of Mythal
is one of the few major religious sites dedicated to the
Elven pantheon
still standing. Its location far from civilization, deep in the
Arbor Wilds
and the persistent disappearances of any who approach the Temple have all served to leave it largely untouched by the outside world. The Temple apparently was somehow distinct from the nearby
Altar
dedicated to
Mythal
.
Background
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
The Temple of Mythal was a place of justice, where petitioners walked religious rites of passage in order to have their pleas for justice from Mythal heard. According to some it is also the site of some mysterious religious artifact called the
vir'abelasan
.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
The Temple of Mythal becomes the objective of a major military engagement between the forces of
The Elder One
and the re-formed
Inquisition
and their allies. As the battle rages outside in the
Arbor Wilds
, the vanguard of the elder one's army breaches the Temple to seize the
Well of Sorrows
guarded by the
Sentinel elves
. The Inquisitor may pursue them immediately or explore the temple briefly and complete the ancient elven rites of petition before proceeding further into the temple.

======================================================================
PAGE: Thaig
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Thaig
======================================================================

Concept art of a thaig
Thaig
is a
dwarven term
used for their
underground
settlements. They were once all connected to one another via the
Deep Roads
. Thaigs are often named after a prominent
house
and in some cases founded by
Paragons
.
Hewn from the underground rock of
Thedas
itself, the physical structure of thaigs can last millennia, even if many of them have been abandoned or destroyed by
darkspawn
raids, their inhabitants killed or fled. This structure often allows the thaigs to serve as fortresses in addition to their other roles.
History
One of the first thaigs was the
Primeval Thaig
, which is so ancient that its existence is not even recorded in
dwarven
history. Though the
Memories
supposedly date back to the foundation of the "First Thaig," the Primeval Thaig is much older.
After the outbreak of the
First Blight
, the darkspawn horde invaded the dwarven empire through the
Deep Roads
and most thaigs were lost to the horde. Only the four major kingdoms of
Orzammar
,
Gundaar
,
Hormak
and
Kal-Sharok
managed to survive, sealing the Deep Roads to delay the horde's advances. By -35
Ancient
(1165 TE), only the kingdoms of Orzammar and Kal-Sharok remained.
Great thaigs
The dwarven kingdoms originally consisted of twelve "great thaigs"
and numerous smaller thaigs spanning the length and breadth of Thedas, but most have fallen. There are only three great thaigs still inhabited, Kal-Sharok, Orzammar and the recently reclaimed
Kal'Hirol
.
Inhabited thaigs
In the present day, these thaigs are still inhabited by dwarves.
Kal-Sharok
– former capital of the dwarven empire, as well as the capital of the Kingdom of Kal-Sharok; it lies deep beneath the
Hunterhorn Mountains
, between
Orlais
and the
Anderfels
Orzammar
– capital of the Kingdom of Orzammar and what remains of the dwarven empire; it lies beneath the
Frostback Mountains
, between
Ferelden
and Orlais
Kal'Hirol
– once the center of smithing knowledge; Kal'Hirol was lost during the First Blight and reclaimed by the dwarves in 9:31
Dragon
Lost thaigs
In the present day, these thaigs have been lost to the darkspawn.
"
Abandoned
"
– an unnamed thaig located beneath the western end of
Sundermount
in the
Free Marches
Aeducan
– the ancestral thaig of
House Aeducan
; it is labeled as "
Ruined Thaig
" in
A Tale of Orzammar
DLC
and the
Dwarf Noble Origin
Amgarrak
– a hidden thaig whereby dwarven smiths sought to research, replicate and improve on Caridin's work by studying and altering existing golems; contact with the thaig was lost under mysterious circumstances
Bownammar
– also known as the
Dead Trenches
or the
City of the Dead
; it is an ancient fortress designed by
Paragon
Caridin
which used to be the former headquarters of the
Legion of the Dead
; having passed possession between the Legion and the darkspawn for several centuries, it was lost to the darkspawn in 9:13 Dragon
Cad'halash
– an ancient settlement which once became a sanctuary for
elven
refugees from
Arlathan
; when this was discovered by the dwarves of Kal-Sharok, they destroyed the thaig in order to cover-up the evidence and not jeopardize their alliance with the
Tevinter Imperium
Cadash
– it was built upon Cad'halash
Darmallon
– it was one of Thedas' greatest sources of gold;
according to legend, the gold and silver veins of Darmallon ran so deep they made the entire city sparkle; the glittering city of Darmallon is located somewhere beneath western Thedas
Felhammar
– the thaig fell during the First Blight
Gundaar
– the capital of the Kingdom of Gundaar, it fell to the darkspawn within a decade after -40 Ancient (1160 TE), around the same time as the kingdom of Hormak;
shortly before its fall, the thaig was ruled by
King Jegrek
, who was slain by the
warrior
Beregrand
in a
Proving
match—an action which considerably weakened the kingdom; it is also known that the Deep Roads directly connected it with Aeducan Thaig
Gwaren
– a dwarven outpost built near the Amaranthine Ocean in order for them to gain access to salt and the sea lanes; a flourishing
human settlement
was built above which collaborated with the dwarves; after the Deep Roads were overrun by darkspawn, the outpost was abandoned and sealed, however its name is still used by the human town
Heidrun
– built before the First Blight, this thaig was named after
Paragon Heidrun
;
it served as an important
lyrium
mining source for the dwarven empire;
in 9:41 Dragon
the Inquisitor
may
visit
Heidrun's ruins
Hormak
– former capital of the Kingdom of Hormak, it fell to the darkspawn in -35 Ancient (1165 TE); the legendary
Paragon Egon Winterbreath
is known to have led the thaig's defenses until its fall
Kal Repartha
– a thaig on the surface not recorded in the
Memories
; it was founded by
Paragon Fairel
in the
Hissing Wastes
after a disastrous conflict between two thaigs; old writings reveal that the thaig was founded before 700 TE (-495 Ancient, a century before the First Blight); by 9:41 Dragon, only half-buried ruins remain of the thaig
Kal'Barosh
– a thaig located south of
Kal'Hirol
; it fell shortly before Kal'Hirol was overrun by the darkspawn horde
Kobaliman
– before the events of the
Fifth Blight
, an expedition was organized to recover the thaig, but only one survivor, Captain
Roshen
, returned
Kul-Baras
– a dwarven fortress located beneath Ferelden; it was used by
the Architect
as its base of operations at 9:10 Dragon; a
Grey Warden
expedition some years later found it abandoned
Ortan
– the ancestral home of
House Ortan
, located beneath Ferelden
Revann
– in 5:10
Exalted
, the explorer
Faruma Helmi
found a strange scrawling by unknown hands on a wall there, mentioning the
profane
, indicating that such creatures lingered in the thaig
Valammar
– in
ancient
times the dwarves used Valammar as a trading post until it fell to the
darkspawn
horde;
the former trading post can be directly accessed from the surface in the
Hinterlands
; in 9:41 Dragon, it was used by the
Carta
as a base to smuggle
lyrium
Valdasine
– an ancient thaig, located even below the level of the Deep Roads; it was once the home of the
mining
House Valdasine;
in present day it is known as the
Primeval Thaig
Varen
– a thaig located south of
Kal'Hirol
; it fell shortly before Kal'Hirol was overrun by the darkspawn horde
Zygmunt
–
Shaper
Czibor
can directly trace his lineage to House Zygmunt from this thaig; the thaig itself was lost thirteen generations prior to the events of the Fifth Blight
Thaigs of unknown status
In the present day, the status of these thaigs is not known:
Rousten
– the ancestral home of House Rousten; during the events of the Fifth Blight it is likely uninhabited and used only for mining activities
The following information is only mentioned in Dragon Age Tabletop. Certain portions of this media may no longer reflect currently established lore.
Sharokovar
– the
Assembly
of
Kal-Sharok
meets in this thaig

The Blooming Rose
is a brothel located in
Hightown
, in the Red Lantern District. According to Madam
Lusine
, they provide premium service for 2 sovereigns, standard service for 1 sovereign, and for 50 silver, "someone may touch you. Briefly."

Services
Upon giving the Madam the requested amount of gold, she instructs
Hawke
to browse through the "companions" in the lounge:
For the cheap service (50
), a selection between an
elven
female, elven male, or
human
male is available.
The standard service (1
) allows a choice between an elf woman, a human man and a
dwarven
man.
For the premium service (2
), there is one elven woman, one elven
drag queen
, and one human man.
Note that Madam Lusine cannot be interacted with while Jethann is present for The First Sacrifice.
Each companion delivers a different one-liner about Hawke being a "hardened adventurer" or "carrying quite the sword," after which the screen cuts to a view of Hawke fastening their pants. The party members enter the room and control is returned.
After Hawke has made use of their services once, they may be randomly given an additional option.
Hawke may get the option of "something surprising," which he says, "Give me something that will blow my mind!" The screen fades out, and when it comes back Hawke and their companions are standing in front of a fully clothed man who finishes his sentence with, "...and he was dead the entire time!" or another sentence similarly detailing the end of a major motion picture. After a pause, Hawke states, "That was definitely not what I was expecting," or, "I'd better not have paid for this."
Depending on gender, Hawke may request the "bad girl/boy special," where Hawke asks the Madam for "something special just for girls/boys who have been bad," to which the Madam claims she has just the thing. After the fade out, Hawke and their companions appear in front of a female
Chantry
priest who speaks of the forgiveness of sins. After the sermon is over, a Hawke may say, "That did not just happen," or "That was definitely not what I was expecting."
Hawke may also be given the option to ask for "something exotic," in which case the screen fades out, and they later appear in the corner of a back room with a line of traps in front of them. Hawke says, "Next time, I should be a little more specific."
Companions' reactions
When Hawke speaks to Madam Lusine in Act 1, she assumes they do not have the money and will state the payment of their services in a blunt manner. If
Merrill
's romance has been initiated and she is in the group, Merrill will naively ask what kind of "payment" they were talking about. Merrill will admit that she wasn't paying any attention because she was trying not to touch anything. If Hawke then pays for any of the services of the brothel, they will get
Merrill: rivalry (
+5
).
In Act 1, if
Anders
is in the group when Hawke speaks to Lusine and his romance has been initiated, he will comment negatively on the possibility of a "treatment," stating that Hawke "cannot be that desperate," and that half of the clients of the brothel are also clients of his clinic. If Hawke then pays for any of the services of the brothel, they will get
Anders: rivalry (
+5
).
If
Fenris
is in the group he will comment, "disgusting," before Lusine states the payments of the services.
Notable items
Design: Rune of Lightning
- in a Chest during Act 1.
Codex entries
The Anderfels
(Act 1)
Notes
No quests take place in the Blooming Rose during Act 2, but the brothel is still accessible and will have several chests containing random loot.
Trivia
There are no human female prostitutes available to Hawke in either cheap, standard or premium service.
Corff
the Bartender mentions that the joint owner of the brothel,
Harlan
, wishes to rename the Rose to "Harlan's Harlots."
Sabina's Brat says that he wants to join the Coterie when he grows up.
Dialogue between Sabina and Katriela, overheard when Hawke first enters the brothel in Act 2, reveals that Sabina's brat robbed Katriela and was arrested.
The icon of the blooming rose resembles female genitalia, and the name is also a reference to the genitalia being open in the manner a rose would bloom.
Bugs
xbox360
At one of the "services," if you speak to Sabina, who is standing at the bar, there is the sound of her voice: "so, it shall be me, hm?" but the screen shows an empty wall. After that line nothing happens, but you still stand in the foyer and three of the prostitutes have the markers on their head. If you try to speak to another, Sabina speaks without being chosen. Changing the "service" solves nothing: Now six prostitutes have markers on their head and you can't talk with anyone.
ps3
xbox360
pc
In Act 2, at the table to the right of the stairs that lead to the upper rooms of the establishment, there's a mug that displays the door symbol when highlighted that can be interacted with. However, upon clicking on it, nothing happens and the option disappears.
pc
Sometimes after clicking on the mug, it can still be "opened" and "closed" like a door.

======================================================================
PAGE: The Bone Pit
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age II locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/The_Bone_Pit
======================================================================

For the quest of the same name, see
The Bone Pit (quest)
.
Location
The Bone Pit
Type
Quarry
Cavern System
Terrain
Strip-Mine
Underground
Location
Free Marches
Sections
Bone Pit Mines
Rotting Cave
Drakestone Mines
Inhabitants
Dragons
Enemies
Looters
Exits
World map
Appearances
Dragon Age II
The Bone Pit
is a large quarry located outside of
Kirkwall
. It consists of several mazelike caverns and abandoned mines.
Background
When the
region
was under the occupation of the
Tevinter Imperium
, the area was originally known as
The Maharian Quarry
. During this time, slave labor was used abundantly, as in most of the Imperium, to mine within. One noteworthy
Overseer
of the quarry had slaves fed to his dragonlings, presumably as a punishment. Some worried that the deaths in the mine had thinned
the Veil
. It was at this time that it acquired the name "Bone Pit."
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age II
.
In Act 1,
Hawke
receives a letter from
Hubert
, an
Orlesian
Hightown merchant. Hubert offers Hawke a reward for finding why production in the quarry has stopped. Hawke finds that the quarry has been overrun by
dragons
, released from a cavern during mining, who have slaughtered many of the workers. Hawke clears out the mine and returns to Hubert, who offers Hawke a partnership in the quarry as a reward. If Hawke accepts, they convince the miners to return to work.
During Act 2, Hubert will mention that there is a pest infestation at The Bone Pit and ask Hawke to investigate. When they travel to the Bone Pit, Hawke will find
Jansen
, the miner rescued during Act 1. He alerts Hawke to troubles in the Bone Pit, which Hawke can resolve to improve production of the mines.
In Act 3, Hawke returns to find the Bone Pit scorched, the workers slaughtered by a
high dragon
. Upon slaying the dragon and returning to Hubert, Hubert declares he is tired of dealing with the quarry, at which point Hawke can ask for it as payment.
Places

The Bone Pit
Notes
After completing one of Jansen's secondary quests, you have to leave the area and come back to get the next quest.
The outside of the pit have a foreshadowing of the High Dragon's presence that can be heard from Act 1. You can heard growls, screams and squeals that looks like Flemeth's growl (when saving the Hawke and Valen families in the
Blighlands
), her roar when taking flight in
Sundermount
and the squeals looks like
dragonlings
' cries. The sound (which is the theme of the place) is also there during the act 2, but disappears once the high dragon is slain.

The Crater
is a
random encounter
from the
Warden's Keep
downloadable content
.
Background
While traveling,
the Warden
encounters a couple named Jon and Marta. The following conversation is going on.
"How did a child survive that? The crater is still smoking."
"It's a boy—five fingers, five toes, that's all that matters to me. The Maker has answered our prayers!"
"Let's go home, Marta, and raise the tyke as our own."

Marta

;

is able to forge the Starfang
longsword
or
greatsword
from this material.

While not limited to any particular location on the world map, there is some indication that it may be encountered more frequently when traveling to/from
Honnleath
on the
World map
.
Players may have this encounter even without the DLC installed.

======================================================================
PAGE: The Crossroads
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Inquisition locations, Dragon Age: The Veilguard locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/The_Crossroads
======================================================================

For the village in the
Hinterlands
, see
The Crossroads (Hinterlands)
.
Location
The Crossroads
Type
Eluvian Nexus
Terrain
Elven Architecture
Exits
Winter Palace
Elven Mountain Ruins
Deep Roads
Shattered Library
The Darvaarad
Abandoned Chateau
Hidden Hollow
Forgotten Tower
Ancient Jail
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Masked Empire
Dragon Age: Inquisition
Trespasser
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
The Crossroads
are a separate dimension beyond the
waking world
and beneath
the Fade
where all
Eluvians
, magical elven mirrors, join,
although not necessarily directly.
Background
“It's not
the Fade
. The runes are
elven
. If I had to guess, I would say that our ancestors actually
created
some sort of tiny world between the
Eluvians
.”
―
Felassan
The Crossroads are described as one of the "places between," pockets within the Fade with their own rules of reality formed by
ancient elves
from the fabric of time and space. Its true name was lost to time, and
Morrigan
coined its current designation. The Crossroads were the means by which ancient elves traveled.
Some of the Eluvians in the Crossroads were left unlocked,
but most were sealed by ancient elves during the infighting to prevent sudden attacks.
Currently, the place is deteriorating, and eventually it will collapse on itself.
Modern elves experience the Crossroads differently from other races: the colours are brighter and the trees are in bloom.
Elves find the place welcoming while
humans
suffer physical discomfort from the twisting light and the insistent noise.
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
During
Solas
' rebellion against the
Evanuris
, Solas would grant the slaves he liberated sanctuary in the Crossroads.
The Crossroads have evolved since the creation of the
Veil
. Once, it was fully controllable by the ancient elves' and now the Crossroads changes itself independently. The Crossroads forms its own islands that reflect the real world based on the information collected by the eluvians, the influence of mortals' memories, and also by how the Crossroads choose to show its interpretation of such reflections.
Involvement
Witch Hunt
This section contains spoilers for
:
Witch Hunt
.
In the aftermath of the
Fifth Blight
,
Morrigan
(and possibly
Kieran
and
the Warden
) escape through the
Eluvian
she has restored and the Crossroads to evade potential pursuit by
Flemeth
.
Dragon Age: The Masked Empire
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: The Masked Empire
.
During the
War of the Lions
, two groups travel through the Crossroads.
Celene
and her companions (
Briala
,
Felassan
and
Michel
) have the keystone from
Imshael
and are attempting to return to
Val Royeaux
, while
Gaspard
and his companions (
Mihris
,
Remache
and
Lienne
) are pursuing them. The groups eventually enter a truce. They defeat a
varterral
in the central chamber together. As previously agreed, Celene's champion and Gaspard then duel for the fate of
Orlais
. While they are at it, Briala steals the keystone and claims the Eluvian network for herself.
Dragon Age: Inquisition
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
Morrigan
brings
the Inquisitor
to the Crossroads through her Eluvian in
Skyhold
. She explains that
Corypheus
seeks a similar mirror in an
elven temple
within the
Arbor Wilds
to enter this place. Although the Crossroads are not actually the Fade, they are very close to it. Someone with enough power could tear down the ancient barriers and walk the
Fade
in the flesh. Morrigan then insists on
stopping
Corypheus together.
Trespasser
This section contains spoilers for
:
Trespasser
.
An orb that raises paths
In 9:44 Dragon, the
Qunari
have gained control over a part of the Eluvian network, and are using the Crossroads to facilitate their invasion of Southern Thedas. They stumbled across it independently.
After finding a dead Qunari in the
Winter Palace
during the
Exalted Council
, the Inquisitor follows the blood trail and discovers an active Eluvian. The Crossroads appear less hazy than two years ago and are made of islands floating, with no bottom in sight.
Mysterious red items allow to recreate paths between the ledges and gain access to more Eluvians as the story progresses.
After the defeat of the Qunari plot, is it revealed that
Briala
has lost control of her Eluvian network, and that the Crossroads are now under sole control of
Fen'Harel
.
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
The
eluvian
within the
Lighthouse
is called the
Vi'Revas
, which means "Freedom of ways." The Vi'Revas opens a portal to The Crossroads, which is a pocket dimension in the
Fade
that leads to other entrances of other eluvians. It is said that The Crossroads is a place where all eluvians meet, and so the
Dreadwolf
had used the Vi'Revas to access any eluvian of his choosing. The
Caretaker
is the steward of The Crossroads, and it seems willing to aid and transport the wielder of
Solas' lyrium dagger
. The Caretaker claims that The Crossroads is both alike and different than The Crossroads that the
Inquisition
traveled through in their search for
Solas
. It also revealed that the
Elven pantheon
vied for control of The Crossroads in the past. The
Veilguard
uses the Vi'Revas to travel to other parts of Thedas.
The Caretaker warns them that
Elgar'nan
and
Ghilan'nain
are trying to spread their influence over the Crossroads, using the
Blight
and their
Venatori
and
Antaam
minions to spread death and madness throughout the area. The Caretaker is unable to protect the party from the Crossroads' danger but they can ferry them to other islands in the Crossroads.
Rook
calls the portion of the Crossroads that the Veilguard interacts with the Dread Wolf's Crossroads.
The Dread Wolf's Crossroads has some of Solas' memories that are still re-enacted by the Fade
and some of the spirits are influenced by his regrets.
The Veilguard can experience these memories, which can be experienced and felt to be real despite it being a re-enactment of a memory, and fight these regrets through various
quests
.
When the Caretaker transports the Veilguard to the Converged City, they inform the party that the local
spirits
were driven out by the elven gods' minions and are now refugees. The Caretaker has summoned the
guardians
of the Crossroads to protect them, but warns Rook's team that the guardians repel all interlopers—including them. After defeating the threats, the Caretaker reveals that Elgar'nan and Ghilan'nain have used
blighted
roots and
blood magic
to block off eluvians in the Crossroads. In order to pass the barriers, the Veilguard must defeat the gods' champions—powerful
Ogre
entities roaming both the Crossroads and mortal world—to obtain their essence and break the seals.
As the Veilguard progress in overcoming the invading forces of the elven gods', the Caretaker begins to regain control of the Crossroads and starts repairing its infrastructure.
Allies
of the Veilguard also begin visiting the Converged City and interacting with the local spirits.
The final obstacle faced by the Veilguard is a blighted
dragon
sealed in the Crossroads by the gods to further spread their corruption. In order to open the entrance to its lair, the Veilguard must defeat three powerful
Revenants
created by Elgar'nan and Ghilan'nain from Solas' worst regrets. After unlocking all seals, the Veilguard can slay the blighted
Revenant Dragon
. With the dragon defeated, the Blight infecting the Crossroads is staunched, thwarting the elven gods' ambitions of conquest.

The Crossroads
during
Trespasser

Eluvian
other than the one leading to the
Winter Palace

Dragon Age: Inquisition
The Crossroads
is a small village in the
Hinterlands
containing four houses.
Background
The village has always been a peaceful place up until the recent conflict between the
Mages
and
Templars
. The Crossroads has become a place of refuge for those injured from the
Mage-Templar War
. Most of the refugees are need of treatment for their wounds and have come seeking the assistance
Mother Giselle
.
Involvement
They're telling everyone to leave until the mages and templars stop burning everything down.
—
a letter found in an empty home
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
It breaks my heart to see this war cross the
Maker
's land, Giselle. I would that you were here with me at the
Temple of Sacred Ashes
. Your words have calmed many irate minds over the years, and we will not lack for those at the Conclave. Still, I am glad that the refugees have you to care for them. Too many in the halls of power have forgotten our faithful. If any pressure you to accommodate their views, you must let me know at once. Maker willing, this war shall all be over soon, Giselle. Be well and go with my blessing.
—In service of the Maker,
Divine Justinia V
The Herald
can assist the refugees at the Crossroads by speaking with
Corporal Vale
, whom is currently stationed at the Crossroads overlooking
Inquisition
troop training. He will provide the Herald with a list of tasks to perform that will ease the suffering of the refugees. After completing all of the tasks Corporal Vale can be recruited as an
agent
for
Cullen
.

This section contains spoilers for:

underground cove
which is the smugglers' hideout. The Crown and Lion is also the last place the

was seen after leaving

the night before the
darkspawn assault

.
Sorcha
– a waitress; she can be asked to provide more information on Kristoff.

– their intoxicated discussions are about who's stronger between Andraste and Archdemon, about their health problems and about rioting against the "nosy guards".

PAGE: The Darvaarad
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Inquisition locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/The_Darvaarad
======================================================================

Location
The Darvaarad
Type
Keep
Terrain
Island Fortress
Inhabitants
Qunari
Enemies
Ben-Hassrath
Exits
The Crossroads
Elven Ruins
Appearances
Dragon Age: Inquisition
-
Trespasser
The
Darvaarad
is a
Qunari
occupied island fortress controlled by the
Ben-Hassrath
.
Background
This section contains spoilers for:
Trespasser
.
"Darvaarad" is the
Qunari
term for a magical quarantine site. They are managed by the branch of
Ben-Hassrath
called "Dangerous Purpose" who are lead by
Viddasala
.
One of such sites was used in the conspiracy called "Dragon's Breath". The
eluvian
leading to it from
the Crossroads
was protected with a keystone and a password: "maraas nehraa".
The Qunari in the Darvaarad both investigated inner workings of the eluvians
and brought in, cataloged
and studied various artifacts, including
red lyrium
,
astrariums
,
ocularum
, ancient elven statues and murals.
They stockpiled knowledge and power, which allowed them to open so many mirrors.
Saarath
was one of the Qunari involved in the research.
The site also served as a base for
elven
and
human
spies
and a gaatlock factory. The Qunari contained a
dragon
they called
ataashi
in the Darvaarad's biggest chamber and used its venom to create the explosives quickly and in large quantities.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Trespasser
.
The Inquisitor
and their companions enter the Darvaarad through the eluvian in
the Crossroads
. As they make their way forward under heavy resistance, the Inquisitor inspects evidence of
Qunari
activities within the "Dragon's Breath" plan. In the Research Tower the Inquisitor finds elven artifacts, including a puzzle required for the "
Lateral Thinker
" achievement and a self-portrait by
Fen'Harel
.
Inside the gaatlock factory the Inquisitor sees the
ataashi
through an arch and realizes its purpose.
Viddasala
arrives to give orders to her forces. She then tells
Iron Bull
to attack the Inquisitor. If
his Chargers
were sacrificed, or the Inquisitor chose not to follow up on the
Demands of the Qun
at all, he cannot be swayed from the
Qun
even if
romanced
and fights to his death. Otherwise he readily refuses to follow the order.
As the Inquisitor reaches the last chamber, they can either kill or release the dragon, thus foiling the Qunari plan. Viddasala and her men prepare to flee via an eluvian when the Inquisitor's hand starts acting up again. Taking pity on them, she turns to provide some revelations:
Solas
is the agent of
Fen'Harel
, he gave the orb to
Corypheus
and pushed a dying Qunari into the
Winter Palace
for the
Inquisition
to find. Viddasala promises to kill Solas and disappears in the mirror.

The Dungeons
beneath
Chateau Haine
serve as a convenient place for

and the dungeon passages were built by the

to help the citizens of the

escape the encroaching
darkspawn
. A crack in the wall within the dungeons leads through a

and

The Final Seal
is the fifth and highest floor of the
Grey Warden
prison
constructed for
Corypheus
. It is a single, round room with four balconies each with an individual seal placed on each. Corypheus' sarcophagus is located in the center of the chamber. It can be accessed from either
Daneken's Floor
or
Riannon's Floor
.

One of the
companions
may make remarks at certain spots:

Morrigan

Leliana

Sten
: "I mislike this."
Wynne
: "Do you feel that? This place is practically infused with magic."
Zevran

Oghren
: "Something about this place makes my skin prickle."
Shale
: "Oh, this should be good."
Upon entering the room with the puzzle bridge
Alistair
: "Uh-oh. I'm terrible at puzzles."
Morrigan
: "Another obstacle? 'Tis almost beyond endurance."
Leliana
: "Oh, this looks fun! I bet we'll have to work together and join hands and sing a happy song to get across!"
Sten
: "Simple enough."
Wynne
: "Well, looks like there's only one way forward."
Zevran

Oghren
: "I bet that thing's going to collapse the second we're all standing on it."
Shale

Upon entering the chamber with the Urn of Sacred Ashes
Alistair

Morrigan

Leliana

Sten

Wynne

Zevran

Oghren

Shale

======================================================================
PAGE: The Hall of Valor
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/The_Hall_of_Valor
======================================================================

Location
The Hall of Valor
Location
Western
Rivain
Inhabitants
Lords of Fortune
Spirits
Factions
Lords of Fortune
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
The Hall of Valor
is the headquarters and fighting arena of the
Lords of Fortune
in
Rivain
.
Background
The Hall of Valor is a training area in
Rivain
run by the
Lords of Fortune
and friendly
Spirits
of Valor; it offers fighters the chance to test their prowess and win bragging rights.
The Hall of Valor has a fighting pit
where fighters test their mettle against various free-willed competitors or spirits
, and spectators can witness gladiatorial combat. The Hall of Valor also includes the Hilt, the bar and grill that the Lords of Fortune use as their base of operations.
Involvement
After completing
The Dragon Slayer
recruitment quest, speak to
Taash
at the
Lighthouse
to unlock the Hall of Valor.
Isabela
will be found on the upper level near the fast-travel beacon and will offer arena fights to
Rook
and the team. The Hilt, a bar and grill and bar
, can be found on the upper level near Isabela, along with
their menu
, though no food items can actually be purchased.
The
Lords of Fortune
faction merchant
Mateo
can be found on the bottom level, and the Hall of Valor overworld map icon will display the Lords of Fortune faction strength.
Places
A map of the zones in the Hall of Valor.
Hall Entryway
- A small entry hall to the Hall of Valor. Two breaks in the left wall can be climbed through for minor loot.
Lower Concourse
- A large area on the ground level of the Hall. The Lords of Fortune faction merchant,
Mateo
, can be found at the base of the stairs.
Rowan
, the Rivaini seer, stands on the east side, discussing spirits and possession with a Fereldan Lord of Fortune. Taking the long path through the water on the left leads to a small cave with a dead body speared on a
Dragon's Teeth
spike from the series
Mass Effect
. In addition, the body on the spike has an LED light pattern consistent with
Commander Shepard
's armor.
The Hilt - Lower Balcony
- A small balcony overlooking the battlefield. The spirit Pluck can be found here, cheering on a struggling warrior who is depressed that his equipment and training are not optimized for ideal success.
The Hilt - Upper Balcony
- The upper level of the map which holds the fast-travel beacon and the restaurant The Hilt. Isabela stands here beside the beacon.
The back room of the Hall of Valor.
The back room of the Hall of Valor - an alternate view.
Back Room
- Only accessible during certain post-quest cinematic conversations, usually with
Isabela
or
Taash
. The table is covered in coins and beverages and surrounded by large armchairs, and a raised bed on the back wall holds a stuffed bear.
The Hilt - Archives and Storage
- A small area under the upper balcony. Two Lords of Fortune here will occasionally discuss the accidental destruction of relics during looting raids. The northernmost corridor in this region leads to a room used in several local quest cutscenes, but the room is not accessible outside of those quests.
Battlefield
- Only accessible during arena fights, which can be begun by speaking to Isabela.
Characters
Mateo
, faction merchant for the Lords of Fortune
Rowan
, Rivaini seer
Isabela
Pluck, a spirit
Quests
The Hall of Valor (quest)
Kataranda the Stormrider
Dragon Class
Horns and Halla at the Hilt
Enemies
The arena fights will randomly cycle through a number of enemy faction types. The first ten victories will count towards the
Hall of Valor (quest)
. See that page for quest rewards. Once the required ten victories are completed, Rook may continue to initiate fights for practice, but no further rewards or approval will be issued.
Antaam
Darkspawn
Deep Roads
creatures
Demons
Fade-Touched Creatures
Frenzied Dead
Mercenaries
Venatori
Notable items and chests
There is one chest available in the Hall of Valor. If looking at Isabela and the beacon, take the long staircase on Rook's right (on the south of the upper balcony) all the way down to ground level. Behind a group of Lords of Fortune, open a hidden bookcase door to reveal a ladder up to a chest containing the heavy armor
Casings of Fortune
.
Codex entries

Notes
Isabela's introductions to the arena fights include references to arenas in other Bioware properties.
"We got the idea for this next match from a traveler named Armax. Said he used it to challenge shepherds." This is a direct reference to the
Armax Arsenal Arena
from
Mass Effect 3
's
Citadel DLC
, where
Shepard
can take on foes they have faced throughout the trilogy.
"They've got the reach, let's see if you have the flexibility!" This is a reference to a story
Garrus
tells Shepard about a sparring match he was involved in aboard a turian ship that ended in a tie-breaker in her quarters.
"Last time the Hall of Valor was this excited, we got the Silver Phoenix fight. You're not gonna want to miss what comes next!" This is a reference to
Jade Empire
; "Silver Phoenix" is an alias the player can adopt while fighting in the
Imperial Arena
.
Trivia
Each companion has a unique introduction to the Hall of Valor read aloud by
Isabela
the first time they enter, as follows:
Bellara
-
Our next challenger comes from the Veil Jumpers of Arlathan. She's deadly and Dalish, so everyone give a halla for Bellara!
Davrin
-
In war, victory! In peace, vigilance! In the Hall of Valor, give it up for renowned Grey Warden and monster slayer Davrin!
Emmrich
-
The spirits of the Hall of Valor are honored to welcome Emmrich "Vol-carnage" Volkarin of the Mourn Watch!
Harding
-
She's the tiny Titan who puts the feral in Ferelden! Please welcome Lace Harding!
Lucanis
(and Spite) -
The Hall of Valor is pleased to welcome legendary Spirit of Determination, Spite! And his spirit-friend, Lucanis Dellamorte of the Crows.
Neve
-
Is that a chill in the air? Please give an ice-cold welcome to the darling of Dock Town, the Winter from Tevinter, Neve Gallus!
Taash
-
The Hall of Valor couldn't make a dragon... so we're bringing our own! Lords of Fortune, let's hear it for our hometown hero, Taash!
In addition, a shark made entirely of 51 cheese wheels can be seen hanging beneath the battlefield. It is only viewable with freecam mods.

merrinla
.

merrinla
.

“The Hanged Man - sometimes you want to go where everyone knows your name.”
―Isabela
Location
The Hanged Man
Type
Tavern
Location
Lowtown
,
Kirkwall
Inhabitants
Humans
Dwarves
Exits
Lowtown
Appearances
Dragon Age II
Legacy
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: Inquisition
(mentioned)
Trespasser
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: Knight Errant
Dragon Age: Hard in Hightown
Dragon Age: Absolution
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: The Missing
The Hanged Man
is a
tavern
located in
Lowtown
, in the lower parts of
Kirkwall
north of the
docks
. It serves as the home base of both
Varric Tethras
and
Isabela
.
Background
The Hanged Man is a major tavern in Kirkwall and the only one accessible to
Hawke
; it is a popular destination for all people, strangers or residents. The interior consists of one main room and four bedrooms, with an opulent suite currently occupied by Varric. The inhabitants of the tavern will vary, depending on what quests are active and progress through the main plot. Varric seems very interested in owning the tavern.
The tavern got its name because people used to be hanged by their feet at the spot, as Varric explains to
Fenris
in a party dialogue.
Characters
A Friend
– appears at night
Alistair
– only appears if he was exiled from Ferelden and
Anora
placed on the throne in
Dragon Age: Origins
Corff
– a Bartender and peddler of rumors
Isabela
– when she is not in the group
Nella
– appears after Emile de Launcet escapes the Gallows
Maraas
– appears in Act 3
Martin
– former raider and friend of Isabela, involved in the quest
Finders Keepers
Norah
– the waitress
Patron/Drunk Patron
Ser
Roderick
Suspicious Lady
– initiates the side quest
Secret Rendezvous
Talkative man
– a character who breaks the fourth wall
Varric Tethras
– appears if he is not in the active party

Trivia
Drinks can be ordered here for a low price of 3 bronze coins per drink. However, it is unknown as to what beverage Hawke actually drinks.
If you order 30 (doesn't have to be in same visit) drinks at the bar, whenever you enter the bar after that, the patrons will all shout "HAWKE!" This is a reference to the television series
Cheers
, in which all the patrons would shout "NORM!" whenever he would enter the pub.
Additionally, Isabela comments, when passing the tavern, that "sometimes you want to go where everybody knows your name." This is a reference to
Cheers's
theme song.
Varric also likes the Hanged Man, and comments that "everybody here knows your name".
Fenris comments that the tavern smells of sour ale, vomit, and desperation.
The tarot card "The Hanged Man" is often depicted in a motif similar to the tavern's signage in front of and behind the building, as well as the loading screen fresco.
When talking to the patron closest to the exit, he will mention that Varric told a story of you escaping the
Blight
on the back of a giant turtle. This is a reference to
Pirates of the Caribbean
movies, whenever a character escapes off-screen such as The Guard Dog, Jack, Will and others will say it was "Sea Turtles."
On the wall next to where
Isabela
stands a hanging picture can be seen of concept art for
Oghren
.
According to a loading screen, the tavern's feature dish is stew, made from a different mystery meat every morning. A recipe for the - apparently famous - dish "The Hanged Man's Mystery Meat Stew" is given in
Dragon Age: The Official Cookbook: Tastes of Thedas
. The narrator mentions that they suspect the stew they were served was pork, but they decided not to inquire further on hearing the waitress claim customers who ask get hung from the ceiling.
The Pig Oat Mash, also called the Hanged Mash, is always served there and is allegedly a hangover cure when accompanied by brandy-spiked-cider.
One of the rumors the bartender gives describes a massive drop in the pigeon population in Ferelden. This is a reference to
Shale
.
Although Hawke can order drinks at the bar, it is not possible for them to rent a room here. Varric's room also cannot be used for that purpose.
The music that plays here at night is slightly different from the music that plays during the day.
Bugs
pc
There is a graphics bug which causes the party to be superpositioned over each other when they first enter the bar (see
gallery
). Moving the characters forward independently (using "hold position" for the others) fixes it.

The Howe Road

Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening
The Howe Road
is the location of a
scripted encounter
in
Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening
. The encounter will only trigger if
the Warden-Commander
released
Nathaniel Howe
during
the Prisoner
questline.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening
.
Nathaniel stops the party on the road and all but begs the Warden-Commander to recruit him into the
Grey Wardens
. He will even argue that maybe restoring honor to his family name is not what is important, but doing his part to help fight the darkspawn is. Ultimately, his fate remains in the Warden-Commander's hands. He can be recruited, told to leave and not come back, or the Commander can elect to kill him. The other companions' approval will change:
If he is recruited:
Anders disapproves (
-3
)
Oghren disapproves (
-6
)
Justice disapproves (
-3
)
If he is told to leave and not come back:
Oghren disapproves (
-3
)
If he is killed:
Oghren disapproves (
-3
)
There are no changes for
Velanna
and
Sigrun
.
Should Nathaniel be put through
the Joining
, he will survive. Choosing to kill him will make Nathaniel hostile. If he was previously allowed to take his family relics, he will not drop the items (the ones found in the chest marked "Prisoner's Effects" in the
dungeon
). If Oghren is present he will comment on the similarity of how
Zevran
requested to join the Warden's party.
Quests
The Prisoner

======================================================================
PAGE: The Lady's Rest
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Inquisition locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/The_Lady's_Rest
======================================================================

Location
The Lady's Rest
Type
Coastal Island
Terrain
Coastline
Location
Frostback Basin
Inhabitants
Spirits
Demons
Quests
What Yet Lingers
Appearances
Dragon Age: Inquisition
-
Jaws of Hakkon
The Lady's Rest
is an island located off of the southeastern coast of the
Frostback Basin
.
Background
The island is home to many spirits which the
Avvar
avoid. However, Professor
Bram Kenric
believes the island holds clues of the whereabouts to the last resting place of Inquisitor
Ameridan
.
The Inquisitor
learns that
Telana
, Ameridan's lover, traveled there to
dream
in order to search for Ameridan after he disappeared. Telana, however, never left the island; a spirit which she attracted waits there to be discovered.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
In order to get to the island, the Inquisitor must use the boat belonging to fisherman
Arvid Rolfsen
. He will inform them that the island belongs to the
Lady of the Skies
and that the Avvar avoid it at all costs. He says he can only allow the use of the boat as long as the Inquisitor obtains permission from Thane
Svarah Sun-Hair
. After heading back to
Stone-Bear Hold
and speaking to Svarah Sun-Hair, she will grant the Inquisitor "Guest" status, which allows for the use of the boat.
In the tent near the dock is a chest containing a
letter
; it reads:
Letter Found near Many Corpses
The Barbarians are afraid to go to this island. I can see why, with all the spirits, but it's completely safe. I was out there myself for a good half hour, and the spirits never bothered me.
Steal one of the Avvar boats and set up a camp. This route will bypass all the taxes, and with Avvar and spirits all around, nobody in their right mnd will track us here.
I know how you feel about demons, but trust me. This is completely safe. Peaceful, even. All you have to do is relax on that island and wait for goods to come your way.
It's completely safe.
Also found on the island is
the Stone Giants
landmark
, which unlocks the
Tale of Hryngnar, Ice-Troll
, and further inland in the destroyed shack, the
Unfallen Shack
.
Slightly northeast of the landmark, below the cliffs at sea level, lies a small unmarked cave. Inside the cave is a skeleton sitting on a throne with a scroll on its lap and surrounded by large piles of treasure. Reading the scroll unlocks the
Ballad of the Murderer's Gold
. Looting the treasure nets significant amounts of coin but when disturbed will spawn four
elite
-ranked
despair demons
at level 30.
As the Inquisitor explores The Lady's Rest they can hear Telana speaking these words:
This blood... my blood? No, I can't...
Ameridan... Ameridan, why?
I can't... not without...
Vhenan... I'm... dreaming...
Sleep. I need to... I must find you...
No, no, no.
Enemies
Demons
Despair demon
Quests
What Yet Lingers
Collections
Landmarks
Landmarks in Frostback Basin
Codex entries
Tale of Hryngnar, Ice-Troll
Unfallen Shack
Gallery

======================================================================
PAGE: The Long Road (Origins)
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/The_Long_Road_(Origins)
======================================================================

Location
The Long Road
Type
Scripted encounter
Terrain
Clearing
Location
Ferelden
Enemies
Assassins
Exits
World map
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
(mentioned)
The Long Road
is a
scripted encounter
in
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Background
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
This encounter will only occur after the cutscene where Arl
Howe
and
Loghain
hire
Zevran Arainai
to kill
the Warden
—which occurs after completing one of the main questlines. When the party arrives, a woman approaches and asks for help; however, when the Warden follows her, it turns out to be an ambush. Zevran and several other assassins move into position, and the woman is revealed to be a mage.
Characters
Traveler
Zevran Arainai
Enemies
Assassins
Assassin (5) (Rogue, Ranged)
Traveler (2) (Warrior, Melee)
Traveler (2) (Rogue, Melee)
Traveler (Mage)
Zevran Arainai
(Rogue, Melee)
Enemy Skills
Cripple
Flurry
Shock
Paralyze
Lightning
Chain Lightning
Pinning Shot
Shattering Shot
Critical Shot
Mark of Death
- Zevran only.
Strategy
Try to take out the mage traveler first, to negate the great damage from her spells. There are numerous traps set up along the inclines, making it difficult to approach the crossbowmen on the hills. Ranged attacks can be helpful to take them out from the clearing.
Once all the enemies are defeated, the Warden will be able to interrogate Zevran, and have the option of recruiting him as a companion if they accept his offer. Otherwise, they can either kill him in the field or simply let him go.
Alternately, having a rogue take the path on the right will avoid the ambush cutscene. This in turn will allow the rogue to disarm the traps manually while hugging the extreme right of the path. If the rogue is able to successfully make their way to the map exit, it is possible to not encounter Zevran altogether.
Note:
If Zevran is recruited, all the traps in the area will disappear.
Approval
If the Warden decides to recruit Zevran, this triggers the following approval changes from companions:
Leliana approves (
+2
)
Morrigan disapproves (
-3
)
Alistair disapproves (
-5
) (he can be persuaded down to
-3
, but depending on your response it could get as high as
-10
if you choose the line about "picking up cast-offs")
Oghren disapproves (
-5
)
No changes for
Shale
,
Wynne
and
Sten
.
You can avoid approval changes with other party members, both positive and negative, by making the offer to let Zevran join you in return for his life (as opposed to letting him propose the same). To make this happen, follow this dialogue path: "I'm done asking questions" -> "You tried to kill me!" -> "Yes. Actually, I can." -> "Never mind that. I want you to serve me." -> "Do you want to live or not?" The party member selection screen will pop up without approval changes. Be careful to avoid conversation choices that lead to Zevran offering to serve you, since that cancels out this option.
Resources
Deathroot
Special objects
Leghold Trap
(4) - steel claw, medium difficulty, 40 XP granted per trap detected and disarmed.
Tripwire
(2) - 20 XP granted per tripwire detected and disarmed.
Codex entries
Friends of Red Jenny
Notes
This encounter is referenced in a conversation between
Lace Harding
and
Lucanis Delamorte
, in Dragon Age: The Veilguard, when discussing the Crows being hired to fight for the Ferelden Throne by Loghain, Lucanis responds by informing Harding that this encounter was the reason for the Crows no longer taking jobs in Ferelden, because "House Arainai embarrassed themselves so badly on that job, the Crows buried six different Eigths Talons".
Gallery
Map of the area

PAGE: The Low Road

For the scripted encounter, see

.
Location

Type

Bereskarn

Dragon Age: Origins
The Low Road
is a
random encounter
in
Dragon Age: Origins
that occurs on the
World map
.
Involvement
The Warden
and accompanying party are beset simultaneously by three
blight wolves
, a
bereskarn
and a
drake
.
Hostile creatures
Ghouls
Blight Wolf
(3) (
Normal
)
Bereskarn
(
Lieutenant
)
Dragon
Drake
(Lieutenant)
Note:
The drake does not appear in the PC version of the game.
Strategy
It's highly recommended that you attempt to paralyze the bereskarn while handling the wolves, it makes the fight much easier.
Don't move too far from the starting area and there will be a fallen tree to the left with the drake on the other side. It makes things a lot easier to just fight the drake over/through the tree. It won't be able to attack you but you should be able to hit it with magic/ranged weapons.
Resources
Deathroot
Elfroot
See also
Bloody Plains
Desolate Highway
Treacherous Path
Gallery
Map of the area

Services
One of the random surprises offered at the Pearl
The Warden can engage in sexual activity with males and females of different races for the fee of 40
or at the negotiated price of 30
.
Note:
Do not take Sanga up on her offer if you have started the
Precious Metals
quest and have the
Smuggled Lyrium
in your inventory. Everything in your inventory is removed during this activity and this is treated as if you sold the Lyrium to someone besides Godwin (even though you immediately get your entire inventory back and can still sell the Lyrium to Godwin). Rogek will act as though you failed to deliver the Lyrium and attack you.
One of the options available at the Pearl is "Surprise Me." The result of this decision can lead to the following humorous scenarios upon waking up:
The Warden wakes with their hands tied behind their back and two tools lying on the bed labeled "Insertion Tool" and "Extraction Tool."
The Warden wakes with two nugs who, when clicked on, flop to the ground lounging and produce the message, "The nugs avoid eye contact, trying to look busy." The reference in
Witch Hunt
with the gossiping mages regarding the Warden could be pulled from this random surprise.
The Warden wakes up in a room filled with flower pots, scarecrows, a dragon egg, some bottles of unknown fluid, a whip, a trellis or monkey bars, and a ring of practice dummies with a wooden fish statue in the middle. The Warden paces looking paranoid or utterly confused.
The Warden wakes up staring a male dwarf dressed in women's clothing who states, "Yes, that was amazing. The world moved for everyone," before crossing his arms. This would seem to be the person that Isabela was referring to in Dragon Age 2, when discussing her sexual experience said that she even tried "a dwarf in drag once" but that she didn't recommend it.
The Warden wakes up staring at the floor and wobbling a bit. By the bed is a two handed maul labeled as "Magic Wand" which produces the message, "You're not sure if you want to remember what happened last night."
Note:
If the Warden is female and chooses a female, and Oghren is in the party, he will "accidentally" enter the room when the Warden is with the female.
Companion reactions
When Sanga asks what you want, if you ask for men, women, or some of both:
If the Warden is male then
Oghren approves (
+2
) if the Warden asks for men, or
+3
if the Warden asks for women or some of both.
If the Warden is female then
Oghren approves (
+3
) if the Warden asks for men or some of both, or
+4
if the Warden asks for women.
Engaging in any sexual activity with the workers at the Pearl will result in approval changes (only once):
Alistair disapproves (
-1
), unless he is hardened
Leliana disapproves (
-1
), unless she is hardened
Oghren approves (
+1
), or
+3
if the Warden is female and selects one of the female workers.
Morrigan disapproves (
-10
) if you are in romance with her.
Note:
The
ps3
version doesn't always assign these approval changes. Possibly this is caused by a bug.

is a section of
the Fade
visited by
the Warden

Involvement
This section of the fade is an exposed island lying on the periphery of the
Sloth Demon
's domain, which is a realm controlled by one of the lesser demons under Sloth's power—as part of the protective ward around the
Inner Sanctum
. After shattering the illusion of
Weisshaupt
fortress, the
Grey Warden
headquarters in the
Anderfels
, the Warden escapes here where they encounter
Niall
, who reveals the how the Sloth Demon's domain functions.
The Warden will then encounter a mage apprentice in the form of a Mouse who is being help captive by a
rage demon
. After releasing him from the
demon
he grants the Warden his
mouse
shape shifting ability. The Warden will require this talent to travel through mouse holes encountered. The mouse form also features stealth, but the form cannot be used in combat.

“I was dining alone at the local inn, which is quaintly perched on the top of a dam, when I overheard the barman mention tunnels beneath the village. I was surprised to learn that a vast cave system riddles the land surrounding
Crestwood

Background
The Spoiled Princess sign
According to the innkeeper, the inn is named after his sister, who was their father's "little princess." She moved to
Denerim
because she wanted to live somewhere "more glamorous," but she was eventually robbed and murdered. Her father passed away in despair, her mother stopped eating, and the innkeeper inherited the inn and kept the name.

======================================================================
PAGE: The Vaults
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age II locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/The_Vaults
======================================================================

Location
Chateau Haine Vaults
Type
Repository
Terrain
Chateau Interior
Understructure
Location
Chateau Haine
,
Vimmark Mountains
Exits
The Dungeons
Appearances
Dragon Age II
-
Mark of the Assassin
The Vaults
beneath
Chateau Haine
are an extensive repository for the considerable supply of valuables amassed by the Montfort family. The Vaults are home to not only a vast collections of jewels and similar valuables but also rare armors and weapons, all of which are guarded by a complicated series of traps and locks.
Quests
Heart of the Many
The Du Lac Sign
(optional, with
Aveline
)
Pirates and their Curses
(optional, with
Isabela
)
Puzzles
For a complete detailed walkthrough of solving the puzzles see
Heart of the Many: The Vaults
.
Map of the area
There are three puzzles within the vaults, and solving one extinguishes the curtain of flame that surrounds the centerpiece of the vault. All three puzzles require two parts; the first to resolve a puzzle in one of the side passages which nets some treasure as well as a clue to resolve the second part.
When a puzzle has been resolved, the framed black painting in that area will fill with a curious picture of men, some masked, some not. This clue will help Hawke  resolve the second part. When Hawke flips the floor panels in the main vault to match the pattern revealed in the paintings in the adjacent corridors, the valves feeding the flames shut off, leaving the treasure they protect, pieces of the class-specific
Messenger
,
Orlesian Lancer
, and
Enchanter Illana
armor sets accessible.
Puzzle Paintings
The first two puzzles present Hawke with a device and two runes of different colors. Doors corresponding to these runes will only open while Hawke is affected by the rune of the same color. Two of the colors, blue and red, may be mixed on the machine by touching the first color, then the machine, then touching the second color, then the machine, then touching the resulting rune it produces before opening the door. Before gaining access to one of the last colors needed to mix the final color, a Rune Golem will challenge the party and must be destroyed.
Vault's Prosper's Portrait solution
The third puzzle appears to be a pattern of floor tiles that flip along with their adjacent neighbors, to reveal a portrait of
Prosper
, apparently in his younger days. Because the tiles flip their neighbors, and a single tile may be affected by at least two others, they must be flipped in a pattern to reveal the entire portrait.
Resolving the last puzzle in the vault shuts off the remaining flame and reveals a chest containing the
Coat of the Messenger
,
Orlesian Lancer Armor
, and the
Robes of Enchanter Illana
completing the set (armor dependent on class).
See also
Heart of the Many: The Vaults

Sha-Brytol EarthShaker
The Guardian
Exits
Bastion of the Pure
Appearances
Dragon Age: Inquisition
-
The Descent
The Wellspring
is a gargantuan cavern in the
Uncharted Abyss
situated far beneath the
Deep Roads
.
Background
Its scale in both depth and length are so massive that it has formed its own ecosystem with clouds and a local fauna and flora. There is also an unknown light source inside the Wellspring.
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
The verdant cavern is in fact a space within a
titan
. Infused with huge veins of
lyrium
throughout, the Wellspring is also filled with
dwarven
structures inhabited by the
Sha-Brytol
.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
Shaper
Valta
and
the Inquisitor
reach the wellspring after a grueling trek through the Deep Roads and the caverns beneath them as part of
The Descent (quest)
. After an encounter with
the Guardian
at the lyrium heart at the center of the wellspring, Valta is somehow altered by her exposure and resolves to remain behind to investigate further. Later the
Inquisition
is alerted to a disturbance nearby and sends a team to investigate if Valta needs assistance, only to discover that the passage to the Wellspring has been sealed up as if it were never there and the stone repels all efforts to break through.

PAGE: Therinfal Redoubt
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Inquisition locations, Locations

Location
Therinfal Redoubt
Type
Fortress
Location
Ferelden
Inhabitants

(formerly)

Enemies
Red Templars
Demons
Quests
Champions of the Just
Demonic Dogma
Appearances
Dragon Age: Inquisition
Therinfal Redoubt
is a fortress located in
Ferelden
which formerly served as a training facility for the
Seekers of Truth
.
Background
The fortress was utilized as a training ground for the Seekers where they could practice and hone their skills without any possibility of distraction or temptation. It was in use since at least the
Storm Age
before the Seekers were finally forced to abandon the fortress in 8:99
Blessed
for financial reasons.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
When
the Inquisitor
arrives in
Val Royeaux
to address the clerics, Revered Mother
Hevara
spots Lord Seeker
Lucius Corin
and his
templars
approaching the stage and assumes they were returning to the
Chantry
to put an end to the
Inquisition
. But that was not the case, as she is punched unceremoniously to the ground. Lucius takes contempt in the revered mother's claim of authority and is equally insulted that the Inquisitor did not seek the templars' aid. Declaring the Chantry unworthy of the Order's protection, Lucius takes his templars and leaves. The templars withdraw and billet themselves at Therinfal Redoubt.
If the Inquisitor recruits the Templars:
The Inquisition drums up the support of ten
Orlesian
noble houses to pressure the templars into closing
the Breach
. However, Lord Seeker Lucius will only permit the meeting at Therinfal Redoubt if the Inquisitor attends the negotiations in person. Later on, it is revealed that the entire meeting was a elaborate trap: to purge the order of the Templars not affected by the
Red Lyrium
and to kill and replace the Inquisitor. A pitched battle erupts between the Templars and
Red templars
as the
Envy demon
who has been posing as Lord Seeker Lucius begins gathering information on the Inquisitor by sending its subordinates to their deaths against them in order to gather information on their leadership style.
Eventually the Envy demon is slain by the combined forces of the Inquisition's party, the Templars and a
Spirit
known as
Cole
; and the Red Templars at Therinfal are ultimately defeated. The remaining Templars may ally with or be conscripted into the Inquisition.
If the Inquisitor recruits the mages:
Once the templars of Therinfal Redoubt's corruption was complete, General
Samson
takes over as leader of the
Red Templars
. The facility is subsequently abandoned, and the Inquisition can dispatch
Bull's Chargers
to
Investigate Therinfal Redoubt
.
