======================================================================
PAGE: Tirashan
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Tirashan
======================================================================

“People need to learn that the Tirashan is a sleeping beast, and enough gnat-bites will rouse it.
”
Location
The Tirashan
Type
Forest
Location
Orlais
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
(mentioned)
Dragon Age II
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: The Last Court
Dragon Age: Inquisition
(mentioned)
Trespasser
(mentioned)
The
Tirashan
is a forest located on the western fringes of
Orlais
.
Background
The Tirashan forest is located at the edge of known
Thedas
, in the far west of the Orlesian Empire. The marquisate of
Serault
lies next to the Tirashan.
Tirashan is an ancient forest, its trees described to be "as old as the world".
It's also known as "a place of primordial appetites".
The following information is mentioned only in
Dragon Age: Origins: Prima Official Game Guide
. Certain portions of this publication, particularly the Traveler's Guide featured in the Collector's Edition, might not reflect currently established lore.
The forest is said to be an unbroken expanse of trees, and presented as one of the wonders of the world, "as mysterious and dangerous as the hottest of deserts". When compared to the
Arlathan Forest
, it's said that Arlathan is neither as dark nor as dangerous as the Tirashan.
Involvement
Dragon Age: The Last Court
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: The Last Court
.
Orlesian homesteads located at the edge of the Tirashan forest are attacked by elves, with many farmers being driven away from their homes as a result. If the Marquis of Serault increases the number of patrols and the attackers are successfully tracked down, their soldiers enter a skirmish with elves who wear brilliant crimson
tattoos
. These elves are hinted to be worshippers of
the Forgotten Ones
, as they do not call out to the typical
elven gods
during the battle.
On another occasion, one of the peasants discovers a strange claw-like object while picking mushrooms on the edge of the Tirashan. It's described to be "black as sin, as long as your forearm, sharp as winter sunlight, and engraved with unidentifiable runes." If the object is displayed in the Marquis' hall, servants come to call it "the dragon's toe".
The Kindly Knight
suggests it could date to the
Second Blight
, possibly linking it to the
archdemon
Zazikel
.
Dragon Age: Inquisition
Clan Lavellan
had once camped near a ruined fortress located on the edge of the Tirashan forest.
Trespasser
This section contains spoilers for
:
Trespasser
.
During the
Exalted Council
,
Leliana
relays to
the Inquisitor
that she has been receiving reports of "strange elves", resembling in some way the
Sentinel elves
of the
Arbor Wilds
, being active in the Tirashan.
See also
Sash of the Tirashan
Tirashan Swiftwind
Notes
In
Dragon Age: Origins: Prima Official Game Guide
Collector's Edition - Traveler's Guide, Tirashan is misspelled as "Tirishan".
References

Location
Tower of Ishal
Type

Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening

Dragon Age II
(mentioned)
The
Tower of Ishal
is a tower located to the east of the abandoned fortress of
Ostagar
and plays an important role in King
Cailan
's plans to defeat the
darkspawn
during the
Fifth Blight
.
Background
Archon
Ishal ordered the construction of the Tower at the height of
Tevinter Imperium
's power as a fortification against the
Chasind
of the
Korcari Wilds
. The tower was named after the Archon that ordered its construction and it was reportedly
constructed by the
dwarves
. Due to its massive height, the tower had primarily served as a lookout post in order to quickly detect Chasind incursions. After the
First Blight
, the whole of Ostagar, along with the tower, was abandoned and fell into disrepair but the tower still stands strong more than a millennium later.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
The Tower of Ishal with the beacon lit.
The entrance to the Tower of Ishal is located to the east of Ostagar. While in the process of
Joining the Grey Wardens
,
the Warden
can speak to the
Tower Guard
located outside the tower to find out a bit about its history. According to the Tower Guard, the tower is off-limits on Teyrn
Loghain
's orders while they secure the tower and survey the tunnels which run underneath the tower.
At the beginning of the
Battle of Ostagar
, during the
Tower of Ishal (quest)
, the tower is overrun by darkspawn coming through tunnels beneath the tower. The Warden, along with
Alistair
, are tasked to enter the tower, reach its top, and light the beacon in order for
Teyrn Loghain
's troops to attack the darkspawn horde from behind. After
Ferelden
's defeat at Ostagar, the tower is taken over by darkspawn.
The first floor of the Tower of Ishal can be revisited, as well as the darkspawn-created tunnels beneath the tower, as part of the
Return to Ostagar (quest)
. This requires the
Return to Ostagar
DLC
. Note that other floors cannot be accessed as the entrance to the second floor is barricaded.

The
Treacherous Path
is a
random encounter
in
Dragon Age: Origins
that occurs on the
World map
.
Involvement
The Warden
and their party stumble upon a camp with two dead refugees and a mysterious note.
Enemies
Demons
Shade
(3) (
Lieutenant
)
Greater shade
(1) (
Boss
)
Boss Abilities
Aura of Weakness
Disorient
Drain Life
Horror
Leap
Misdirection Hex
Slam
Weakness
Containers
Dead Refugee
Special objects
Tattered Note
Notes
It is suggested that the player buff the party before approaching the corpses. The Greater shade is incredibly powerful, even on lower difficulties.
While
Paralyze
and
Crushing Prison
may be somewhat useless on the Greater Shade,
Stonefist
knocks it down quite easily if the player has invested heavily in
Spellpower
.
Mana Clash
will greatly hurt them, but will also generate a lot of aggro for a mage.
The encounter seems to be an Easter Egg referencing the Lord of the Rings film. In the
console commands
, this encounter is named "LotR", and the note found on one of the bodies, the
Tattered Note
, mentions the eagles from the third film.

======================================================================
PAGE: Treviso
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Treviso
======================================================================

Location
Treviso
Type
Port-City
Terrain
Urban
Location
Coastal Antiva
Inhabitants
Humans
Elves
Dwarves
Qunari
Appearances
Dragon Age II
(mentioned)
The Black Emporium
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: Inquisition
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: Last Flight
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: Tevinter Nights
(mentioned)
As We Fly
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
Treviso
is a port-city in northern
Antiva
located on the coast of Rialto Bay.
Treviso has always been a free port. Even the Merchant Princes respect this. Their fortunes exist because its people trade and work where they will.
History
The
Antivan Crows
originate from the area surrounding Treviso. Their order was founded by a group of monks who poisoned their tyrannical duke.
Treviso remains an important city for the Crows; it is the seat of
House Valisti
,
whose members have frequently served as Talons for the Crows.
Treviso was captured and liberated several times during the Qunari Wars and the
New Exalted Marches
. It was first captured by the
Qunari
in 6:35
Steel
, as a result of which the city burned.
It was presumably liberated sometime during the first New Exalted March before being recaptured by the Qunari during the second New Exalted March, along with most of the rest of Antiva.
Treviso burned again in 7:52
Storm
when it was liberated by the armies of the
Imperial Chantry
and
Orlesian Chantry
.
A fire engulfed the city yet again in 8:62
Blessed
when a dockhand accidentally spilled a lantern in a warehouse full of lamp oil.
Involvement
As We Fly
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Tevinter Nights
.
Treviso has been invaded and captured by the
Qunari
. The occupying Qunari are led by
Daathrata
, a member of the
Antaam
who's nicknamed the Butcher due to his cruelty. The Antivan Crows in the city are actively engaged in resistance efforts, taking out Qunari lieutenants such as Kaathrata the Lash and destroying Qunari weapon caches.
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
The first act of the Antaam invasion was to blockade Treviso's trading ships with their
dreadnoughts
. Then they somehow took out all Treviso guard outposts in a single evening. After that, the Antaam built fortified camps right inside Treviso, positioned as though they'd already figured out where to choke off resistance. Some Antivan Crows believe there is a traitor in Treviso that facilitated the Antaam's invasion of the city.
Treviso is still under Antaam occupation, with their troops setting up checkpoints and regularly interrogating and stealing from citizens; the Crows continue to resist. After
Neve Gallus
suggests hiring the
Demon of Vyrantium
to help in the fight against the
Evanuris
,
Rook
and the
Veilguard
travel to Treviso to recruit him. After this, the Veilguard continue to work with the Crows to resist the Antaam occupation as the Antaam have aligned themselves with the elven gods.
In the quest
A Slow Poison
, the Antivan Crows send the Veilguard to discover what became of Dareth, their Crow spy on the
Butcher
, and follow up on his investigation. The Veilguard discovered Dareth was killed by the Antaam but they managed to release his cousin,
Jacobus
, from a cage. From Dareth's notes, it is revealed that the Butcher had employed a Qamekmaster to create large amounts of
Qamek
, but after sneaking into the Butcher's garrison, the team find out that someone stole some of the qamek he made. This infuriated the Butcher, who confined him to a room. The Qamekmaster took up
Ghilan'nain's
offer of power and transformed himself, transforming himself into a monstrous
reaver
in order to spite his punishment. Rook and their team slay the Qamekmaster. After inspecting his room, they find the Qamekmaster's note. The note made mention of the Butcher's inside traitor, confirming the Antaam occupation had help. Rook then reported their findings to
Viago
and
Teia
. When Rook returns to the Cantori Diamond, they meet Governor
Rayan Ivenci
, an uptight bureaucrat blaming the Crows for exacerbating Treviso's occupation and angry at how much agency they have.
After completing the quest
A Warden's Best Friend
,
Ghilan'nain
launches simultaneous dragon attacks on both Treviso and
Minrathous
. Rook may choose to save Treviso, in which case Minrathous will be overrun by the Venatori and most of the
Shadow Dragons
executed or murdered.
If Rook chooses to save Minrathous:
Should Rook choose to save Minrathous, the Crows and Treviso civilians suffer heavy losses and Treviso becomes infested with
Blight
. The Antaam appear to be in league with Ghilan'nain, as they fought the Crows during the dragon attack and claimed to have used the chaos to crush the Crows' "meaningless resistance." The Grande Market becomes a field hospital for the sick and injured. Many Trevisians have a lower opinion of the Antivan Crows who remain allied with the Veilguard, the group they blame for their city getting blighted.
In the quest
Statuary, Fine Art, and Antaam, Part 1
, the Veilguard follow a lead from Mistress Trella and discover the Antaam in Treviso have been confiscating Treviso art and trading it to
Venatori
for magical components that will magically enhance their
gaatlok
powder. The Antaam and Venatori form an uneasy alliance to develop this more powerful version of magical gaatlok and in
Statuary, Fine Art, and Antaam, Part 2
, the two organizations have completed development of it; although neither party seems impressed with the completed product nor enjoy the company of their partnering faction. During the quest, the Antaam and Venatori have begun to set up these new and improved explosives across Treviso, with the terrorists seeking to detonate them all at once to intimidate the city's citizens and to destroy the homes of those they deem troublesome. The Antivan Crows task Rook and their team to dispatch these terrorists and to destroy the gaatlok caches discreetly.
Later on, it is revealed that
Illario Dellamorte
conspired with the
Venatori
to remove
Lucanis Dellamorte
as a contender for the title of
First Talon
so that he could seize it for himself. When
Elgar'nan
and
Ghilan'nain
escaped their
Fade
prison and recruited the Venatori to be their agents, Illario aligned himself with the
elven gods
. Elgar'nan gifted Illario a bird-like brooch that gave him the ability to use
blood magic
to subdue Lucanis, who was
possessed
by
Spite
. Elgar'nan likely saw Illario as a useful tool to further his plan to consolidate his control over Antiva. After Illario killed
Zara Renata
to cover up his ties to the Venatori, this provoked Spite to a fury that nearly compelled Lucanis to murder Illario but Illario used blood magic to subdue him. Using Spite's attempted murder of him to discredit Lucanis as a suitable First Talon in the eyes of the Crows, Illario had told the Veilguard to keep Lucanis away from Treviso as he would spread word that Lucanis was a danger to the Crows and his family.
Eventually Lucanis discovered Illario's treachery and also that Illario faked
Caterina Dellamorte
's death and has been imprisoning her in the Dellamorte Estates. While Illario was hosting a gathering of the Antivan Crows at the Dellamorte opera house to propose an alliance between the Crows and the Venatori and also to formally recognize him as First Talon, Lucanis and the Veilguard set Caterina free and went on to challenge Illario's ascension and plan. The Veilguard defeated Illario and his Venatori supporters and Caterina revealed herself to be alive, which confirmed Illario to be a traitor. After Lucanis sentenced Illario for his crimes, Caterina formally declared Lucanis First Talon, thus validating his authority.
It is later revealed that Treviso's
Governor Ivenci
was working with the Antaam behind the scenes to destroy the Crows and take full control of the city. Ivenci justifies their betrayal by claiming they were trying to keep their people from being slaughtered by the Antaam. Rook also discovers that the
Butcher
has grown fond of Treviso during his time occupying the city, to the point where he will deliberately give himself up to be
blighted
in order to discover the location of the Evanuris' chosen ritual site, hoping to ultimately save the city from the gods. After his death, Ivenci takes command of Treviso's Antaam, who seems to believe in whatever purpose Ivenci persuaded them with. Ivenci's Antaam followers state that the Butcher was weak, and so they follow the governor now. Some of the Butcher's loyalists decry Ivenci's Antaam for going against the
Qun
. It is suspected that after destroying the Antivan Crows, Ivenci will install the Antaam as Antiva's "protectors."
Ivenci plans to release
qamek
all around Treviso to "pacify violent seditionists." In the quest
A Time to Kill
, Rook attacks Ivenci's forces and may choose to either kill Ivenci or keep him alive to face trial.

Places
The in-game map of the Treviso zones.
Cantori Diamond
- A large casino owned by the Cantori family that is used as a hideout for the Crows.
Teia Cantori
and
Viago de Riva
can be found here, as well as faction merchant
Fletcher
and the Treviso
eluvian
.
Crow's Road Rooftops
- A large section of Treviso north of the Cantori Diamond. A Blight Cyst containing
Desmal, Bloodbound to Elgar'nan
can be found here. Much of this map section, denoted with the dark purple line, is not available outside of key quests.
✱ - This section of Crow's Road Rooftops, including the pictured chest, are not available until beginning
A Murder of Crows
, and may not be available at all pending player choice during
On Blighted Wings
/
On Deadly Wings
.
† - This section of Crow's Road Rooftops is only available during
A Slow Poison
,
On Deadly Wings
, and
A Time to Kill
.
Antaam Garrison
- A subsection of the Crow's Road Rooftops where the occupying Antaam army, including the
Butcher
, is based. Only available during
A Slow Poison
,
On Deadly Wings
, and
A Time to Kill
.
Heart and Central
- The central portion of Treviso, containing the merchant
Mistress Trella
's
art gallery
(southwest corner), a large respawning squad of Antaam (east, over the bridge), and a Fen'harel altar.
Streets of Coin Financials
- The financial district in Treviso, including the banking quarter. The bank itself is on an upper level on the southernmost street, only accessible by climbing a vine-covered lattice and a ladder. It also contains a small chantry on the eastern side, which is a separate entity from the Fallen Chantry accessed during
Bloodbath
.
Grande Markets
- The shopping center of Treviso, this section contains Café Pietra, a famous Antivan café, and may contain a
number of unmarked merchants
pending player choices during
On Blighted Wings
/
On Deadly Wings
.
Governer Ivenci
can be found here arguing with Noa de Acutis when not participating in a quest. A Qunari woman struggling with the common tongue may also be found here, and
Taash
will offer language assistance if in the party when speaking to her.

The Drowned District
- A poor region of Treviso peopled by refugees and the disenfranchised. Three unmarked merchants may be found in the northern section near the upper travel beacon; the southern portion comprises some extensive dilapidated docks and two entrenched squads of respawning
Antaam
.

Lucanis

A Murder of Crows
.
Fallen Chantry
- A large Trevisan chantry with extensive underground tunnels, only accessible during
Bloodbath
.

Zara Renata
, as well as several of her
Venatori
lieutenants and soldiers, will be found and killed here during this quest.
Chest locations
A map of the Treviso chest locations and relevant puzzle clues. For information on the pink-circled chest, see the Quest Chests subsection.
Thirteen regular chests, two lyrium-dagger chests (fifteen chests total), and one Evanuris statue are required for 100% completion. One chest appears on the exploration map but does
not
count towards the 100% total (chest #8). See the map for individual locations.
Ten additional chests appear during region-specific quests, including the pink-circled one in the Crow's Road Rooftops near the Blight Cyst, but these also do
not
count towards the 100% total (see Quest Chests subsection below).
Not accessible until
Bloodbath
has been acquired. This is a very tricky chest to find.
Access the
Venatori
hideout by clambering up the boxes marked with the blue X on the map. Take the right stairs upward until Rook reaches an open-air balcony overlooking two suspended wooden platforms and a Venatori fight on the lower level of the other side.
Jump across, fight the Venatori, and climb up the vine-covered trellis. (To the right from here, Rook can see a closed door back to the Cantori Diamond; jumping across the gap and opening it will allow for a shortcut in future.)
However, Rook should head left across the grated walkway into the nearby building, then take the right-side drop back down to the lower level. The double-doors can now be opened to provide shortcut access back outside, and a table on the north wall will hold an
Official Document
to advance the Bloodbath quest. (A small shortcut door to the right of this table may be opened as well, but it may close again on future visits.)
Location of the breakable barricade blocking the long final path to chest #1.
From the table where this document is read, turn south and see a wooden barricade blocking a small chest (reference image to right). Break the barricade, then follow the long, winding, but linear path to the chest.
From the upper level of the building to the north (near the zipline icon), sidle along the high, narrow ledge to drop down into this barred cell from above. Pull the lever near the barred gate to leave. This chest cannot be accessed until completing
Sea of Blood
, as NPCs will block access to the ledge.
From the Heart and Central fast-travel beacon, take the vine-covered lattice immediately to the beacon's left. A second ladder will become available to Rook's right; take this ladder up one more level. Instead of hopping through the broken window and fighting the Venatori, follow the roofline around to the left, then drop down to the lower landing with the chest.
One of two lyrium chests in Treviso. This large puzzle requires you to turn four
Grey Warden
statues to face the locked door on the north wall near the chest. Each of the four braziers will light as you correctly align each statue. Spite can be activated with Veil Instability near each statue to either provide access to relevant levers or to indicate which way the statue should face.
Northwest corner: turn the statue twice to face it towards the gate.
West side: turn the statue once clockwise to face it towards the gate.
Northeast corner: turn the statue once counterclockwise to face it towards the gate.
East side: three levers within the garden must be flipped to open this locked gate and access this statue. Use Spite's Reach on the large winged statue near the gate to create a hand up to the platform and flip the first lever. The second and third levers are on the lower level in the middle of the garden; use Spite's Reach near the northernmost one to create two bridges to reach both levers. Once all three levers have been flipped, the gate will open. Clamber up the boxes to reach the statue and turn it once counterclockwise to unlock the chest's gate at last. Inside, find a lyrium chest and a
Scrawled Note
from the puzzle's inventor.
Not accessible until
Statuary, Fine Art, and Antaam, Part 1
can be acquired. Break the scaffolding in front of the art gallery, then interact with the northern wall once inside to reveal the chest and the
Randy Dowager Quarterly, Vol. 3
.
Behind an unlocked door, along with a second smaller chest hidden behind some crates.
Activate the hidden door to access the large southern chamber where a
Flame-Touched Sentinel
has been sealed. After defeating the Sentinel, follow the corridor southwest to find the chest.
While this chest appears on the exploration map, it is part of the
Bloodbath
quest and does
not
count towards map completion. It can be acquired after speaking to
Illario
and dropping down into the Fallen Chantry map; it is not accessible outside of this quest.
Follow this path south as far as possible until it reaches the water. Use Harding's Eternal Hymn to elevate three slabs of stone and jump across to the southernmost platform. Follow the path around to the right, then open the door to find the chest (trapped) and the memento
The Means of Antiva
hidden behind some boxes.
Walk as far east as possible along this path until it ends in a balcony. Use Spite's Reach to create a bridge around the right corner, and follow the path the rest of the way to access the room with the chest. On the way back, pull the lever to open the gate back to the streets.
The second of two lyrium chests in Treviso and a long series of climbs.
From the Streets of Coin Financials fast-travel beacon, head east. Immediately after the turn to the right/south, climb up the vine-covered trellis and then the ladder.
Atop the roofs, turn to the right to see some boxes which can be clambered up to reach (after a small jump) another level of rooftop. Once you have reached this balcony, look to the right to find heartwood in the shadows indicating a walkable roof. (Taking the path left here will lead you back to a zipline.)
Hop the balcony railing toward the heartwood and follow the new path up and left, then climb through the broken window. To the left (near the lyrium chest), activate the hidden door within the bookcases to finally reach the chest.
The Ravens of Antiva
memento can also be found here, tucked behind a statue.
From the nearby pink X on the map (south, at water level), use Harding's Eternal Hymn to elevate three stone slabs. Jump across to reach this chest.
Requires Taash's Ignite ability. Light four braziers to open the ground-level gate and access the chest.
On ground level in the southwest corner.
Up the trellis near the first brazier, then to the left through a breakable wooden barricade. A second wooden barricade within this room must be broken to access this brazier. Note: this brazier's yellow-lock icon may not appear on the map.
Up the trellis near the first brazier, then around to the right, on a small wooden platform overlooking the square.
From the third brazier, jump across to the rooftops east and then south, then tightrope across a narrow wooden beam to find the brazier behind some crates. (The second trellis found along this path should be ignored, unless Rook has not yet activated the zipline which can be found atop it.)
Behind a closed but unlocked door, near the
Writ of Free Commerce
memento.
At the nearby purple X marked on the map, clamber up the boxes to reach the dock building's roof. Follow the roofs south, then use Spite's Reach to continue eastward on the upper level. Jump atop the roof of the building containing the chest, then drop down through the hole in the roof directly above the chest. Bash down the long beam to exit the building.
From the westward beacon in the Drowned District, take the ladder immediately beside it up one level. Clamber through the lit window immediately to Rook's left, then work straight westward to find a wooden barricade blocking a small nook. Break the barricade and take the ladder inside to reach the upper level and the chest, as well as the
Vint-6 the Common Red
memento.
Evanuris statue
One
Evanuris
statue can be found in Treviso. Just south of the Dellamorte estates, activate the
Fen'Harel
altar. The wolf statue can be found to the northwest, behind some crates.

. The chest circled in pink on the map above is part of
A Murder of Crows
and is inaccessible before it begins (and after it completes). See each quest's page for full details on its chest locations.

Trivia
During the occupation of Treviso in the Qunari Wars or the New Exalted Marches, a famine food known as Treviso energy balls was invented to help people get by in these periods of hardship and scarcity. They are comprised of peanut butter, oats, and dried fruit. Though small in size, they are energy-rich.
Treviso
is the name of a city in the Veneto region of northern Italy.
Rialto
(the bay where Treviso is located) is a central area in Venice, which is the main city of Veneto region.
Similar to the market in
Dock Town
, there is a lute player in the Grande Market that plays a selection of tavern songs that were originally played in the
Singing Maiden
and the
Herald's Rest
.
According to
Lucanis Dellamorte
, Treviso is a merchant city with many canals. It has no defenses aside from the protection of the Antivan Crows.

Location

Unadin Grotto
is an ancient
Dalish
tomb located below the sacred Elven burial site of
Var Bellanaris
on the
Exalted Plains
.
Background
Unadin Grotto is a tomb within
Var Bellanaris
.
Var Bellanaris. "Our Eternity." Where hundreds of our ancestors are buried, where those of us who can, still lay our kin to rest. Although this land now belongs to Orlais, the Shemlen have never reclaimed it. Perhaps their guilty conscience stops them. Or perhaps those uncomplimentary tales of elven curses and vengeful spirits have some use after all.
—
Keeper
Gisharel
of the
Ralaferin clan
Quests
The Spoils of Desecration
Enemies
Undead
Arcane horror
Corpse
Revenant
Mosaics
Invasion
numbering
Invasion
(piece #5) - found in the final chamber, head through the locked door and up the stairs.
Notes
It is possible to enter Unadin Grotto without destroying the tombs to find the key and thus incurring the disapproval of
Keeper
Hawen
. To do so, get on your
mount
and move it until its left side is as close to the door as possible. When you dismount, you might be able to clip through the door and enter without having to open it. As the door will still be considered locked, there will be no enemies inside the grotto and you will need to use fast travel or the mount trick again to leave. This may be easier on smaller mounts like the
dracolisk
.
Alternatively, wait until
From the Beyond
is completed, after which the tomb may be looted without consequence.
Bugs
The spawn point on the north side of the final room pings on search.

======================================================================
PAGE: University of Orlais
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/University_of_Orlais
======================================================================

For the
War table
mission in
Dragon Age: Inquisition
, see
The University of Orlais
.
Location
University of Orlais
Type
University
Location
Val Royeaux
,
Orlais
Leaders
Henri Morrac
(formerly)
Jurgen Haulis
(as of 9:41)
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Masked Empire
Dragon Age: The Last Court
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: Inquisition
(mentioned)
Jaws of Hakkon
(mentioned)
The
University of Orlais
in
Val Royeaux
is one of the oldest universities in
Thedas
. It is a major center of learning that attracts young nobles from all over Thedas as it provides some of the best education one can buy. The university is a relatively progressive institution whose liberal-minded professors have clashed with religious conservatives over the content of classes. In theory, commoners, including elves, are allowed to attend if sponsored by a noble patron.
Background
The university was founded by one of the former
rulers
of
Orlais
. For many ages, it was just a way to get rid of unpromising children, with the Chantry snapping up all of the greatest minds. Under political and religious pressures, the school could only study what none of the former had problems with, thus producing little more than harsh critiques of other nations and occasional mathematical findings. This changed when newly crowned Empress
Celene
announced that she would study at the university to learn from the greatest minds of the nation. In truth, her duties meant that she was only able to visit with the professors, but thanks to Celene's influence, the university became one of the most respected learning institutions in all of Thedas. With the empress' public support, it soon became fashionable for even the eldest children of noble families to study at the University of Orlais, both for scholarly pursuits and as a field of play in
the Grand Game
. The heightened prestige of the university attracted students not only from Orlais, but all across Thedas, including
Nevarra
, the
Free Marches
,
and the
Tevinter Imperium
.
Fearing that religious and political pressure might hinder the university's capacity for innovation and research, Empress Celene has made a concerted effort to ensure that the University is free of any such constraining interests.
When the Chantry objected to the university's plan to study changes to the
Chant of Light
over the ages, Celene declared that as long as the research dealt only in history, it was the domain of mortals and not the
Maker
, and that inquiries into the means by which the Chant was perfected could lead to better understanding of the Chant. This support, along with a substantial donation to the Chantry, waylaid any further concerns.
Celene's efforts to transform the university into a respected institution is regarded by some as the most vehemently opposed project in Orlesian history, successful only because of her ability to win the support of even the most bitter rivals.
An ardent patron of the arts, she hoped the university would be her legacy.
In recent years, Celene has been strongly encouraging the university to overlook the lack of status and rank for individuals who show prodigious talent or potential which could help further the interests and prestige of the Orlesian Empire, provided a noble sponsors them. Most recently, this has even extended to Celene personally interceding in support of an elven math prodigy, Lennan, who was sponsored by Comtesse Helene.
Elves are now allowed into the university, although it is common for their work to go unacknowledged and uncredited.
In addition to humans and elves, the University of Orlais is also home to several
dwarven
academics.
The university offers classes on nature, magic, history, mathematics, and the arts that are respected throughout the world.
Home to some of the best scholars in Thedas, it also offers lectures from visiting scholars such as renowned Chantry scholar
Ferdinand Genitivi
and Mother Clothilde.
Although it is an infamously liberal institution, at least one brilliant scholar has been thrown out for radical and revolutionary political ideas.
The grounds of the university include a great Chantry Courtyard,
as well as the Great Library, one of the largest libraries in the world and rivalled in Orlais only by those at the Imperial Palace and the
Winter Palace
in
Halamshiral
.
The university and library's collections include a detailed series of woodcuts on the anatomy of various livestock and other creatures (such as
nugs
) by Ambrose Dumont, now used as a primary reference by visiting naturalists,
a wide variety of texts containing possible references to the fortress of
Skyhold
in the
Frostback Mountains
,
and the personal library of Comte Brevin
de Chalons
, donated upon his death.
Involvement
Dragon Age: The Masked Empire
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: The Masked Empire
.
Empress
Celene
travels to the university where she meets with the Chancellor to discuss the institution's lack of non noble-blooded students despite her decree opening it to commoners with noble patronage. She convinces the Chancellor to reconsider the application of Lennan, an elven math prodigy sponsored by Comtesse Helene. She then retires to the university's chantry to meet with
Sister Nightingale
, the representative of
Divine
Justinia V
.
The move to allow elves into the University is controversial. In response, several professors are asked to write papers about elves. One asserts that they are similar to rabbits and therefore not to be trusted. Another states that interracial relationships are an affront to the
Maker
.
Dragon Age: The Last Court
A Travelling Scholar
who was thrown out of the University for his unorthodox views eventually makes his way to
Serault
and seeks patronage from the
Marquis of Serault
.
Dragon Age: Inquisition
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
In the wake of the disaster at the
Temple of Sacred Ashes
, the University of Orlais establishes a good working relationship with the newly reformed
Inquisition
, offering resources and researchers in exchange for political and military protection.
Bram Kenric
, a scholar at the University also requests the Inquisition's aid in investigating the original incarnation of the Inquisition, particularly its leader,
Inquisitor
Ameridan
.
If
Gaspard de Chalons
becomes Emperor:
Kenric mentions that Emperor Gaspard has surprisingly made no demands of the University and ostensibly intends to continue Celene's policy of non-involvement and support.

Trivia
By decree of
Divine
Renata I
, any
Chantry
art in
Orlais
depicting
elves
was destroyed save a single mural of
Shartan
. A reproduction of this mural is depicted at the university, albeit with Shartan's ears cropped. This was the Divine's sole concession.
Brother
Ferdinand Genitivi
lectured at the university on the conflict between fact and belief.
The University of Orlais is one of the few institutions of higher learning mentioned in the series that is
not
a
Circle of Magi
.
The University's chantry is the tallest building in the university, nicknamed the "bosom of Andraste" by students because of its twin bronze domes.

PAGE: Val Royeaux

“Unlike other cities, where the people are the life-blood and the character, Val Royeaux was her own person, and her people little more than decorations. There was always music in Val Royeaux, streaming from the many windows—quiet refrains and triumphant choruses. And always, floating above that all, the
Chant
, coming from the
Grand Cathedral
. It was magnificent.”
―
Leliana
Location
Val Royeaux
Type
Capital City
Terrain
Urban
Location
Orlais
Demonym
Royans
Inhabitants
Humans
Elves
Dwarves
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Stolen Throne

Dragon Age: Origins

Trespasser

Dragon Age: Tevinter Nights
(mentioned)
Val Royeaux
is the capital-city of
Orlais
and seat of the
Orlesian Chantry
. It lies on the northern coast of the far inland tip of the
Waking Sea
. It is one of the largest cities in
Thedas
.
Background
Val Royeaux is not only the secular capital of Orlais, but also the capital of the Chantry, the seat of the
Divine
being located at the
Grand Cathedral
of Val Royeaux. Inside the Cathedral, members of the Chantry perform a ritual where the entire
Chant of Light
is sung, a process thought to take about a fortnight to complete. From the
White Spire
, the
Knight-Vigilant
of the
templars
directs the Order. Val Royeaux is also where aging templars retire to when they can no longer adequately perform their services due to mental deterioration caused by long-term
lyrium
dependency.
The city can be entered through two different gates—the imposing Sun Gates made of steel covered with golden façade, and the more modest Night Gate. In times of crisis, the entrances are sealed. During the
Dragon Age
, the city was closed off at least twice—in 9:22 when dragons attacked the Grand Cathedral, and then in 9:40 as the
Orlesian Civil War
began.
Val Royeaux is home to the
University of Orlais
, a major center of learning that attracts young nobles from all over Thedas with the best education money can buy. It is a relatively modern institution whose liberal-minded professors have already clashed with religious conservatives over the content of their classes.
The
Alienage
of Val Royeaux is the most populated and dilapidated in Thedas, with over ten thousand elves living in an area roughly equivalent in size to the
market
in
Denerim
.
History
The area of Val Royeaux was once populated by the tribes of the
Ciriane
. At the end of
Andraste
's
Exalted March
, these lands were granted to
Isorath
, eldest of Andraste's adopted sons. In -155
Ancient
, to centralize trade and power, Isorath ordered sweeping changes in trade and forced relocations to break old allegiances in a moment known as the Grand Unification.
Several existing cities were leveled to make space for a capital worthy of his new nation:
Val Royeaux
.
In 2:10
Glory
, the
elven
army's capture of Montsimmard and march towards Val Royeaux prompted
Divine
Renata I
to order the
Exalted March of the Dales
. Val Royeaux was eventually sacked by the elves in 2:14 Glory,
but was liberated later during the war.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
The Inquisitor
may visit Val Royeaux anytime after speaking with
Giselle
in the
Hinterlands
during
The Threat Remains
and obtaining 4 Power. They go there in order to talk to the remaining Chantry mothers regarding their accusations and hoping that at least some of them will grow doubtful of the Chantry's claims about the newly formed
Inquisition
. After Revered Mother
Hevara
publicly denounces the Inquisition and declares its followers heretics, Lord Seeker
Lucius
interrupts her speech and one of the Templars under his command knocks her out. He mocks the Chantry mothers and dismisses any idea of allying with the Inquisition before leading all Templar forces out of the city, claiming it is not worthy of their protection.
Following this incident, the Inquisitor receives an official invitation by Imperial enchanter
Vivienne
to attend a party at the Duke Ghislain's estate, as well as instructions to meet
Friends of Red Jenny
member
Sera
in a secluded courtyard. Upon leaving the city,
Grand Enchanter
Fiona
approaches the Inquisitor, explaining her rebel mages might be open to an alliance, if the Inquisitor comes to
Redcliffe Village
to negotiate.
The Inquisitor has no more official business in Val Royeaux, but may visit the capital again for shopping or on business related to or on behalf of their
inner circle
.
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
The strange, new
darkspawn
that
Elgar'nan
and
Ghilan'nain
had deployed to southern Thedas have spread fear and corruption greater than any previous
Blight
in history. Darkspawn have cut through the center of Orlais. Val Royeaux is barely holding out.
Eventually, Val Royeaux was conquered by an alliance of
Venatori
and the Rebel Orlesians, and from there the Venatori launch attacks as far east as
Kirkwall
. The Venatori and the Orlesian royal armies clash daily in Orlais as they vie for the Orlesian Empire.
The Inquisitor
has sent several
Fereldan
companies to assist the Orlesian royal armies in the Orlais front, but while the Orlesian troops don't attack them, they refuse to coordinate tactics. Presumably, the history of distrust between Ferelden and Orlais has soured the notion of cooperation between the two nations against a common foe.

Val Royeaux Prison
consists of a series of holding cells just off the
Summer Bazaar
in
Val Royeaux
to house those arrested of local crimes.

Blackwall

companion quest
Revelations
.

Blackwall
is taken into custody in

detailing the crime. Cullen suggests that the

back at

.

Location
Valammar
Type
Lost Thaig
Terrain
Dwarven Architecture
Underground
Location
Beneath the
Hinterlands
Inhabitants
Dwarves
(formerly)
Carta
Darkspawn
Enemies
Carta Assassins
Carta Bowmen
Carta Grunts
Hurlocks
Hurlock archers
Hurlock alphas
Quests
Deep Trouble
The Vault of Valammar
Well, Shit
Exits
Hinterlands
Appearances
Dragon Age: Inquisition
Valammar
is a lost
thaig
accessible from the
Hinterlands
in
Ferelden
.
Background
In
ancient
times the
dwarves
used the region as a trading post until it fell to the
darkspawn
horde.
The uppermost layer of the soil has since collapsed, leaving the surface exposed, allowing for a massive waterfall to form. The built up humidity from the water has allowed trees and other flora to flourish about the region.
Architecturally, Valammar is very similar to
Orzammar
in the sense that the settlement is designed in a series of tiers.
The thaig lay docile beneath the Hinterlands unbeknownst to those above.
Darkspawn
roam freely undisturbed—until the
Carta
's sudden interest in the abandoned fissure.
Involvement
“It's extremely surprising to find such a high quantity of the product this close to the surface. If I didn't know better, I'd think it was growing.”
―
carta smuggler
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
Bianca Davri
discovers
red lyrium
growing about the connecting
mines
to Valammar. She deduces that red lyrium is a byproduct of
lyrium
infused with Darkspawn
taint
, and quickly realizes that the dangers far outweigh its potential. The risk associated with the spread of its corruption motivates Bianca to eagerly seek out anyone with knowledge on the matter. Bianca's connections allow for her to make contact with an unknown
Grey Warden
Mage
.
At this point either
Larius
or
Janeka
,
(depending on whom
Hawke
allied with whilst navigating the
Legacy
DLC
for
Dragon Age II
)
, crosses paths with Bianca masquerading as the
Grey Warden Mage
. Larius/ Janeka subtly disclose to Bianca that they are able to safely handle the red lyrium, conning Bianca into providing them with the key and exact location to the red lyrium caches. Bianca, unaware that the Warden Mage is in fact an
ancient corrupted magister
, reaches out to
Varric Tethras
with a possible lead for
Corypheus
' red lyrium source.
During the events of the
Mage-Templar War
outbreak, the mine location and key were passed along to Carta dwarves via the Elder One's interceding. They realized that the thaig would provide a lucrative source of income by mining the lyrium at the source rather than smuggle it in from Orzammar, which involved way too many intermediaries, and thus produced much smaller profit margins.
Without having to work with the
old families
back in
Orzammar
, or even take this through official
guild
channels, our families stand to make a killing. On the other hand, the proximity to the surface and to the populated areas of
Redcliffe
raises an interesting challenge. The
war
between the
mages
and
templars
should keep people out of the
area
for now, but as soon as the humans are done trying to kill each other, any operation we start will be uncovered. Talk to the families and make it happen. This is too good to pass up.
In addition to the current discord ravaging the
Hinterlands
, via the violence and onslaught produced between the Mages and Templars, mercenaries are hired to further destabilize the region—pillaging refugees and raiding nearby settlements allowing for mining operations to continue in secrecy.
We hired your mercenary organization to facilitate this business venture, not add complications. In order for the
Carta
to extract the
product
without attracting unwanted attention, the area must be free from observation. The fighting between the apostate mages and the renegade templars must remain unaltered; your efforts now are to dissuade refugees from exploring the area and prevent interference with our operation.
We don't need any heroes on this operation. We're being paid to keep things quiet and keep trade away from "The East Road". When reasonable, allow targets to flee and spread word of the danger in the area. When such is unreasonable, kill all targets, regardless of colors flown.
However, despite the Carta's best efforts to secure the thaig, the numbers needed to do so were in-vain due to the constant threat of
Darkspawn
swarming about in the dark on the lower levels. The over-looming threat of Darkspawn encroaching on the upper levels via the vast interconnecting chasms kept the Carta on edge,
forcing them to barricade off most of the area including multiple connecting bridges to the
Primeval Thaig
.
Acting on a tip from Davri, Varric informs
the Inquisitor
of the dangers stirring in Valammar. Bianca discloses that the location of
Bartrand's Folly
has been leaked, and that the Carta are using Valammar as a smuggling front. Varric imparts the he provided Bianca with the thaig's location from a letter he sent her post returning from an expedition with his brother
Bartrand
in
Dragon Age II
. The Warden Mage, whom successfully dispatched either of the high ranking Wardens, Larius or Janeka, preceding the events from the
Legacy
DLC
, is finally reveled and an undertaking is set with an agenda to eliminate the Carta's presence in Valammar permanently shutting down the red lyrium excavations. To access Valammar the
Valammar Key
is required. The key can be obtained on an overlook in the
Grand Forest Villa
during the side quest
The Mercenary Fortress
.
Note:
If side quest
Well, Shit
is active,
Bianca
will have already traveled to Valammar and unlocked the door allowing for entry to the area. The side quest
Deep Trouble
can still be completed once the Valammar Key has been acquired from the Grand Forest Villa. However, initiating and completing the aforementioned quests out of their intended order will prevent the side quest
The Mercenary Fortress
from being completed.

Notes
A pot, hidden beneath rubble, in
The Vaults
area contains tier 3 loot.
A loading screen will trigger upon entering Valammar from the Hinterlands, once this occurs the area is considered "indoors" and fast travel will be disabled.
It is advisable to have at least one
rogue
, one
warrior
and one
mage
in your party as there are walls to be bashed, locks to be picked, and a barrier to the entrance of a darkspawn cave to be dispelled.
If the Inquisitor comes here for one quest and makes changes to the environment such as bashing a hole in the wall or opening the vault doors, these changes will still be present when they return for another quest. However, loot containers will restock and creatures will respawn.
Bugs
At the top of the stairs leading to the upper terrace is a
Drakestone
node and behind it is an interaction point that says "Take." Interacting with the point causes it to disappear, but nothing is received in your inventory, codex entry menu, or journal.
If you seal off the Darkspawn tunnel, leaving Valammar and returning to that section of the dungeon will cause the Inquisitor and any companions who are present to act as if you never did. However, it only needs to be sealed off once, and doesn't reset.

PAGE: Valarian Fields
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Valarian_Fields
======================================================================

Location
Valarian Fields
Terrain
Plains
Location
Tevinter Imperium
Appearances
Historical
Dragon Age: Origins
(mentioned)
Dragon Age II
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: Inquisition
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: Magekiller
The
Valarian Fields
are a plain in the northwestern
Tevinter Imperium
, on the coast of the Nocen Sea, at the foot of the
High Reaches
. The
Imperial Highway
crosses the Fields. It was the site of an important battle during the
Exalted March against the Tevinter Imperium
.
Background
The armies clash on Valarian Fields
At Shartan's word, the sky
Grew black with arrows.
At Our Lady's, ten thousand swords
Rang from their sheaths.
A great hymn rose over Valarian Fields gladly, proclaiming:
Those who had been slaves were now free.
The
Battle of Valarian Fields
occurred in -171
Ancient
. The
Alamarri
army under
Andraste
and
Maferath
's leadership advanced deeper into the
Tevinter Imperium
, emboldened by previous victories. They joined with elven forces made up of ex-
slaves
led by
Shartan
. The Battle of Valarian Fields was where they first faced significant difficulties fighting Tevinter, since they entered the heart of the Imperium. The battle proved bloody as the situation was changing rapidly from siege to open fighting. In the end, the Alamarri and elven forces emerged victorious.
It is said that the southern forces managed to defeat the greatest army Tevinter ever mustered. Andrastians believe that the Maker smiled on the world that day.
When Andraste met Shartan on the Valarian Fields, she gave him a sword that originally belonged to her mother
Brona
;
Shartan then renamed the blade "Glandivalis".
According to the
Canticle of Transfigurations 10:1
, Andraste gave a sermon at Valarian Fields.
A part of that canticle is often sung in
mourning ceremonies
.
Involvement
Dragon Age: Magekiller
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Magekiller
.
Old building in the Valarian Fields
After fulfilling a mission in
Hercinia
,
Marius
is approached by
Flavius
, a slave in the service of
Magister
Illeneva. The Magister has learned of a group of blood mages sacrificing children to fuel uniquely powerful blood magic rituals in Tevinter and enlists Marius to destroy the group. Despite his misgivings about returning to Tevinter, Marius and
Tessa
travel to the site of the ritual, a set of ruins in the Valarian Fields just north of
Minrathous
. At their heart, Marius encounters Flavius himself, accompanied by a whole cohort of Tevinter soldiers, and led by
Radonis
,
Archon
of the Imperium, who has a task for the magekillers. Marius bluntly refuses Radonis' offer of work but is eventually compelled to relent by Tessa. Radonis reveals that the targets he wants assassinated are senior members of a cult known as the
Venatori
. Marius and Tessa leave the ruins and set out on their new mission.
References

======================================================================
PAGE: Valence Cloister
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Inquisition locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Valence_Cloister

is a small but ornate
Chantry
in
Orlais
. It is most famous for being the former residence of Revered Mother
Dorothea
, who would go on to become
Divine
Justinia V
.

Var Bellanaris
is a sacred Elven burial site located on the
Exalted Plains
.
Background
Unadin Grotto within Var Bellanaris
Var Bellanaris' interior is known as the
Unadin Grotto
.
Var Bellanaris. "Our Eternity." Where hundreds of our ancestors are buried, where those of us who can, still lay our kin to rest. Although this land now belongs to Orlais, the Shemlen have never reclaimed it. Perhaps their guilty conscience stops them. Or perhaps those uncomplimentary tales of elven curses and vengeful spirits have some use after all.
—
Keeper
Gisharel
of the
Ralaferin clan

the aforementioned quest will provide detailed instructions.

======================================================================
PAGE: Velabanchel
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Velabanchel
======================================================================

Location
Velabanchel
Type
Prison Fortress
Terrain
Island Citadel
Location
Near
Antiva City
Inhabitants
Antivan Crows
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Silent Grove
Dragon Age: Inquisition
(mentioned)
Velabanchel
is a prison controlled by the
Antivan Crows
.
Background
Velabanchel is an infamous and feared prison where the Antivan Crows incarcerate victims for "fun and torture". The prison's reputation has earned it the nickname "The House of Graves". The prison is virtually impenetrable, as it located on a rocky outcrop over the freezing sea and literally swarming with seasoned assassins.
It was first built by King Semion and used to house anyone who displeased him.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: The Silent Grove
.
The plot follows BioWare's own canon
, meaning that Alistair is king of Ferelden in the comic's story.
Velabanchel interior
Alistair
,
Varric
and
Isabela
break into Velabanchel as part of a "rescue" planned by Alistair. Varric attempts to open the gates for his companions, though inadvertently alerts the Crows. Once the trio are inside, a huge battle ensues. Varric and Isabela volunteer to hold off the attackers, while Alistair goes after his target. Alistair fights with the apparent commander of the prison, a
Qunari
who cripples Alistair's arm with a mace, before being slain. Alistair then opens a cell, and addresses its inhabitant as "
King Maric
". The man replies "You're too late. Much too late," as he is not Maric but did share a cell with him: Maric was freed long ago by
Yavana
the
Witch of the Wilds
.
See also
Agony
References

Location
Ventus
Type

(formerly)

Terrain

Location
Tevinter Imperium

Qunari

Dragon Age: Origins
(mentioned)

Dragon Age: Those Who Speak
Dragon Age: Inquisition

Dragon Age: Deception
Dragon Age: Blue Wraith
Dragon Age: Tevinter Nights
Ventus
, formerly known as
Qarinus
,  is a northeastern coastal city in the
Tevinter Imperium
. It was initially a kingdom founded in -1700 Ancient by the
Qarinus
, an offshoot of the
Neromenian
tribes.
The city has great strategic importance as it lies across the
Ventosus Straits
from the disputed island of
Seheron
. Qarinus was renamed "Ventus" following a Tevinter victory on the Ventosus Straits.
History
Prior to the founding of the Imperium, Qarinus stood as a mighty kingdom of its own, settled by the Qarinus tribe. In -1195
Ancient
,
Darinius
, High King of
Neromenian
and
Tevinter
, two of the remaining three pre-Imperium kingdoms, married High Queen Rathana of Qarinus to join her kingdom to his,
forming the Tevinter Imperium. He declared himself first
Archon
of this new empire. While
Minrathous
became the capital of the Imperium, Qarinus retained its importance.
The city architecture
A subterranean
embassy
for the dwarves was built following Tevinter's alliance with the
dwarven empire
.
Qarinus was captured by the
Qunari
during their
invasion
in the
Steel Age
. It remained under their control until the
Three Exalted Marches
called by the
Imperial
and
Andrastian Chantries
between 7:25-85
Storm
.
Qarinus was later re-named Ventus to celebrate a Tevinter military victory, likely the victory of 9:12 Dragon, a battle which involved an entire fleet of Qunari ships attacking Qarinus and being eventually forced to turn back to defend Seheron when Tevinter legions, heavily reinforced by mages, tore down the positions they had gained on the mainland.
Involvement
The plot follows BioWare's own canon
, meaning it may not follow some of the player's choices in the games.
Dragon Age: Those Who Speak
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Those Who Speak
.
Alistair
travels to Ventus in search of
Magister
Aurelian Titus
, who is alleged to have kidnapped
King
Maric Theirin
to use his blood to fuel his
magic
and ambitions. Alistair and his companions,
Varric Tethras
and
Isabela
, confront him at a gala, which causes a skirmish to break out. Magister
Maevaris Tilani
, a relation by marriage to Varric, aids them in the fight.
During the battle,
Isabela
slays her former lover and comrade, Lord
Devon
, to hide their past collaboration in transporting
slaves
. After the brawl, Alistair and his companions leave the city by ship.
Dragon Age: Deception
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Deception
.
By 9:44 Dragon, rumours that a major Qunari invasion of Ventus was imminent became widespread. The city falls soon after.
Dragon Age: Tevinter Nights
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Tevinter Nights
.
The plot follows BioWare's own canon
, meaning it may not follow some of the player's choices in the games.
Three Trees to Midnight
Without the
Ben-Hassrath
to measure the doses of
qamek
, Bas-taar of the Antaam claims that they had no choice but to give massive doses to all the mages taken during the fall of Ventus, even those who didn't fight, and that "they will not come back to their minds." Other prisoners considered troublesome were also given qamek and put to work doing menial labor. Strife narrowly escapes this fate thanks to Myrion's interference.
This brutality, unprecedented even by Qunari standards, in combination with the
Dragon's Breath Conspiracy
that was attempted against
all
of the non-Qunari nations, may galvanize support for Tevinter's ongoing war with the Qunari, both within the Imperium and from the other nations, as the former demonstrates what would happen across Thedas if the Qunari were victorious, and the latter shows that the Qunari have no interest in honoring the peace between them and the other nations besides Tevinter that resulted from the
Llomeryn Accords.

– Master of Varania

— the son of a reclusive Magister

– a Father of the Imperial Chantry
(
Blessed Age
)

– servant of House Qintara

companion
to
the Inquisitor

Dorian Pavus

– a failed actress turned con-woman
Magister
Qintara

– High Queen of Qarinus
(
Ancient Age
)
Varania
Trivia
Though in-game, Ventus has had a new name among Thedosians for decades, the real-world reason for the change was confusion between the words "Qarinus" and "Qunari."

Location
Veridium Mine

The
Veridium Mine
is an
elven
ruin in the
Dales
.
It is unknown if the elves of old used it as a mine or for other purposes, but by 9:42
Dragon
the ruin was used to mine
veridium
, and more recently as a military base for the
Freemen of the Dales
.
Background
Costeau
, a former
Chantry
sister, abandons the Chantry after the
Divine Conclave
and joins the Freemen situated in
the Graves
.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
Costeau amasses the Veridium Mine as a forward base of operations for the Freemen. The mine functions as a storage for the hording of supplies from raids being carried out about the region as well as a mining operation for
red lyrium
that the
Red Templars
have brought in.
Gertrude
, one of the refugees hiding out from the current ongoing
War of the Lions
, sneaks into the mine in order to steal supplies for her people holed up at
Watcher's Reach
. However, Gertrude gets caught and Costeau imprisons her for the attempted theft.
When
the Inquisitor
arrives at the scene, Costeau and the Freemen mistake the trespassers for a rescue party, and become hostile. Costeau proves no match for the Inquisitor and is slain. The party then proceeds to rescue all of the prisoners being held
captive
.

Vicinius's Home
is the summer residence of a Tevinter merchant,
Vicinius
. It is located within the
Orlesian
capital city of
Val Royeaux
by the docks just off the
Summer Bazaar
.
Background
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
Vicinius's Home is only accessible during
Leliana
's
Inner Circle quest
Under Her Skin
.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
The
Inquisition
contacts Vicinius in pursuit of information regarding
Calpernia
; the leader of the
Venatori
. However upon arrival,
the Inquisitor
and their party discover a Venatori cohort has already murdered Vicinius and will attack.
While searching for clues, three pieces of a
memory crystal
can be found within the apartment.
Quests

Under Her Skin
Codex entries
Charred Notebook

Note texts
Invoice for Slaves
Bugs
xboxone
Attempting to enter the home may cause the game to crash and return you to the dashboard. If this occurs, it will continue to happen every time, indefinitely, making the quest impossible to complete.
Attempting to enter the home may alternatively cause you to be automatically sent back to Skyhold rather than into the home.

======================================================================
PAGE: Vigil's Keep
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Vigil's_Keep
======================================================================

Location
Vigil's Keep
Type
Fortress
Location
Arling of Amaranthine
, Northeast
Ferelden
Factions
Avvars
(formerly)
Howe family
(previously)
Grey Wardens
Exits
World map
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening
Dragon Age: Warden's Fall
The Golems of Amgarrak
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: Last Flight
(mentioned)
Vigil's Keep
, informally known as
the Vigil
, is an ancient fortress in the
Arling of Amaranthine
, in north-east
Ferelden
. It was formerly the seat of the
Howe family
and is now the
Grey Wardens
' main base in Ferelden.
Background
Vigil's Keep is one of the oldest settlements in Ferelden, older than Denerim and Gwaren. The barbarians who battled the Tevinter Imperium chose this location for a fortress so that their warning fires would be visible at great distance, when Tevinter ships neared the coast.
—
Codex entry: The Vigil
Map of Vigil's Keep - Exterior
The fortress was built by
Alamarri
Avvars
to stop the
Tevinter
advance from the North. In their time it was known as the
Fort of a Thousand Vigils
. It was built over caves leading to the
Deep Roads
, believed to be carved as a gift from
Korth
, an Avvarian god of mountains.
The Avvars residing in the fortress lived peacefully with the
dwarves
, which was unusual as the dwarves were allied with their enemy, the Tevinters. The alliance of Avvars and dwarves predates building the fortress. The dwarves helped the Avvars defeat and bind a dangerous
shade
called
the Dark Theurge
, who was previously one of their own,
Ruadan
.
As the Avvar culture declined, the ownership of the keep changed, eventually becoming the property of the Fereldan noble
Howe family
from which they governed the entire Arling of Amaranthine.
Vigil's Keep was the first fortress to fall during the
Orlesian
invasion in Ferelden and the last to be liberated during the
Fereldan Rebellion
. It is probably also the place where Prince
Maric Theirin
met
Byron Howe
in order to thank him for his support of the rebellion.
Involvement
In 9:31
Dragon
, after Arl
Rendon Howe
disgraced his family during the
Fifth Blight
, the Howes were stripped of all their holdings, including Vigil's Keep. The keep itself was then run by Fereldan soldiers. The last soldier to be in charge of the keep was Lieutenant
Gable
. The stronghold became a temporary base for Orlesian Grey Wardens who were helping Ferelden in the aftermath of the
Blight
, killing
darkspawn
stragglers.
In the month of
Ferventis
of the same year,
the keep was officially given to the Grey Wardens by the Fereldan monarchy along with the rest of the Arling.
The Warden-Commander
was appointed to re-establish the Grey Wardens presence in Ferelden, using Vigil's Keep as the order's main base.
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening
.
The keep was assaulted by
the Architect
's
disciples
who either killed or kidnapped all of the Grey Wardens stationed there. It was a surprise attack on the day the keep was to be officially handed over to the Warden-Commander. The darkspawn entered the keep from a Deep Roads entrance located in the
basement
. The Deep Roads run deep underneath the keep.
The Warden-Commander may
investigate the Deep Roads
and, with the help of
Voldrik Glavonak
, shut the access to the keep. The Warden-Commander may also
upgrade the walls
of the keep, also through the help of Voldrik.
If the Warden-Commander decides to
save Amaranthine
instead of Vigil's Keep, it is destroyed by
the Mother
's invading darkspawn army. Depending on player choices, the Keep may be rebuilt completely within five years. If the Warden-Commander decides to protect the Vigil, instead, their victory during the
siege
becomes one of the most important and famous military victories in the history of the Grey Warden Order.
If the Warden-Commander
invested in silverite armor
for the Vigil's soldiers, they eventually come to be known as the Silver Order. Under the tutelage of the Wardens, the Silver Order developed into one of Ferelden's most revered military forces, a lasting memory of the Vigil's famous commander.
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Last Flight
.
The plot follows BioWare's own canon
, meaning it may not follow some of the player's choices in the games.
During the
Mage-Templar War
there is a new Warden-Commander in Vigil's Keep. They request supplies from
Weisshaupt
after an encounter with
sylvans
.

Upgrading the Keep
The Keep can be upgraded in the following ways.
Defenses
The following have the largest effect on the ending:
Completing
Cost of Doing Business
rebuilds the gatehouse.
Completing
What is Built Endures
upgrades the walls and gatehouse and replaces a nearby palisade with a stone wall.
Completing
It Comes From Beneath
protects the keep from attack by way of the Deep Roads.
Completing
Elemental Requirements
improves your soldiers' armor.
Merchants
Recruiting merchants for the quest
Trade Must Flow
allows for new items to be available for purchase:
Lillith
- is recruited during the
Abandoned Farm
encounter.
Armaas
- can be found near the end of the
Silverite Mine
.
Resources
Blood Lotus
(2)
Notable items
Lucrosian's Silken Cord
- source: from a locked
chest
during
the Assault on Vigil's Keep
.
Stormchaser Helm
- source: from a locked
chest
during the Assault on Vigil's Keep.
Special objects
Statue - initiates dialog with
Velanna
and
Anders
.
Kitten
- initiates dialog with Anders, allowing it to be given to him as a
gift
.
Codex entries

Notes
The east side of the Keep (where the kitten is found wandering) has two locked chests and a wooden crate. These periodically respawn containing new items.

Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening
The
basement
of
Vigil's Keep
is entered through a doorway directly across from the portcullis leading to the
throne room
. The basement can be accessed immediately after reclaiming the upper floors. Vigil's Keep has seen many passageways and chambers added to its labyrinthine structure over the years, some of them dating back to the time of barbarians. Some say the halls extend all the way down to the
Deep Roads
.

and so on. There is a

the Warden-Commander
.

Velanna
after taking

after killing the

Villa Maurel
is the former estate of Lord Ramond Maurel, situated in the
Emerald Graves
in the
Dales
of
Orlais
.
Background
During the
War of the Lions
, Lord Maurel abandons the Villa for the safety of
Val Royeaux
, dismissing his staff and hiding his valuables away in a secured room, one which may only be opened via using a
runed key
. The abandoned Villa then is taken over by the
Freemen of the Dales
,  deserters of the Orlesian Imperial Army looking to establish a new settlement under their own banner, and is currently serving as their headquarters.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
The operations being carried out by the Freemen of the Dales in Villa Maurel, unbeknownst to them, are in servitude of
the Elder One
. The Freemen are being manipulated by the
Venatori
and the
Red Templars
of whom are both actively progressing the Elder One's sinister agenda. After clashing with
Costeau
, a corrupt
Chantry
sister and high ranking member of Freemen (operating out of the
Veridium Mine
),
the Inquisitor
sets out to investigate Villa Maurel and confront another Freemen's high ranking member, Commander
Duhaime
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Village of Crestwood
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Inquisition locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Village_of_Crestwood
======================================================================

For the Region, see
Crestwood
.
Location
Village of Crestwood
Type
Village
Location
Crestwood
,
Western Ferelden
Inhabitants
Humans
Appearances
Dragon Age: Inquisition
The
Village of Crestwood
is located north of
Lake Calenhad
, lying between
Val Royeaux
and
Denerim
. The village's primary income stems from trade between
Ferelden
and
Orlais
.
Background
King Brandel erected
Caer Bronach
in 8:26
Blessed
, intending the fort as a stopover garrison for
Fereldan
soldiers fighting
Orlesian
invaders. The village of
Crestwood
takes its name from the fort's first captain Ser Venar Crestwood, who held Caer Bronach longer than anyone thought possible against a vanguard of the Orlesian army. Impressed by Ser Crestwood's tenacity and endurance, the remaining officers left the small village around the fort untouched as the Orlesians made for the capital, sparing hundreds of innocent lives.
Crestwood is a small village of no real consequence to the lords and ladies who ride through on their way to
Val Royeaux
or
Denerim
. The people are glad for visitors; residents tend livestock and grow what crops they can, but their chief income comes from trade.
Involvement
Three Trout Pond
hides a
sinkhole
hundreds of yards deep.
Darkspawn
emerged from this and other caves to attack Crestwood during the Blight. The flood that wiped out
Old Crestwood
drowned the
blighted ones
, and the excess water created the pond we see today.
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
A
Fade rift
opens underneath the nearby lake that enveloped the ruins of Old Crestwood which were destroyed during the events surrounding the
Fifth Blight
.
This results in
Demons
possessing the drowned
Corpses
residing in the ruins; the residents of Crestwood Village find themselves under constant threat from the undead.

Mayor
Gregory Dedrick

Vaughn

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Ville Montevelan
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Inquisition locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Ville_Montevelan
======================================================================

Location
Ville Montevelan
Type
Ruin
Terrain
Abandoned Village
Location
Exalted Plains
,
Dales
Inhabitants
Humans
(formerly)
Demons
Appearances
Dragon Age: Inquisition
Ville Montevelan
is a
region
in the
Exalted Plains
. It was the first
human
settlement established in the
Dales
following the conclusion of the
second Exalted March
.
Background
When Ville Montevelan was first established after the end of the
Exalted March of the Dales
, it was originally populated by soldiers. In 2:21
Glory
,
Sister
Amity
, Champion of the Exalted March, laid the foundation stones of the village's chantry. When its construction was completed in 2:22, she put her arms aside to become the leader of Ville Montevelan's flock and assume the title of
Revered Mother
, a position she would keep for over forty years. When she passed away in her sleep in 2:64, her ashes were interred in the chantry's vaults, attracting even more pilgrims to the historic town.
A hundred years after the end of the Exalted March, after several villagers drowned in a ferry accident while trying to cross the nearby river, Mayor Agur of Ville Montevelan raised funds to have a bridge built. However, some villagers took offense at the dedication, which read: "Dedicated to all, elven and human, who lost their lives in the second Exalted March," for honoring elven losses. Mayor Agur was forced to stepped down after rumors that he was
elf-blooded
started circulating. In spite of this, the bridge, Pont Agur, still bears his name.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
As a result of the
War of the Lions
, Ville Montevelan has been abandoned and sustained severe damage. Pont Agur was destroyed in the fighting, but can be
repaired
by
the Inquisition
to gain access to the northwest section of the region. A
Fade rift
has also opened in the center of town.
Ville Montevelan is the location of several
caches
of Imperial army supplies. It is also the location of
Codex entry: The Silver Knight
, a prerequisite for being able to retrieve
Evanura
in the
Emerald Graves
.
Quests
Pressed for Cache
Rifts
Rifts on the Battlefield
War table operations
Repair Pont Agur
Enemies
Demons
Despair demon
Rage demon
Revenant
Wraith
Collections
Bottles on the Wall
Vintage: Warden Korenic - search inside a yellow house by the river.
Landmarks
Landmarks on the Plains
Shards
Shards on the Plains
Songs
Codex entry: The Silver Knight
- on the ground in a ruined pinkish house just south of the
Ville Montevelan
landmark
(west of the
Triumph of the Light
landmark).
Codex entries
The Silver Knight
Ville Montevelan
Note texts
Infatuation
Instructions for the Maid
References

led by an
Arvaarad
. Sister Petrice's mission was in fact a set-up; she left a trail leading from a slain karataam straight to the pass to force a confrontation between Hawke and the Qunari.

The
Vimmark Mountains
are a steep mountain range in the
Free Marches
, stretching along the northern coast of the
Waking Sea
. They separate a narrow coastal strip in the south, including the major city-states of
Kirkwall
and
Ostwick
, from the basin of the
Minanter
river to the north.
Background
The creatures that appear in the Vimmark Mountains vary depending on location, but include
ghasts
,
wyverns
and
darkspawn
. The
Grey Wardens
have left their mark on the mountains, leaving behind ancient structures and tunnels dating back to the
First
and
Fourth
Blights
.

PAGE: Viscount's Keep
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age II locations, Kirkwall locations, Locations
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Viscount's_Keep

Location
Viscount's Keep
Type
Citadel
Terrain
Tevinter Architecture
Location
Hightown
,
Kirkwall
Inhabitants
Nobles
City Guards
Appearances
Dragon Age II
Legacy
(mentioned)

(mentioned)
Trespasser
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: Hard in Hightown
The Viscount's Keep
is the residence of the Viscount of
Kirkwall
and the seat of government for the city. It towers above the other manors and estates of
Hightown
and is only rivaled in size by the
Chantry
.
Background
Viscount's Keep heraldry
The keep was originally constructed for
Tevinter
nobles by the
dwarves
.
It is located just off Hightown and by 9:30
Dragon
is the home of Viscount
Marlowe Dumar
and his heir
Saemus Dumar
. In addition to this the Keep serves several other functions for the city. It provides facilities for various institutions of Kirkwall's government and also function as a meeting place for petitions to the Viscount's office. It also serves as a site for the conduct of public ceremonies.
The barracks, home of the
Kirkwall City Guard
, is located in the southern wing along with the Guard Captain's office. The Viscount's office and Seneschal's office are situated in the northern wing. The eastern wing houses the throne room which is used for the conduct of public ceremonies as well as the coronation of the new Viscount or Viscountess.
Day-to-day affairs in the keep are administered by Seneschal
Bran Cavin
, who is usually found near the Viscount's offices in the north wing.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age II
.
In 9:34, the
Arishok
launches a
full-scale invasion
of Kirkwall. He beheads Viscount
Dumar
inside the Keep in front of the city's nobility but is quickly stopped by
Hawke
, who either defeats him in combat or convinces him to leave the city. Hawke is named
Champion of Kirkwall
for stopping the Qunari invasion.

PAGE: Vyrantium

Location
Vyrantium
Type
City
Terrain
Urban
Location
Coastal Tevinter
Inhabitants
Humans

Dwarves
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins

Dragon Age: Inquisition
